Table manufacturing.manufacturing_facility {
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the manufacturing facility. Primary key. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: ESG commitments are defined per facility for compliance; linking facility to its commitment enables facility‑level ESG reporting.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.jurisdiction. Business justification: Facility compliance reporting must reference the jurisdiction governing its operations for regulatory filings and audit purposes.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Facility manager required for safety compliance, production reporting, and labor accountability; obvious to a consumer‑goods plant manager.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Required for Supply Network Planning report to map each plant to its network node, enabling ATP, capacity, and logistics calculations; domain experts expect this mapping.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the manufacturing facility location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or additional location details. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the manufacturing facility is located.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the facility was officially commissioned and began production operations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the manufacturing facility is located (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the manufacturing facility record was first created in the system.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email contact for the manufacturing facility operations team. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  energy_consumption_kwh_per_year decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual energy consumption of the facility measured in kilowatt-hours, used for sustainability reporting and cost management.']
  epa_site_number varchar [note: 'EPA facility identifier for environmental compliance tracking and reporting under EPA regulations.']
  erp_plant_code varchar [note: 'Plant identifier in the SAP S/4HANA ERP system used for integration with MM, PP, SD, and WM modules.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Official business name of the manufacturing facility or plant site.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the manufacturing facility based on primary production function: mixing (formulation), filling (liquid/semi-solid), packaging (secondary), co-manufacturing (third-party), integrated (end-to-end), or contract (toll manufacturing).']
  fda_establishment_number varchar [note: 'FDA-issued establishment identifier for facilities manufacturing FDA-regulated products (cosmetics, OTC drugs, food). Required for US market distribution.']
  gmp_certification_date date [note: 'Date when the facility received its current GMP certification.']
  gmp_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility holds current Good Manufacturing Practice certification required for consumer goods production.']
  gmp_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current GMP certification expires and requires renewal.']
  iso_14001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility holds ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification.']
  iso_22716_compliance_tier varchar [note: 'Internal classification of the facility\'s compliance level with ISO 22716 cosmetics GMP standards: tier_1 (full compliance), tier_2 (partial compliance), tier_3 (basic compliance), non_compliant.']
  iso_9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility holds ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent quality or compliance audit conducted at the facility.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the manufacturing facility record was last updated in the system.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the facility location in decimal degrees for mapping and logistics optimization.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the facility location in decimal degrees for mapping and logistics optimization.']
  mes_system_integrated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is integrated with Siemens Opcenter MES for real-time production tracking and shop floor data collection.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next quality or compliance audit at the facility.']
  number_of_production_lines varchar [note: 'Total count of active production lines within the facility available for manufacturing operations.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the manufacturing facility in its lifecycle.']
  osha_establishment_number varchar [note: 'OSHA establishment identifier for workplace safety reporting and compliance tracking.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal ownership structure of the facility: owned (company-owned), leased (long-term lease), contract (third-party operated), joint_venture (shared ownership).']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the manufacturing facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the manufacturing plant used across SAP S/4HANA PP module and operational systems. Typically 4-10 alphanumeric characters.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the facility address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  primary_product_category varchar [note: 'Main product category or line manufactured at this facility (e.g., skincare, haircare, household cleaning, personal care).']
  production_capacity_units_per_day decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum daily production capacity of the facility measured in finished goods units per day under normal operating conditions.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard operating shift pattern for the facility: single (8 hours), two_shift (16 hours), three_shift (24 hours), continuous (24/7), flexible (variable).']
  square_footage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total floor area of the manufacturing facility in square feet, including production, warehouse, and administrative spaces.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or regional administrative division where the facility is located.']
  sustainability_rating varchar [note: 'Internal sustainability performance rating for the facility based on environmental impact, energy efficiency, waste reduction, and sustainable sourcing practices.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the facility location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/Berlin) used for production scheduling and shift planning.']
  water_consumption_cubic_meters_per_year decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual water consumption of the facility measured in cubic meters, tracked for environmental compliance and sustainability reporting.']
  workforce_headcount varchar [note: 'Total number of employees (full-time equivalent) assigned to the manufacturing facility.']

  Note: 'Physical manufacturing plant or factory where products are produced. SSOT owner for production facility master. Distinct from distribution_facility which represents warehouses/DCs.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_line {
  production_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production line. Primary key for the production line master record.']
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  energy_certificate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.energy_certificate. Business justification: Energy certificates certify renewable energy for a line; linking enables line‑level energy compliance tracking.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_facility. Business justification: Production line belongs to a specific manufacturing facility; replace generic facility reference with direct link to manufacturing_facility.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Line supervisor drives OEE, quality and shift planning; plant experts expect a dedicated supervisor link per line.']
  allergen_handling_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production line handles products containing allergens. Critical for cross-contamination prevention and regulatory compliance.']
  allergen_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of allergen types handled on this line (e.g., nuts, dairy, gluten). Used for allergen management and cleaning validation protocols.']
  asset_tag_number varchar [note: 'Physical asset tag number assigned to the production line for asset management and tracking purposes. Links to the fixed asset register in the ERP system.']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Degree of automation on the production line. Indicates the level of human intervention required and impacts labor planning and quality consistency.']
  changeover_time_standard_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard time in minutes required to change over the production line from one product or batch to another. Used for production scheduling and OEE loss calculation.']
  cleaning_validation_protocol varchar [note: 'Reference to the cleaning validation protocol document applicable to this production line. Ensures GMP compliance and prevents cross-contamination.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the production line was commissioned and became operational. Used for asset lifecycle tracking and depreciation calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production line record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  crew_size_standard varchar [note: 'Standard number of operators required to run the production line at full capacity. Used for labor planning and cost allocation.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting depreciation method applied to this production line asset. Used for financial reporting and asset valuation.']
  design_capacity_units_per_hour decimal(18,2)
  design_speed_units_per_hour decimal(12,2) [note: 'Theoretical maximum production capacity of the line measured in units per hour under ideal conditions. Used for capacity planning and scheduling.']
  energy_consumption_kwh_per_unit decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average energy consumption per unit produced on this line, measured in kilowatt-hours. Key metric for sustainability reporting and cost analysis.']
  environmental_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the production line requires controlled environmental conditions such as temperature, humidity, or air quality. Critical for GMP compliance and product quality.']
  gmp_classification varchar [note: 'GMP classification level of the production line indicating the quality and cleanliness standards required for products manufactured on this line. Critical for regulatory compliance and product safety.']
  humidity_range_percent decimal(6,2) [note: 'Acceptable relative humidity range for the production line as a percentage. Format: min-max (e.g., 40-60). Used for environmental monitoring and compliance.']
  installed_power_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total installed electrical power capacity of the production line in kilowatts. Used for energy consumption tracking and sustainability reporting.']
  last_major_overhaul_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent major overhaul or refurbishment of the production line. Used for maintenance planning and asset condition assessment.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production line record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  line_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the production line. Unique within the facility and used for operational reference in MES systems and production scheduling.']
  line_length_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical length of the production line in meters. Used for facility layout planning and material flow analysis.']
  line_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the production line. Used for display in dashboards, reports, and operator interfaces.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the production line based on its primary manufacturing function. Determines the type of operations and products that can be processed on this line.']
  mean_time_between_failures_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time in hours between equipment failures on this production line. Key reliability metric used for maintenance optimization and capacity planning.']
  mean_time_to_repair_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average time in hours required to repair and restore the production line to operational status after a failure. Used for downtime analysis and maintenance resource planning.']
  mes_asset_tag varchar [note: 'Unique asset identifier for this production line in the Siemens Opcenter MES system. Used for real-time data collection, production tracking, and integration with shop floor systems.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this production line record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the next planned preventive maintenance activity for this production line. Used for maintenance scheduling and production planning coordination.']
  nominal_oee_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OEE percentage for this production line. OEE is calculated as Availability × Performance × Quality and represents the benchmark for line performance evaluation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or operational considerations specific to this production line.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the production line. Indicates whether the line is available for production scheduling and execution.']
  plc_system_type varchar [note: 'Type and model of the PLC system controlling the production line. Used for technical support, integration planning, and system upgrade management.']
  product_category_capability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product categories that can be manufactured on this line. Used for production planning and product-line matching in scheduling systems.']
  quality_inspection_frequency varchar [note: 'Standard frequency for quality control inspections on this production line. Defines the QC sampling plan and compliance with GMP requirements.']
  record_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system from which this production line record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA PP, Siemens Opcenter MES). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  scada_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the production line is integrated with the SCADA system for real-time monitoring and control. Enables remote visibility and data collection.']
  scrap_rate_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target scrap rate percentage for this production line. Used for quality performance benchmarking and waste reduction initiatives.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard operating shift pattern for this production line. Defines the daily operating schedule and impacts capacity calculations.']
  temperature_range_celsius varchar [note: 'Acceptable operating temperature range for the production line in Celsius. Format: min-max (e.g., 18-25). Used for environmental monitoring and compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected useful life of the production line in years for depreciation and asset lifecycle planning purposes.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical production line within a manufacturing facility. Captures line code, line name, facility reference, line type (filling, blending, packaging, assembly), design speed (units/hour), nominal OEE target, GMP classification, MES asset tag (Siemens Opcenter), changeover time standard (minutes), and current operational status. Enables OEE benchmarking, scheduling capacity allocation, and MES integration at the line level.'
}

Table manufacturing.work_center {
  work_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the work center. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Labor and overhead budgeting links each work center to a finance cost center for expense tracking.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee responsible for managing and maintaining this work center, supporting accountability and contact management.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this work center is physically located.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production line to which this work center belongs, enabling line-level capacity and performance analysis.']
  available_capacity_hours_per_day decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total available production hours per day for this work center after accounting for planned downtime, breaks, and maintenance windows.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Next scheduled calibration date for measurement and control instruments on this work center, ensuring accuracy and compliance with quality standards.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the rated capacity indicating how production output is quantified for this work center.']
  capacity_units_per_hour decimal(18,2)
  clean_room_class varchar [note: 'ISO 14644 clean room classification for work centers in controlled environments, critical for cosmetics and personal care product manufacturing per GMP requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iso_3|iso_4|iso_5|iso_6|iso_7|iso_8|not_applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the work center was first commissioned and put into production service, establishing the asset lifecycle baseline.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work center record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage.']
  crew_size varchar [note: 'Standard number of operators required to run this work center during normal production operations, used for labor planning and scheduling.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the work center was retired from production service, supporting asset lifecycle tracking and capacity planning.']
  efficiency_pct decimal(6,2)
  energy_consumption_kwh_per_hour decimal(12,4) [note: 'Average energy consumption in kilowatt-hours per operating hour, supporting sustainability reporting and cost allocation per ISO 14001 environmental management.']
  gmp_qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification status of the work center per GMP requirements. IQ (Installation Qualification), OQ (Operational Qualification), PQ (Performance Qualification) track equipment validation stages per ISO 22716.']
  hazardous_area_classification varchar [note: 'Electrical area classification for work centers handling flammable or explosive materials, ensuring compliance with Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) and ATEX directives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_hazardous|class_1_div_1|class_1_div_2|class_2_div_1|class_2_div_2|atex_zone_0|atex_zone_1|atex_zone_2 — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  humidity_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work center operates in a humidity-controlled environment requiring environmental monitoring per GMP standards.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent calibration performed on this work center, supporting traceability and compliance documentation.']
  maintenance_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the work center by maintenance priority and frequency requirements, driving preventive maintenance scheduling.']
  maintenance_strategy varchar [note: 'Maintenance approach applied to this work center defining how maintenance activities are planned and executed.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the equipment manufacturer, supporting warranty management, spare parts procurement, and technical support.']
  mes_equipment_code varchar [note: 'Unique equipment identifier in the Siemens Opcenter MES system enabling real-time production tracking and shop floor data collection.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model number of the work center equipment, enabling precise identification for maintenance, parts ordering, and technical documentation.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work center record was last modified, supporting change tracking and audit compliance.']
  operator_skill_level_required varchar [note: 'Minimum operator skill level required to operate this work center safely and effectively, supporting workforce planning and training requirements.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the work center achieved its current GMP qualification status, establishing the baseline for requalification scheduling.']
  rated_capacity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum production capacity of the work center expressed in units per time period, used for Material Requirements Planning (MRP) capacity calculations.']
  requalification_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the work center must undergo requalification to maintain GMP compliance and production authorization.']
  safety_certification varchar [note: 'Safety certifications held by this work center such as CE marking, UL listing, or other regulatory approvals required for operation.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to this specific equipment unit, supporting warranty tracking and asset identification.']
  setup_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard time in minutes required to prepare the work center for production, including changeover and configuration activities.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard shift pattern for this work center defining operational hours and crew rotation schedules.']
  standard_cycle_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard time in minutes required to complete one production cycle or unit at this work center, used for scheduling and costing.']
  standard_rate_per_hour decimal(18,2)
  teardown_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard time in minutes required to clean, sanitize, and reset the work center after production completion per Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) requirements.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work center operates in a temperature-controlled environment requiring environmental monitoring per GMP standards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp
  utilization_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target utilization rate as a percentage of available capacity, used for capacity planning and Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) benchmarking.']
  work_center_category varchar [note: 'High-level category grouping work centers by operational domain for capacity planning and resource allocation.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the work center used in production planning and scheduling systems. Externally known unique code.']
  work_center_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work center including its purpose, capabilities, and operational characteristics.']
  work_center_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the work center for identification and reporting purposes.']
  work_center_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the work center indicating availability for production scheduling and capacity planning.']
  work_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work center by its primary function in the manufacturing process. Categorizes equipment by operational role.']

  Note: 'Master record for individual work centers (machines, stations, cells) within a production line. Captures work center code, description, work center type (mixing vessel, filling machine, labeler, capper, palletizer), rated capacity, standard cycle time, maintenance class, MES equipment ID (Siemens Opcenter), GMP equipment qualification status (IQ/OQ/PQ), and calibration due date. Supports MRP capacity planning, scheduling, and equipment qualification tracking per ISO 22716.'
}

Table manufacturing.manufacturing_bom {
  manufacturing_bom_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bill of materials record. Primary key for the manufacturing BOM entity.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar
  manufacturing_sku_id varchar
  primary_manufacturing_sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finished good or semi-finished SKU that this BOM defines. Links to the product master for which this material structure applies.']
  product_bom_id varchar
  allergen_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component contains or may contain allergens as defined by FDA and EU REACH regulations. True if allergen present. Used for product labeling, consumer safety warnings, and regulatory compliance.']
  alternative_item_group varchar [note: 'Identifier for a group of interchangeable components that can substitute for each other in production. Used for supply chain flexibility, multi-sourcing strategies, and material availability optimization.']
  alternative_item_priority varchar [note: 'The selection priority for this component within its alternative item group. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by MRP to determine preferred material selection when multiple alternatives are available.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when this BOM version was formally approved for production use. Establishes the official release date for regulatory compliance and change control purposes.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the person who approved this BOM version for production use. Required for GMP compliance and quality assurance. Typically a product development manager, quality manager, or regulatory affairs specialist.']
  backflush_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component is automatically consumed (backflushed) when the finished good is confirmed in the MES system, rather than requiring explicit material issue transactions. True if backflushed. Common for high-volume, low-value components.']
  base_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The reference quantity of the finished good for which the component quantities are defined. Typically 1 unit, 100 units, or 1 batch. All component quantities are expressed per this base quantity.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the base quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter, BOX for box). Must align with the SKU primary UOM.']
  base_uom varchar
  bom_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique business identifier for this BOM, typically assigned by the PLM or ERP system. Used for cross-system reference and production order material requirements.']
  bom_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BOM. Active BOMs are used for production planning and MRP runs. Inactive or obsolete BOMs are retained for historical reference and regulatory traceability.']
  bom_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the BOM structure. Production BOM is used for standard manufacturing, co-product for joint production scenarios, phantom for intermediate assemblies not stocked, engineering for R&D prototypes, and planning for demand forecasting.']
  bom_version varchar
  component_effective_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this specific component is no longer valid within the BOM structure. Nullable for open-ended components. Used for material phase-out and supplier transition management.']
  component_effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this specific component becomes valid within the BOM structure. Supports phased material transitions, supplier changes, and cost optimization initiatives.']
  component_item_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the component type. Stock items are inventory-managed materials, non-stock are direct procurement items, phantom are intermediate assemblies not physically stocked, text items are instructions, and variable size items have flexible dimensions.']
  component_item_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number for each component within the BOM structure. Defines the order of components for display, reporting, and regulatory ingredient listing (descending order by weight for INCI compliance).']
  component_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The quantity of this component required to produce the base quantity of the finished good. Used for MRP explosion, production order material requirements, and COGS calculation.']
  component_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the component quantity (e.g., KG for raw materials, EA for packaging units, L for liquids). Must align with the component material master UOM.']
  cost_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of total product cost attributed to this component. Used for COGS calculation, profitability analysis, and cost optimization initiatives. Sum of all component cost allocations should equal 100% for the BOM.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this BOM record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and regulatory compliance documentation per ISO 9001 requirements.']
  critical_component_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component is critical for product quality, safety, or regulatory compliance. True if critical. Triggers enhanced quality control, supplier qualification requirements, and inventory safety stock policies.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this BOM version is no longer valid for new production orders. Nullable for open-ended BOMs. Used for phase-out management and regulatory compliance transitions.']
  effective_from date
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this BOM version becomes valid for production planning and MRP explosion. Supports planned material transitions and new product launches.']
  effective_until date
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, and disposal per OSHA and EPA regulations. True if hazardous. Triggers safety data sheet requirements and GMP compliance protocols.']
  inci_name varchar [note: 'The standardized INCI name for cosmetic ingredients as required by FDA and EU regulations. Used for product labeling, regulatory submissions, and consumer transparency. Mandatory for personal care and cosmetic products.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this BOM record was last updated. Tracks formulation changes, component substitutions, and regulatory updates. Critical for change control and GMP compliance.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The procurement or production lead time for this component in days. Used for MRP scheduling, production order release timing, and inventory planning. Includes supplier lead time plus internal processing time.']
  lot_size_requirement varchar [note: 'Defines the lot sizing rule for this component in production orders. Exact requires precise quantity, minimum allows overages, multiple requires quantities in specific increments, none has no restriction. Impacts material planning and waste management.']
  manufacturing_bom_status varchar
  minimum_lot_size decimal(18,6) [note: 'The minimum quantity that must be procured or produced for this component. Driven by supplier MOQ, production batch constraints, or economic order quantity calculations. Nullable if no minimum applies.']
  plm_system_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier linking this BOM to the source PLM system record. Enables traceability to engineering change orders, formulation documents, and R&D specifications.']
  regulatory_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing regulatory compliance requirements, restrictions, or special handling instructions for this component. Includes FDA registration numbers, EU REACH compliance status, GMP requirements, and safety data sheet references.']
  scrap_factor_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The expected waste or loss percentage for this component during production. Used to adjust gross material requirements in MRP runs. Typical values range from 0% to 10% depending on material type and process complexity.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Certification or standard that this component meets for sustainable sourcing (e.g., FSC for paper, RSPO for palm oil, Fair Trade, Organic). Used for corporate sustainability reporting and consumer transparency initiatives.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the BOM structure. Incremented when material composition changes due to reformulation, cost optimization, or regulatory compliance updates. Supports change control and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Bill of Materials master record (header + component lines) defining the complete material structure for each finished good or semi-finished SKU. Header captures BOM number, BOM type (production, co-product, phantom), base quantity, unit of measure, validity dates, PLM system reference, and BOM status. Component lines capture item number, material reference (raw material, packaging, semi-finished), component quantity per base quantity, scrap factor percentage, item category (stock, non-stock, phantom), INCI name for cosmetic ingredients, allergen flag, hazardous material flag, and component validity dates. SSOT for MRP explosion, production order material requirements, COGS calculation, and regulatory ingredient disclosure per FDA/EU REACH. This is the single authoritative source for all BOM component data — no separate component entity exists.'
}

Table manufacturing.routing {
  routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production routing master record. Primary key.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar
  primary_routing_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created this routing record. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  product_bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Bill of Materials (BOM) that defines the raw materials and components consumed by this routing. Links routing to material requirements.']
  production_line_id varchar
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finished or semi-finished SKU that this routing defines the manufacturing process for.']
  tertiary_routing_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this routing record. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the routing for production use. Approved routings are validated and authorized; pending routings await review; rejected routings require rework.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this routing was approved for production use. Supports audit trail and compliance requirements.']
  base_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Standard production lot size for which the routing operation times and resource requirements are defined. Used as the basis for scaling production schedules.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the base quantity (e.g., KG, L, EA, M). Aligns with GS1 unit codes.']
  changeover_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time required to transition production lines from the previous product to this routing. Critical for production scheduling and minimizing downtime.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard cost amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this routing record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_from date
  effective_until date
  expected_scrap_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall expected scrap percentage for the routing, representing anticipated waste or defects. Used for material planning and cost estimation.']
  expected_yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall expected yield percentage for the routing, representing the ratio of good output to total input. Used for production planning and variance analysis.']
  gmp_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing includes GMP-critical operations requiring enhanced documentation, validation, and compliance controls per ISO 22716 and FDA regulations.']
  ipc_checkpoint_count varchar [note: 'Number of in-process control checkpoints defined in this routing. IPC checkpoints are quality inspection points during production per ISO 22716 and GMP requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this routing record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  lot_size_restriction varchar [note: 'Constraint on production lot sizes for this routing. Fixed requires exact base quantity; minimum/maximum define bounds; multiple requires lot sizes to be multiples of base quantity; none allows any lot size.']
  maximum_lot_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum production lot size allowed for this routing. Nullable if no maximum constraint exists.']
  minimum_lot_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum production lot size allowed for this routing. Nullable if no minimum constraint exists.']
  operation_count varchar
  operation_sequence varchar [note: 'JSON array or delimited string capturing the complete sequence of operation steps. Each operation includes: operation_number, work_center_code, operation_description, control_key (internal_processing|external_processing|milestone), setup_time_minutes, machine_time_minutes, labor_time_minutes, yield_quantity, scrap_percentage, gmp_critical_step_flag, ipc_checkpoint_flag. This is the single authoritative source for all routing operation data — no separate operation entity exists.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility where this routing is executed. Aligns with organizational plant master data.']
  production_scheduler_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the production scheduling strategy or algorithm used for this routing (e.g., forward scheduling, backward scheduling, finite capacity).']
  routing_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the routing, including its purpose, scope, and any special instructions for production planners and shop floor operators.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the routing. Externally-known unique code used in production planning and scheduling systems.']
  routing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing. Active routings are available for production scheduling; draft routings are under development; inactive routings are temporarily disabled; obsolete routings are retired; under_review routings are pending approval.']
  routing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the routing defining its usage pattern. Standard routings are default production paths; reference routings are templates; rate routings define production rates; alternative routings provide backup paths; special routings are for one-time or custom production runs.']
  routing_version varchar
  standard_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard production cost per base quantity for this routing, including labor, machine, and overhead costs. Used for cost accounting and variance analysis.']
  total_labor_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative labor time across all operations in the routing, used for workforce planning and standard cost calculation.']
  total_machine_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative machine processing time across all operations in the routing, used for capacity planning and Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) calculations.']
  total_setup_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative setup time across all operations in the routing, used for capacity planning and scheduling.']
  total_standard_lead_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total cumulative lead time in hours required to complete all operations in this routing, including setup, processing, and queue times. Used for Material Requirements Planning (MRP) and capacity planning.']
  total_standard_time_minutes decimal(12,2)
  updated_timestamp timestamp
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this routing becomes effective and available for production scheduling.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this routing remains effective. Nullable for open-ended routings.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the routing, enabling tracking of routing changes over time. Supports engineering change management and audit trails.']

  Note: 'Production routing master (header + operation steps) defining the complete manufacturing process sequence for a finished or semi-finished SKU. Header captures routing number, routing type (standard, reference, rate), base quantity, plant, validity dates, and total standard lead time. Operation steps capture operation number, work center assignment, standard values (setup time, machine time, labor time), control key (internal processing, external processing, milestone), yield quantity, scrap percentage, GMP critical step flag, and IPC checkpoint flag. SSOT for production scheduling, capacity planning, standard cost calculation, and GMP batch record generation per ISO 22716. This is the single authoritative source for all routing operation data — no separate operation entity exists. Aligned with SAP PP Routing master.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_order {
  production_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production order. Primary key for the production order entity.']
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost accounting report requires each production order to be charged to a finance cost center for OPEX allocation.']
  inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_plan. Business justification: Production orders trigger an inspection plan; linking allows the order execution system to retrieve the exact inspection plan governing sampling and testing for that batch.']
  manufacturing_bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_bom. Business justification: Production order consumes a specific manufacturing BOM; adding manufacturing_bom_id FK connects BOM to order and eliminates isolation of manufacturing_bom.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Production Planning Dashboard ties orders to brands to enable demand forecasting and coordinated marketing launch planning.']
  product_bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bill of materials used for this production order. Defines component requirements.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'FK to manufacturing.production_line']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Pilot production orders are generated from R&D projects; the Production Planning system needs the project ID to track development cost and schedule.']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.routing. Business justification: Each production order follows a routing; adding routing_id FK links routing to order and eliminates isolation of routing.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Order fulfillment process requires linking each production order to the originating sales order to track manufacturing against sales commitments.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU being produced in this production order. Links to the product master data.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Order‑level supervisor oversees execution, cost tracking and GMP compliance; required for order‑performance dashboards.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.trade_account. Business justification: Required for Production Order to Customer Account linkage for OTIF SLA reporting and order fulfillment traceability.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for the production order. Includes material, labor, and overhead costs. Used for variance analysis.']
  actual_end_date date
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when production execution completed. Used for OEE calculation and performance analysis.']
  actual_start_date date
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when production execution began on the shop floor. Captured from MES system.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Unique batch or lot number assigned to the production run. Critical for traceability and recall management per GMP requirements.']
  changeover_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Time in minutes required for line changeover before starting this production order. Used for scheduling and efficiency analysis.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity produced and confirmed as good output. Represents yield after quality inspection.']
  cost_object varchar [note: 'Cost object (production order number or process order) used for collecting actual costs. Links to CO-PC product costing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production order was created in the system. First event in order lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost amounts. Typically company code currency.']
  downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Total unplanned downtime in minutes during production execution. Impacts OEE availability component.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production order is subject to GMP compliance requirements per ISO 22716 standards. True for regulated products.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'Inspection lot number created for quality control of the production output. Links to QM inspection results.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the production order record. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number for the finished good being produced. Business identifier for the product in ERP system.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'Three-character code identifying the MRP controller responsible for planning this material. Links to planner master data.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the production order. Captures special instructions, issues, or observations from shop floor.']
  oee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall Equipment Effectiveness percentage for this production run. Calculated as Availability × Performance × Quality. Industry standard KPI.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the production order. Externally-known unique order number assigned by SAP S/4HANA PP system.']
  order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Planned quantity to be produced in base unit of measure. Target output for the production order.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production order. CRTD=Created, REL=Released, PCNF=Partially Confirmed, TECO=Technically Complete, CNF=Confirmed, DLV=Delivered, CLSD=Closed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CRTD|REL|PCNF|TECO|CNF|DLV|CLSD — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of production order. PP01=Standard Production, PP03=Rework, PP05=Co-Product, PP10=Process Order, PP20=Batch Order.']
  planned_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard or planned cost for producing the order quantity. Calculated from BOM and routing costs.']
  planned_end_date date
  planned_order_id varchar
  planned_quantity decimal(18,4)
  planned_start_date date
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the manufacturing facility where production occurs. Maps to SAP plant master.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for production scheduling. 1=Highest priority (rush/expedite), 5=Lowest priority (standard). Used for sequencing and resource allocation.']
  production_supervisor varchar [note: 'Name of the production supervisor responsible for overseeing execution of this order on the shop floor.']
  production_version varchar [note: 'Production version defining the specific BOM and routing combination used. Enables multiple production methods for same material.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether quality inspection is mandatory before goods receipt. Drives QM integration and inspection lot creation.']
  released_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who released the production order for execution. Audit trail for production authorization.']
  released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the production order was released for shop floor execution. Triggers material availability check and capacity reservation.']
  scheduled_finish_date date [note: 'Planned date when production is scheduled to complete. Used for capacity planning and delivery commitments.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date when production is scheduled to begin. Set during production planning and MRP run.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material scrapped or rejected during production. Used for yield analysis and cost accounting.']
  settlement_rule varchar [note: 'Rule for settling production order costs. FUL=Full settlement, PAR=Partial settlement, PER=Periodic settlement.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for quantities. Standard ISO codes such as EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), M (meter).']
  uom varchar
  updated_timestamp timestamp
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual yield percentage calculated as (confirmed_quantity / order_quantity) * 100. Key performance metric for production efficiency.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created the production order in the system. Audit trail for order origination.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record representing a manufacturing production order issued to produce a specific SKU quantity at a facility. Captures order number, order type (PP01 standard, PP03 rework), SKU reference, plant, production line, scheduled start/finish dates, actual start/finish dates, order quantity, confirmed quantity, scrap quantity, order status (created, released, partially confirmed, technically complete, closed), MRP controller, and cost object. SSOT for production execution tracking in SAP S/4HANA PP.'
}

Table manufacturing.batch_record {
  batch_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the electronic batch manufacturing record (eBMR). Primary key for GMP-compliant batch traceability.']
  batch_approved_by_employee_id varchar
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Batch cost roll‑up uses finance cost center to allocate raw material and labor expenses per batch.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Regulatory traceability: links each production batch to its inventory lot batch for recall investigations and quality audits.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_facility. Business justification: Batch record should reference the specific manufacturing facility for traceability.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand Batch Traceability Report requires linking each batch to its brand for recall, quality and marketing performance analysis.']
  primary_batch_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality assurance personnel who performed final batch release. Links to workforce master data.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific production line used for this batch. Links to equipment master data.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order that authorized this batch manufacturing run. Links to SAP PP production order master.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Batch‑to‑PO traceability is needed for audit, recall, and cost allocation linking each manufactured batch to its sourcing PO.']
  research_formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.research_formulation. Business justification: Traceability report requires linking each manufacturing batch to the exact formulation used, essential for regulatory compliance and product recall.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Batch traceability for recalls and quality investigations needs to associate each batch with the sales order that generated the demand.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finished goods SKU produced in this batch. Links to product master data.']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual cost per unit incurred for this batch. Includes material, labor, and overhead variances.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Unique manufacturing batch identifier assigned at production initiation. Used for product traceability and recall management per GMP requirements.']
  batch_size decimal(18,4)
  batch_size_actual decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual production quantity achieved for this batch. Used for yield calculation and inventory reconciliation.']
  batch_size_planned decimal(18,3) [note: 'Planned production quantity for this batch as specified in the production order. Measured in base unit of measure.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the batch. Controls inventory availability and distribution eligibility per GMP workflow.']
  batch_uom varchar
  bom_version varchar [note: 'Version of the bill of materials used for this batch. Ensures traceability of raw material specifications.']
  changeover_minutes varchar [note: 'Time in minutes required for line changeover before this batch. Key metric for SMED and lean manufacturing initiatives.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this batch record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for cost amounts. Supports multi-currency manufacturing operations.']
  downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Total unplanned downtime in minutes during batch production. Used for OEE calculation and maintenance planning.']
  electronic_signature_count varchar [note: 'Number of electronic signatures captured during batch record lifecycle. Required for FDA 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the product should not be used or sold. Critical for FEFO inventory management and consumer safety.']
  gmp_compliant_flag boolean
  gmp_deviation_count varchar [note: 'Number of GMP deviations recorded for this batch. Used for quality trend analysis and CAPA tracking.']
  gmp_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any GMP deviations were recorded during batch production. Triggers quality review workflow.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this batch record was last updated. Audit trail for GMP compliance and data integrity.']
  lot_genealogy_complete_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether complete raw material lot traceability has been captured for this batch. Required for recall readiness.']
  manufacture_date date
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'Calendar date of batch production. Printed on product packaging for consumer information and regulatory compliance.']
  manufacturing_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when batch production was completed and final packaging finished. Used for cycle time analysis.']
  manufacturing_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when batch production was initiated on the shop floor. Captured from MES system.']
  oee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall equipment effectiveness achieved during batch production. Composite metric of availability, performance, and quality.']
  quality_release_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch has been released by Quality Assurance for distribution. Required before inventory availability.']
  quality_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when Quality Assurance approved the batch for release. Marks transition from quarantine to available inventory.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch is subject to a product recall. Triggers supply chain hold and customer notification.']
  recall_initiated_date date [note: 'Date when recall was initiated for this batch. Used for regulatory reporting and recall effectiveness tracking.']
  record_locked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch record is locked from further edits. Required for GMP record integrity after quality release.']
  regulatory_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether batch is on regulatory hold pending investigation or approval. Prevents distribution until cleared.']
  release_date date
  release_status varchar
  rework_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of product requiring rework during batch production. Indicator of process capability and quality issues.']
  routing_version varchar [note: 'Version of the production routing used for this batch. Documents the manufacturing process steps executed.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of product scrapped during batch production. Used for yield calculation and waste reduction analysis.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between manufacturing date and expiry date. Derived from product stability testing.']
  standard_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard cost per unit for this batch based on BOM and routing. Used for cost variance analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for batch size quantities (e.g., KG, LTR, EA, CS). Aligns with GS1 standards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the master batch record document stored in Veeva Vault. Links to regulatory document management system.']
  yield_pct decimal(6,2)
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of actual batch size to planned batch size expressed as percentage. Key performance indicator for manufacturing efficiency.']

  Note: 'Electronic batch manufacturing record (eBMR) capturing the complete GMP-compliant execution history for a production batch. Captures batch number, production order reference, SKU, batch size, manufacturing date, expiry date (FEFO), facility, production line, batch status (in-process, released, rejected, quarantine, recalled), GMP deviation flag, electronic signature records, yield percentage, and Veeva Vault document reference. SSOT for GMP batch traceability per ISO 22716 and FDA 21 CFR Part 211.'
}

Table manufacturing.oee_record {
  oee_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the OEE measurement record. Primary key for the OEE record entity.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_facility. Business justification: OEE records are captured per manufacturing facility; add direct FK for accurate reporting.']
  primary_oee_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supervisor responsible for the production shift.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific production line for which OEE was measured.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing work order executed during this shift.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU that was produced during this shift. Links OEE performance to specific product.']
  work_center_id varchar
  actual_cycle_time_seconds decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average actual time in seconds to produce one unit during the shift, calculated as Actual Operating Time / Total Units Produced.']
  actual_operating_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual time the production line was operating during the shift in minutes, after subtracting unplanned downtime.']
  availability_pct decimal(6,2)
  availability_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of planned production time that the line was actually operating, calculated as (Planned Production Time - Downtime) / Planned Production Time. Component of OEE calculation.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number produced during the shift. Critical for traceability and GMP compliance.']
  changeover_count varchar [note: 'Number of product or format changeovers performed during the shift. Impacts availability and performance metrics.']
  changeover_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total time spent on changeovers during the shift in minutes.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes regarding the OEE measurement, including explanations for anomalies or performance issues.']
  created_timestamp timestamp
  data_collection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which OEE data was captured (automated from sensors, manual entry, or semi-automated).']
  downtime_minutes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total unplanned downtime during the shift in minutes, including equipment failures, material shortages, and unscheduled stoppages.']
  good_units_produced decimal(18,4) [note: 'Count of units that passed quality inspection and met specifications, calculated as Total Units Produced - Total Units Rejected.']
  ideal_cycle_time_seconds decimal(10,4) [note: 'Theoretical minimum time in seconds to produce one unit at maximum design speed under optimal conditions.']
  mes_data_source varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for the OEE data, typically Siemens Opcenter MES or equivalent system.']
  micro_stop_count varchar [note: 'Number of brief stoppages (typically under 5 minutes) that impact performance but may not be classified as downtime.']
  oee_pct decimal(6,2)
  oee_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Composite OEE metric calculated as Availability Rate × Performance Rate × Quality Rate. Single measure of manufacturing productivity and effectiveness.']
  oee_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the OEE record indicating validation and approval state.']
  operator_count varchar [note: 'Number of operators assigned to the production line during the shift.']
  performance_pct decimal(6,2)
  performance_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of ideal production speed achieved, calculated as (Ideal Cycle Time × Total Units Produced) / Actual Operating Time. Component of OEE calculation.']
  planned_production_time_minutes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total scheduled production time for the shift in minutes, excluding planned downtime such as breaks and scheduled maintenance.']
  quality_loss_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time equivalent of production lost due to quality defects and rework in minutes.']
  quality_pct decimal(6,2)
  quality_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of good units produced without defects, calculated as (Total Units Produced - Total Units Rejected) / Total Units Produced. Component of OEE calculation.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the OEE record was first created in the system.']
  record_date date
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the OEE record was last modified.']
  rework_units varchar [note: 'Count of units that required rework or reprocessing to meet quality standards during the shift.']
  scrap_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of scrap material generated during the shift in kilograms.']
  shift varchar
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the production shift occurred. Principal business event timestamp for OEE measurement.']
  shift_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the production shift (e.g., day, evening, night).']
  shift_number varchar [note: 'Sequential shift identifier within the shift date (e.g., 1 for first shift, 2 for second shift, 3 for third shift).']
  speed_loss_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time lost due to production running below ideal cycle time in minutes, representing performance inefficiency.']
  total_units_produced decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total count of units produced during the shift, including both good units and rejected units.']
  total_units_rejected varchar [note: 'Total count of units rejected due to quality defects during the shift.']
  unplanned_stop_count varchar [note: 'Number of unplanned production stoppages during the shift.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the OEE record was validated by production management.']

  Note: 'Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) measurement record captured per production line per shift. Captures OEE record ID, facility, production line, shift date, shift number, planned production time (minutes), downtime (minutes), speed loss (minutes), quality loss (minutes), availability rate, performance rate, quality rate, composite OEE percentage, total units produced, total units rejected, and MES data source (Siemens Opcenter). SSOT for line performance analytics and continuous improvement programs.'
}

Table manufacturing.downtime_event {
  downtime_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the downtime event record. Primary key for the downtime event entity.']
  downtime_reported_by_employee_id varchar
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_facility. Business justification: Downtime events are tied to a manufacturing facility; replace generic reference with direct FK.']
  primary_downtime_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production operator who reported or was present during the downtime event.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line where the downtime event occurred.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production order that was impacted by this downtime event.']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production shift during which the downtime event occurred.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific work center or machine that experienced the downtime.']
  batch_hold_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether production batches affected by this downtime event require quality hold pending investigation.']
  corrective_action varchar
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action taken to resolve the downtime event and restore production.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this downtime event record was first created in the data platform.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the financial impact amount.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or method by which the downtime event was captured (MES automatic detection, manual operator entry, sensor, SCADA).']
  downtime_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the downtime event type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanical_failure|electrical_failure|changeover|material_shortage|operator_absence|planned_maintenance|cleaning_sanitation|quality_hold|tooling_issue|system_failure|unplanned_maintenance — promote to reference product]']
  downtime_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the downtime event ended and production resumed. Null if downtime is ongoing.']
  downtime_reason_code varchar
  downtime_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the downtime event began. Captured from MES system or manual entry.']
  downtime_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime was planned (scheduled maintenance, changeover) or unplanned (breakdown, shortage).']
  duration_minutes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total duration of the downtime event measured in minutes. Calculated as the difference between end and start timestamps.']
  end_timestamp timestamp
  equipment_failure_mode varchar [note: 'Specific failure mode of the equipment that caused the downtime, used for reliability analysis and FMEA.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Level of management escalation required or triggered by this downtime event based on duration and impact.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the downtime event in local currency, including lost production value and recovery costs.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime event and its resolution comply with GMP requirements per ISO 22716.']
  is_planned boolean
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about the downtime event, root cause investigation, or resolution.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated notifications were sent to relevant stakeholders about this downtime event.']
  oee_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this downtime event is included in OEE availability loss calculations.']
  preventive_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether preventive action is required to avoid recurrence of this downtime event.']
  production_loss_units decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated number of production units lost due to this downtime event, based on standard production rate.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the downtime event was first reported or logged in the system.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the downtime event resolution process.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department responsible for addressing or preventing this type of downtime event.']
  root_cause varchar
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the root cause of the downtime event. Aligned with facility-specific root cause taxonomy.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the root cause analysis findings for this downtime event.']
  safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime event was associated with a safety incident or near-miss.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the downtime event based on impact to production schedule and safety.']
  start_timestamp timestamp
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this downtime event record was last updated in the data platform.']

  Note: 'Individual downtime event record capturing each unplanned or planned stoppage on a production line or work center. Captures event ID, facility, production line, work center, downtime start/end timestamps, duration (minutes), downtime category (mechanical failure, changeover, material shortage, operator absence, planned maintenance, cleaning/sanitation), root cause code, responsible department, production order impacted, and corrective action taken. Feeds OEE loss analysis and maintenance work order creation.'
}

Table manufacturing.changeover {
  changeover_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the changeover event. Primary key for the changeover record.']
  from_sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU that was being produced before the changeover.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_facility. Business justification: Changeover events occur at a specific manufacturing facility; link directly to manufacturing_facility.']
  primary_changeover_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary operator or technician responsible for executing the changeover.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line or work center where the changeover was executed.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production order that triggered or required this changeover event.']
  to_sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU that will be produced after the changeover.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to manufacturing.work_center']
  actual_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual elapsed time in minutes from changeover start to completion, calculated from actual start and end timestamps.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the changeover execution was completed and the line was ready for production.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the changeover execution began on the shop floor.']
  changeover_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the changeover event indicating its progress through the execution workflow.']
  changeover_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the changeover based on the nature of the change: flavor (product variant), format (packaging type), size (package size), cleaning (sanitation), material (raw material switch), or color (product color change).']
  cleaning_validated_flag boolean
  cleaning_verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the GMP cleaning verification was completed and approved by quality assurance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this changeover record was first created in the system.']
  downtime_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason for any delays or extended downtime during the changeover.']
  downtime_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the reason for any delays or extended downtime during the changeover execution.']
  duration_minutes decimal(12,2)
  end_timestamp timestamp
  equipment_adjustment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mechanical or equipment adjustments were required as part of the changeover process.']
  first_pass_yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of production output that met quality standards on the first production run immediately following the changeover, indicating changeover quality effectiveness.']
  gmp_cleaning_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required GMP cleaning and sanitation procedures were completed and verified as part of the changeover process.']
  improvement_initiative_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this changeover to a specific continuous improvement initiative or project.']
  material_waste_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of raw material or work-in-process material wasted or scrapped during the changeover, measured in kilograms.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments from operators, supervisors, or quality inspectors regarding the changeover execution, issues encountered, or lessons learned.']
  oee_impact_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated impact of this changeover event on the production lines overall equipment effectiveness for the shift or day, expressed as a percentage.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the changeover was scheduled to be completed according to the production schedule.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the changeover was scheduled to begin according to the production schedule.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the production shift during which the changeover occurred.']
  smed_improvement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this changeover was part of a SMED continuous improvement initiative or pilot program.']
  standard_duration_minutes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard or target duration in minutes for this type of changeover based on engineering standards and historical performance.']
  start_timestamp timestamp
  team_size varchar [note: 'Number of operators or technicians involved in executing the changeover.']
  tooling_change_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tooling or die changes were required as part of the changeover process.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this changeover record was last modified or updated in the system.']
  variance_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Difference in minutes between actual and standard changeover duration. Positive values indicate the changeover took longer than standard; negative values indicate faster than standard performance.']

  Note: 'Changeover execution record capturing each line or work center changeover event between production runs. Captures changeover ID, facility, production line, from-SKU, to-SKU, changeover type (flavor, format, size, cleaning), scheduled start/end, actual start/end, actual duration (minutes), standard duration (minutes), variance (minutes), changeover status (planned, in-progress, completed), operator ID, and GMP cleaning verification flag. Supports SMED improvement programs and scheduling optimization.'
}

Table manufacturing.yield_record {
  yield_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the yield tracking record. Primary key for the yield record entity.']
  batch_record_id varchar
  primary_yield_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production operator or technician who recorded the yield measurement. Links to workforce master data for training and performance tracking.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order for which this yield record is captured. Links yield data to the manufacturing order in SAP S/4HANA PP module.']
  sku_id varchar
  tertiary_yield_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or system account that last modified this yield record. Audit trail for data governance and regulatory compliance.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center (production line, machine, or station) where the yield measurement was captured. Links to manufacturing resource master data.']
  actual_yield decimal(18,4)
  actual_yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual yield percentage achieved calculated as (output_quantity / input_quantity) * 100. Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for manufacturing efficiency.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch identifier for traceability and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) compliance. Critical for product recall and quality investigations.']
  batch_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the batch record documentation. Tracks progression through Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) review and approval workflow.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes from production operators or quality personnel regarding yield performance, issues encountered, or corrective actions taken.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for this manufacturing operation. Used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) allocation and variance accounting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this yield record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data governance and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this yield record. Typically the plant\'s local currency or corporate reporting currency.']
  erp_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number in the Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system (SAP S/4HANA) that records the goods movement associated with this yield. Links to material document for inventory reconciliation.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this yield record meets Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) documentation and traceability requirements. Critical for regulatory audits and product release.']
