  actual_closure_date date [note: 'Actual date when the risk was closed or fully mitigated, enabling risk resolution cycle time analysis and mitigation effectiveness measurement.']
  affected_node_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of supply network node identifiers (plants, distribution centers, suppliers) that are exposed to this risk, supporting network resilience analysis.']
  affected_sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SKU identifiers that are exposed to this supply chain risk, enabling product-level impact analysis and mitigation planning.']
  assessed_date date [note: 'Date when the risk assessment (probability and impact scoring) was completed, establishing the baseline for risk monitoring.']
  contingency_cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial cost of implementing the contingency plan, including expedited freight, alternative sourcing premiums, and safety stock carrying costs, measured in the base currency.']
  contingency_plan varchar [note: 'Reactive response plan to be executed if the risk materializes, including alternative sourcing strategies, safety stock deployment, and business continuity procedures.']
  contingency_stock_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Additional safety stock quantity recommended to buffer against this risk, expressed in the base unit of measure. Used for inventory optimization and DRP planning.']
  contingency_stock_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the contingency stock quantity (e.g., EA, CS, KG, LB), aligned with the SKU base UOM for consistency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this risk record was first created in the system, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this risk record (impact_score, contingency_cost_estimate).']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score indicating the quality of the data record']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the risk was escalated to higher management levels, tracking the escalation timeline for governance and audit purposes.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this risk has been escalated to senior management or executive leadership due to high severity or strategic importance.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or country code where the risk is localized, using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or regional identifiers for geopolitical and natural disaster risk tracking.']
  identified_date date [note: 'Date when the supply chain risk was first identified and entered into the risk register, supporting risk aging and trend analysis.']
  impact_score decimal(15,2) [note: 'Quantitative financial estimate of potential loss or cost if the risk event occurs, measured in the base currency. Used for risk prioritization and contingency budget planning.']
  impact_severity varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the potential business impact if the risk materializes: critical (business-threatening), high (major disruption), medium (moderate impact), low (minor inconvenience), or negligible (minimal effect).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this risk record, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX, enabling change tracking and audit compliance.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent risk review or reassessment, ensuring risks are periodically evaluated as part of the S&OP and IBP governance process.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the proactive actions and controls implemented to reduce the probability or impact of the risk, including timelines and responsible parties.']
  mitigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when mitigation activities commenced, tracking the timeline of risk response execution.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next risk review or reassessment, supporting proactive risk management and compliance with review frequency policies.']
  probability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative assessment of the likelihood that the risk event will occur, expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00) based on historical data, expert judgment, and predictive analytics.']
  record_source varchar [note: 'Source system or process that created this record']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the supply chain risk type: supplier (vendor failure, capacity constraints), logistics (transportation disruption, port delays), geopolitical (trade restrictions, political instability), regulatory (compliance changes, import/export restrictions), natural_disaster (weather events, earthquakes, pandemics), or quality (product defects, contamination).']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the supply chain risk, including context, potential triggers, and business impact scenarios.']
  risk_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional context, updates, or observations related to the risk, supporting collaborative risk management and knowledge sharing.']
  risk_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the supply chain risk, formatted as RISK-NNNNNNNN for external communication and tracking.']
  risk_register_status varchar
  risk_score decimal(7,2) [note: 'Composite risk rating calculated as probability_score multiplied by impact_score, used for risk ranking and prioritization in the S&OP and IBP processes.']
  risk_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the risk: identified (newly discovered), assessed (analysis complete), active (currently impacting operations), mitigated (controls in place), closed (no longer a threat), or monitoring (under observation).']
  risk_subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing additional granularity within the primary risk category for more precise risk segmentation and analysis.']
  risk_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title summarizing the nature of the supply chain risk for quick identification and reporting.']
  source_system_record_code varchar
  target_closure_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the risk is expected to be fully mitigated or closed, used for risk management performance tracking and SLA monitoring.']

  Note: 'Master entity tracking identified supply chain risks that could disrupt supply continuity. Captures risk category (supplier, logistics, geopolitical, regulatory, natural disaster), affected SKUs/nodes, probability score, impact severity, risk owner, mitigation plan, contingency stock requirement, and risk status. Supports supply risk management and business continuity planning.'
}

Table supply.planning_exception {
  planning_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the planning exception record. Primary key for the planning exception entity.']
  primary_planning_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supply planner or demand planner assigned to resolve this exception.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU affected by this planning exception.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the plant, distribution center, or location where the planning exception occurred.']
  tertiary_planning_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the planning exception record.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the recommended action type for resolving the exception. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: expedite_order|cancel_order|increase_production|decrease_production|transfer_stock|adjust_forecast|override_plan — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  auto_resolution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exception was automatically resolved by the system without planner intervention.']
  business_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the exception if not resolved, expressed in the reporting currency.']
  constraint_type varchar [note: 'Type of supply chain constraint that contributed to the planning exception.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the planning exception record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the business impact amount.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score indicating the quality of the data record']
  demand_forecast_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The forecasted demand quantity for the affected SKU-location during the exception period.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exception was escalated to management or cross-functional teams for resolution.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'The organizational level to which the exception was escalated, if applicable.']
  exception_date date [note: 'The date when the planning exception was identified or when the exception condition is projected to occur.']
  exception_message varchar
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to the planning exception for planner reference.']
  exception_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique exception number assigned to this planning exception for tracking and reference purposes.']
  exception_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The magnitude of the exception expressed as a quantity (e.g., shortage quantity, excess quantity, unmet demand quantity).']
  exception_recurrence_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this type of exception has occurred for the same SKU-location combination within a defined time window.']
  exception_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the planning exception indicating the urgency and business impact of the issue.']
  exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the planning exception indicating its resolution progress.']
  exception_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the planning exception was detected and recorded by the IBP system.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the planning exception indicating the nature of the supply planning issue. OOS Risk (Out of Stock Risk), Excess Inventory, Late Replenishment, Unmet Demand, Capacity Breach, or Safety Stock Breach.']
  gap_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The calculated gap between demand and supply that triggered the exception (positive for shortage, negative for excess).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the planning exception record was last modified.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp
  planning_exception_status varchar
  planning_group varchar [note: 'The planning group or team responsible for managing exceptions for this SKU-location combination.']
  planning_horizon_date date [note: 'The specific date within the planning horizon where the exception condition manifests.']
  planning_run_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the IBP planning run or DRP execution run that generated this exception.']
  priority_score varchar [note: 'Calculated priority score used to rank exceptions in the planner workbench, based on severity, business impact, and urgency.']
  projected_inventory_balance decimal(18,3) [note: 'The projected inventory balance at the time of the exception, used to assess the severity of stock-out or excess inventory conditions.']
  raised_timestamp timestamp
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'System-generated or planner-defined recommended action to resolve the planning exception.']
  record_source varchar [note: 'Source system or process that created this record']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date when the planning exception was marked as resolved or closed.']
  resolution_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The elapsed time in hours from exception detection to resolution, used for performance tracking.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the actions taken to resolve the exception and the outcome.']
  resolution_status varchar
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the planning exception was marked as resolved or closed.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the root cause that triggered the planning exception.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the root cause analysis findings for the planning exception.']
  safety_stock_target decimal(18,3) [note: 'The target safety stock level for the SKU-location combination at the time of the exception.']
  source_system_record_code varchar
  supply_plan_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The planned supply quantity (production or replenishment) for the affected SKU-location during the exception period.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the exception quantity (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, PAL for pallet).']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing supply planning exceptions and alerts generated during IBP supply planning and DRP execution runs. Stores exception type (OOS risk, excess inventory, late replenishment, unmet demand, capacity breach), affected SKU/location, exception severity, exception date, recommended action, planner assignment, resolution status, resolution notes, and originating run reference. Drives exception-based planning workflows and planner workbench prioritization.'
}

Table supply.sku_planning_param {
  sku_planning_param_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SKU planning parameter record. Primary key for this entity.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or production facility where this SKU is produced or sourced. Used for plant-specific planning parameters.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU in the product master. Links planning parameters to the specific product variant being planned.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: sku_planning_param needs to be associated with the network node it applies to; added new FK supply_network_node_id referencing network_node.supply_network_node_id.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC inventory classification based on revenue contribution or consumption value. A items are high-value requiring tight control, C items are low-value with relaxed control.']
  backorder_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether backorders are permitted for this SKU when inventory is insufficient. True allows demand to be backordered, false requires immediate fulfillment or cancellation.']
  coverage_profile_days varchar [note: 'Target days of supply to maintain in inventory. Represents desired inventory coverage horizon based on demand forecast and replenishment frequency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this planning parameter record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  critical_part_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is classified as a critical part requiring special planning attention. Critical parts may have higher service levels or expedited replenishment.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score indicating the quality of the data record']
  demand_pattern_type varchar [note: 'Characterization of the SKUs historical demand pattern. Determines appropriate forecasting method and safety stock calculation approach.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which these planning parameters are no longer active. Null indicates parameters are currently active with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which these planning parameters become active and are used by planning systems. Supports time-phased parameter changes.']
  fixed_lot_size_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Fixed production or procurement lot size quantity when using fixed lot size method. Represents standard batch size for manufacturing or ordering.']
  forecast_model_type varchar [note: 'Statistical or machine learning model used for demand forecasting. Determines the algorithm applied during forecast generation in IBP or demand planning systems.']
  goods_receipt_processing_time_days varchar [note: 'Time required for quality inspection and goods receipt processing after physical delivery. Adds to total replenishment lead time.']
  inventory_valuation_method varchar [note: 'Method used for inventory valuation and consumption. FIFO (First In First Out), FEFO (First Expired First Out), LIFO (Last In First Out), or weighted average costing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this planning parameter record was last updated. Tracks most recent change for change management and audit purposes.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when planning parameters were last reviewed and validated by the planning team. Supports periodic parameter maintenance and accuracy audits.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp
  lead_time_days varchar
  lot_size_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate production or procurement lot sizes. Determines how order quantities are calculated in MRP and supply planning runs.']
  lot_size_rule varchar
  maximum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum quantity that can be ordered or produced in a single transaction. Limited by production capacity, storage constraints, or supplier capabilities.']
  maximum_stock_level_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum inventory level target for this SKU. Used in min-max planning to cap inventory investment and prevent overstock situations.']
  min_order_quantity decimal(18,3)
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered or produced in a single transaction. Enforced by supplier contracts or manufacturing constraints.']
  minimum_remaining_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Minimum remaining shelf life required at time of shipment to customer or retail delivery. Ensures product quality and retailer acceptance.']
  mrp_controller_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the MRP controller or planner responsible for this SKU. Used for workload assignment and exception management routing.']
  mrp_type varchar [note: 'MRP procedure that controls how the planning system generates procurement proposals. Determines planning logic and automation level.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for next planning parameter review. Ensures regular validation of planning assumptions and parameter accuracy.']
  order_multiple_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity increment in which orders must be placed. All order quantities must be multiples of this value due to packaging or production constraints.']
  parameter_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special planning considerations, parameter rationale, or exceptions for this SKU. Provides context for planning decisions.']
  parameter_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the planning parameter record. Active parameters are used in planning runs, inactive parameters are ignored.']
  planned_delivery_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard lead time in days from order placement to goods receipt. Used for MRP scheduling and supply plan timing calculations.']
  planning_group varchar [note: 'IBP planning group assignment for demand and supply planning segmentation. Groups SKUs with similar planning characteristics for collaborative planning processes.']
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days into the future for which demand and supply planning is performed. Defines the forward-looking planning window for this SKU.']
  planning_method varchar
  planning_strategy varchar [note: 'Manufacturing planning strategy that determines how the SKU is planned and produced. Make-to-stock produces to forecast, make-to-order produces against customer orders.']
  planning_time_fence_days varchar [note: 'Number of days within which the planning system cannot automatically change planned orders without planner approval. Protects near-term production schedule stability.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is externally procured from suppliers, produced in-house, or both. Determines supply planning logic and sourcing strategy.']
  record_source varchar [note: 'Source system or process that created this record']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment order. Calculated based on lead time demand plus safety stock to prevent stockouts.']
  rounding_profile_code varchar [note: 'Code defining rounding rules for planned order quantities. Ensures quantities align with packaging units, pallet configurations, or production batch constraints.']
  safety_stock_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate safety stock levels. Fixed quantity uses static buffer, service level based uses demand variability and lead time uncertainty.']
  seasonality_profile_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the seasonality pattern applied to this SKU for demand forecasting. Links to predefined seasonal index curves (e.g., summer peak, holiday spike).']
  service_level_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target service level percentage for this SKU (e.g., 95%, 98%, 99%). Drives safety stock calculation to achieve desired product availability.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Total shelf life of the SKU in days from production date to expiration. Critical for FEFO inventory management and demand planning for perishable goods.']
  sku_planning_param_status varchar
  source_system_record_code varchar
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for all planning quantities (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, KG for kilogram). Standardizes quantity representation across planning processes.']
  xyz_classification varchar [note: 'XYZ demand variability classification. X items have stable demand, Y items have moderate variability, Z items have highly erratic or sporadic demand patterns.']

  Note: 'Master entity storing SKU-level planning parameters used in demand and supply planning calculations. Captures planning horizon, lot size, minimum order quantity (MOQ), shelf life (days), FEFO/FIFO flag, planning strategy (make-to-stock/make-to-order), ABC/XYZ classification, demand pattern type, seasonality index, and IBP planning group assignment. Distinct from product master (owned by product domain) as this captures supply-planning-specific parameters.'
}

Table supply.inventory_projection {
  inventory_projection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory projection record. Primary key for this transactional projection entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supply planner or planning team responsible for this SKU-location combination. Enables accountability and collaboration.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU for which inventory is being projected. Links to the product master data.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location (distribution center, plant, warehouse) for which inventory is projected.']
  atp_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity available to promise to new customer orders on the projection date, calculated from projected balance minus committed allocations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory projection record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  ctp_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity capable to promise considering future production capacity and material availability, extending beyond current ATP.']
  days_of_supply decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected number of days the available inventory balance will cover forecasted demand. Critical metric for inventory health and service level assessment.']
  demand_source varchar [note: 'Source system or process that provided the demand forecast used in this projection (e.g., statistical forecast, demand sensing, customer orders, consensus demand).']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Standardized exception or alert code triggered by this projection (e.g., LOW_STOCK, EXCESS_INV, CAPACITY_CONSTRAINT). Supports exception-based planning workflows.']
  excess_inventory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that the projected available balance exceeds maximum stock levels or days-of-supply targets, signaling potential overstock risk.']
  forecast_accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical forecast accuracy percentage for this SKU-location combination, used to assess confidence in the projection and adjust safety stock.']
  inventory_projection_status varchar
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory projection record was last updated. Tracks changes and supports version control.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp
  oos_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that the projected available balance falls below safety stock or service level thresholds, signaling potential stockout risk.']
  planning_bucket varchar [note: 'Time granularity of the projection. Daily for near-term tactical planning; weekly or monthly for mid-term; quarterly for long-term strategic planning.']
  planning_group varchar [note: 'Organizational planning group or segment responsible for this projection (e.g., regional planning team, product category planning team).']
  planning_run_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the supply planning run that generated this projection. Enables traceability to planning execution and version control.']
  planning_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the supply planning run that generated this projection was executed. Distinct from record creation; represents the business event time.']
  projected_available_balance decimal(18,3) [note: 'Net projected inventory balance after accounting for on-hand, receipts, and demand. Key metric for Available-to-Promise (ATP) calculations.']
  projected_available_quantity decimal(18,3)
  projected_days_of_supply decimal(10,3)
  projected_demand_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Forecasted demand (consumption, sales, or shipments) expected to occur on the projection date. Input from demand planning or demand sensing.']
  projected_on_hand_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Forecasted physical inventory quantity expected to be available on-hand at the location on the projection date. Core output of supply planning calculation.']
  projected_on_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Forecasted quantity of inventory currently on order (in-transit or planned receipts) expected to arrive by or on the projection date.']
  projected_receipt_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Forecasted quantity of planned receipts (production output, purchase orders, or replenishment) expected to arrive on the projection date.']
  projection_confidence_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of confidence in this projection based on demand volatility, forecast accuracy, and supply reliability.']
  projection_date date [note: 'The specific future date for which this inventory projection applies. Core dimension for time-series inventory planning.']
  projection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments from the planner regarding assumptions, exceptions, or special considerations for this projection.']
  projection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this projection record. Indicates whether the projection is in draft, approved for execution, published to downstream systems, or superseded by a newer version.']
  projection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the projection methodology. Baseline uses unconstrained demand; constrained applies capacity limits; optimized applies optimization algorithms; scenario and simulation support what-if analysis.']
  projection_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this projection scenario. Supports multiple what-if scenarios and plan iterations within the same planning cycle.']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The reorder point threshold used in the planning logic. When projected balance falls below this level, replenishment is triggered.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The safety stock target quantity used as the baseline for OOS risk assessment on the projection date. May vary by date if dynamic safety stock policies are applied.']
  service_level_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target service level (fill rate or in-stock probability) that the projection aims to achieve. Used to calibrate safety stock and OOS risk thresholds.']
  source_system_record_code varchar
  supply_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score assessing supply chain vulnerabilities (supplier reliability, lead time variability, geopolitical risk) that may impact this projection.']
  supply_source varchar [note: 'Source of supply for projected receipts (e.g., internal production, external procurement, transfer from another location).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which all projected quantities are expressed (e.g., EA, CS, KG, LB). Must be consistent across all quantity fields in this record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the forward-looking inventory projection (projected available balance) for each SKU/location/date combination as output from the supply planning run. Stores projected on-hand, projected on-order, projected demand, projected receipts, days-of-supply projection, OOS risk flag, excess inventory flag, and planning run reference. Distinct from physical inventory positions (owned by inventory domain).'
}

Table supply.demand_event {
  demand_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand event record. Primary key.']
  channel_classification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution channel or sales channel affected by this demand event.']
  demand_sku_id varchar
  primary_demand_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand planner or business user responsible for creating and maintaining this demand event.']
  primary_demand_sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU affected by this demand event. For multi-SKU events, this may reference a product family or be null with SKU scope defined elsewhere.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: REQUIRED: Demand events (e.g., product launches) are linked to a promotion event for lift analysis and spend tracking.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: demand_event should be linked to the network node where the event impacts supply; added new FK supply_network_node_id referencing network_node.supply_network_node_id.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or retail account affected by this demand event. Null for market-wide events.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this demand event requires formal approval before being included in the demand plan. True for high-impact or high-risk events.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this demand event was approved.']
  cannibalization_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'For new product launches, the percentage of new product demand that is expected to cannibalize existing SKU demand rather than represent net-new volume.']
  confidence_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level or business certainty that the event will occur and deliver the projected volume impact. Used for risk-adjusted planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this demand event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts in this record.']
  demand_event_status varchar
  discontinuation_reason varchar [note: 'For product discontinuation events, the business rationale for discontinuing the SKU (e.g., low sales, regulatory, reformulation, portfolio rationalization).']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code for the demand event, used for external reference and cross-system tracking.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the demand event, including business rationale, scope, and expected market impact.']
  event_end_date date [note: 'The date when the demand event ceases to impact demand. Null for indefinite events such as permanent price changes or discontinuations.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the demand event for business user identification and reporting.']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for planner comments, assumptions, risks, or additional context about the demand event.']
  event_quantity_impact decimal(18,3)
  event_start_date date [note: 'The date when the demand event begins to impact demand. For promotions, this is the promotion start date. For launches, this is the market availability date.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demand event. Controls whether the event is included in demand planning calculations.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the demand event indicating the nature of the business activity that creates demand variance. Determines which event-specific attributes are populated.']
  initial_stocking_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'For new product launches, the initial pipeline fill quantity required to stock distribution centers and retail shelves before consumer demand begins.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this demand event record was most recently updated.']
  last_order_date date [note: 'For product discontinuation events, the final date on which orders for the SKU will be accepted.']
  last_ship_date date [note: 'For product discontinuation events, the final date on which the SKU will be shipped to customers.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp
  launch_channel_scope varchar [note: 'For new product launches, describes which distribution channels and customer segments are included in the launch (e.g., DTC-only, mass retail, specialty, omnichannel).']
  market_geography_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code or market region code where the demand event applies. For new product launches, indicates launch market scope.']
  planning_run_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand planning run or Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) cycle in which this event was incorporated into the forecast.']
  price_change_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For price change events, the absolute change in unit price. Positive for price increases, negative for price decreases.']
  price_change_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'For price change events, the percentage change in unit price relative to the previous price.']
  price_elasticity_coefficient decimal(6,4) [note: 'For price change events, the estimated price elasticity of demand used to calculate volume impact. Negative values indicate normal goods (price up, demand down).']
  promotion_funding_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'For trade promotion events, the total budget allocated to fund the promotion, including trade spend, discounts, and allowances.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'For trade promotion events, the specific promotion mechanism (e.g., temporary price reduction, buy-one-get-one, display allowance, feature ad).']
  ramp_up_weeks varchar [note: 'For new product launches, the number of weeks over which demand is expected to ramp from zero to steady-state volume.']
  source_system_event_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this demand event in the source system, used for traceability and reconciliation.']
  source_system_record_code varchar
  supply_readiness_status varchar [note: 'For new product launches, indicates whether manufacturing, procurement, and supply chain are ready to support the launch volume. Critical gate for launch approval.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for volume impact quantities. Aligns with SKU base unit of measure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|KG|LB|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  volume_impact_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage change in demand relative to the statistical baseline forecast. Positive for uplift, negative for downlift.']
  volume_impact_units decimal(18,3) [note: 'Absolute incremental or decremental demand volume attributed to this event, expressed in base units. Positive for uplift events, negative for downlift events.']

  Note: 'Master entity capturing planned business events that create demand uplift or downlift adjustments to the statistical forecast. Includes trade promotions, new product launches (with ramp-up volume curves, cannibalization impacts, and supply readiness status), product discontinuations, price changes, seasonal events, and market expansion activities. Stores event type, affected SKUs, affected customers/channels, volume impact (units and percentage), event start/end date, confidence level, initial stocking quantity (for launches), launch market/channel scope, and source system reference. Provides the causal layer that explains forecast adjustments beyond statistical extrapolation and bridges R&D/product NPD pipeline with supply chain readiness planning.'
}

Table supply.otif_target {
  otif_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the OTIF service level target record.']
  sku_id varchar
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: otif_target is defined per supply node; added new FK supply_network_node_id referencing network_node.supply_network_node_id.']
  target_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: OTIF Performance Ownership assigns a responsible employee for each OTIF target in the OTIF Management Dashboard.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific customer account for which this OTIF target is defined. Null if target applies to a customer segment or channel rather than individual customer.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this OTIF target was formally approved and authorized for use in supply planning and performance measurement.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for this OTIF target definition, indicating whether it has been reviewed and approved by authorized stakeholders.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who approved this OTIF target definition, typically a supply chain director or customer service leader.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Distribution channel classification for which this OTIF target is defined (retail, wholesale, direct-to-consumer, e-commerce, foodservice, export).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this OTIF target record was first created in the system.']
  customer_segment_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the customer segment (e.g., national retailers, regional chains, independent stores) to which this OTIF target applies.']
  delivery_tolerance_hours varchar [note: 'Acceptable time window variance (in hours) before or after the scheduled delivery time within which delivery is still considered on-time.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this OTIF target expires or is superseded by a new target definition. Null indicates an open-ended commitment.']
  effective_from_date date
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this OTIF target becomes effective and is used for performance measurement and compliance evaluation.']
  escalation_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Performance threshold below which OTIF failures trigger escalation to senior management or customer service recovery actions.']
  in_full_fill_rate_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage for in-full order fulfillment, representing the proportion of orders that must be delivered with 100% of requested quantity.']
  in_full_target_percentage decimal(5,2)
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this OTIF target record, used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of this OTIF target definition to assess continued relevance and appropriateness.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which OTIF performance against this target is measured and reported to stakeholders.']
  measurement_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days over which OTIF performance is measured and evaluated against the target (e.g., rolling 30 days, quarterly 90 days).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this OTIF target to ensure alignment with business strategy and customer expectations.']
  on_time_delivery_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage for on-time delivery performance, representing the proportion of orders that must be delivered within the agreed delivery window.']
  on_time_target_percentage decimal(5,2)
  otif_composite_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite OTIF target percentage representing orders that must meet both on-time AND in-full criteria simultaneously. This is the primary service level commitment metric.']
  otif_target_status varchar
  penalty_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether financial penalties or chargebacks apply when OTIF performance falls below the target threshold.']
  penalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to calculate financial penalties when OTIF targets are not met, typically applied to order value or invoice amount.']
  percentage decimal(5,2)
  planning_group varchar [note: 'Supply planning team or organizational unit responsible for achieving this OTIF target and managing associated supply plans.']
  priority_tier varchar [note: 'Priority classification for this OTIF target, indicating the strategic importance and resource allocation priority (tier 1 = highest priority customers).']
  product_category_code varchar [note: 'Product category or hierarchy level to which this OTIF target applies, enabling category-specific service level commitments.']
  quantity_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable variance percentage for order quantity fulfillment. Orders within this tolerance are considered in-full (e.g., 98% fulfillment may be acceptable).']
  retailer_mandated_target_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this OTIF target is mandated by the retailer/customer as part of their vendor compliance program versus internally set by the manufacturer.']
  sla_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the formal SLA document or contract section that defines this OTIF commitment.']
  source_system_record_code varchar
  target_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the OTIF target definition, used for external reference and reporting.']
  target_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the OTIF target configuration, typically indicating the customer segment or channel it applies to.']
  target_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, special conditions, or business rationale for this OTIF target definition.']
  target_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the business owner or supply chain manager accountable for achieving this OTIF target.']
  target_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the OTIF target definition (active, inactive, pending approval, expired, suspended).']

  Note: 'Master entity defining On-Time-In-Full (OTIF) service level targets by customer, channel, and product category. Captures on-time delivery target (%), in-full fill rate target (%), OTIF composite target (%), measurement window, penalty clause flag, retailer-mandated target flag, and effective date range. Provides the service level commitment baseline for supply planning and distribution execution.'
}

Table supply.atp_rule {
  atp_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for atp_rule']
  fallback_atp_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on atp_rule (fallback_atp_rule_id)']
  sku_id varchar
  supply_network_node_id varchar
  action_expression varchar [note: 'Expression defining the allocation or availability action taken when the condition is met.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate inventory when the rule is applied.']
  atp_rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the rule\'s purpose and logic.']
  atp_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  condition_expression varchar [note: 'Logical expression that must be satisfied for the rule to fire.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the ATP rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the ATP rule record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the rule becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the rule is no longer applicable (null if open‑ended).']
  is_active boolean
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this rule is the default fallback rule.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp
  max_allocation_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper limit on quantity that can be allocated by this rule.']
  min_allocation_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower limit on quantity that must be allocated when the rule fires.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority determining rule evaluation order; lower numbers indicate higher precedence.']
  priority_rank varchar
  rule_category varchar [note: 'Supply‑chain domain where the rule is applied (e.g., order level, line level, inventory level).']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used to reference the ATP rule in downstream systems.']
  rule_logic varchar
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the ATP rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of the ATP rule defining its logical purpose.']
  safety_stock_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to safety stock calculations within the rule.']
  source_system_record_code varchar
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the ATP rule record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for atp_rule. Referenced by atp_rule_id.'
}

Table supply.planning_period {
  planning_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for planning_period']
  previous_planning_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on planning_period (previous_planning_period_id)']
  supply_network_node_id varchar
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the planning period record was created.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score indicating the quality of the data record']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days covered by the planning period.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the period becomes effective for planning purposes.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the period ceases to be effective (nullable for open‑ended).']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar day of the planning period.']
  is_closed boolean
  is_historical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the period is historical (true) or future (false).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp
  period_code varchar [note: 'External code used in planning systems to reference the period.']
  period_end_date date
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the planning period (e.g., FY2024 Q1).']
  period_start_date date
  period_type varchar [note: 'Category of the period (fiscal, calendar, rolling, forecast).']
  planning_horizon varchar [note: 'Planning horizon classification for the period.']
  planning_period_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the planning period purpose.']
  planning_period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the period.']
  record_source varchar [note: 'Source system or process that created this record']
  source_system_record_code varchar
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar day of the planning period.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Time zone applicable to the period timestamps.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the planning period record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for planning_period. Referenced by planning_period_id.'
}

Table supply.planning_run {
  planning_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for planning_run']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that initiated the planning run.']
  previous_planning_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on planning_run (previous_planning_run_id)']
  supply_network_node_id varchar
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the planning run record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score indicating the quality of the data record']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the input data used for the planning run.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time of the run measured in minutes.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Error details captured when the run fails or encounters issues.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations captured by the planner.']
  outcome_metric varchar [note: 'Key quantitative metric produced by the run (e.g., total forecast error, service level).']
  planning_algorithm varchar [note: 'Name or code of the algorithm/model used to generate the plan.']
  planning_horizon_end date [note: 'Last date of the planning horizon covered by the run.']
  planning_horizon_start date [note: 'First date of the planning horizon covered by the run.']
  planning_run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the planning run.']
  record_source varchar [note: 'Source system or process that created this record']
  result_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level textual summary of the planning outcomes.']
  run_completed_timestamp timestamp
  run_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the planning run execution finished.']
  run_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name given to the planning run for easy identification.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or code used by business users to identify the run.']
  run_priority varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned priority level influencing scheduling and resource allocation.']
  run_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the planning run execution began.']
  run_started_timestamp timestamp
  run_status varchar
  run_type varchar [note: 'Category of planning activity performed in the run (e.g., demand, supply, inventory, S&OP, optimization).']
  scenario_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the scenario (e.g., baseline, best‑case, worst‑case) used for this run.']
  source_system_record_code varchar
  success_flag boolean [note: 'True if the run completed without errors; otherwise False.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the planning run record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the run, useful for tracking iterative improvements.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for planning_run. Referenced by planning_run_id.'
}

Table supply.drp_run {
  drp_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for drp_run']
  drp_last_successful_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the most recent successful DRP run preceding this one.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the planner (person or team) responsible for initiating the DRP run.']
  planning_run_id varchar
  primary_previous_drp_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on drp_run (previous_drp_run_id)']
  supply_network_node_id varchar
  algorithm_version varchar [note: 'Version of the DRP algorithm or model applied during the run.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the planner or system about the DRP run.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the run passed all configured compliance validations.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or service that created the DRP run record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DRP run record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values in this DRP run.']
  data_snapshot_date date [note: 'Date of the master data snapshot (inventory, demand) used as input for the run.']
  drp_run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DRP run.']
  drp_status varchar
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DRP run execution finished or was terminated.']
  forecast_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the demand forecast used for this DRP run.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DRP run was triggered automatically (true) or manually (false).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp
  net_requirement_quantity decimal(18,3)
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days covered by the DRP planning horizon.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the DRP run.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO region code indicating the geographic scope of the DRP run.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk score for the supply plan generated by this run (0‑100 scale).']
  run_code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the DRP run in reports and interfaces.']
  run_date date
  run_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total execution time of the DRP run measured in seconds.']
  run_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose or scenario of the DRP run.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DRP run execution mode.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Name of the planning scenario (e.g., "Base", "Optimistic", "Pessimistic").']
  source_system_record_code varchar
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DRP run execution began.']
  total_actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate cost realized after execution of the supply plan (if available).']
  total_planned_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate cost forecasted for the supply plan generated by this DRP run.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or service that last updated the DRP run record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the DRP run record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for drp_run. Referenced by drp_run_id.'
}

Table sales.trade_account {
  trade_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  account_segment_id varchar
  parent_trade_account_id varchar
  territory_id varchar
  account_hierarchy_id varchar
  account_name varchar
  account_number varchar
  account_status varchar
  account_type varchar
  annual_revenue decimal(18,2)
  created_timestamp timestamp
  credit_limit decimal(18,2)

}

Table distribution.distribution_facility {
  distribution_facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for facility']
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Facilities have specific ESG commitments (energy, waste, emissions) that are tracked for performance dashboards and compliance.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tracks the facility manager employee for each distribution center, required for operational performance metrics and compliance reporting.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Links DC to supply network node; distinct from manufacturing_facility which is a production site']
  address_line1 varchar
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the distribution center facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or unit information. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  capacity_sqft decimal(18,2)
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the distribution center is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution center ceased operations. Null for active facilities. Used for historical analysis and network optimization studies.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the distribution center. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the distribution center. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the distribution center is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution center record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  cross_dock_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility supports cross-docking operations where inbound goods are directly transferred to outbound shipments with minimal storage time.']
  cycle_count_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Standard frequency in days for cycle counting inventory at this facility. Used for inventory accuracy management and audit compliance.']
  dc_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the distribution center used across operational systems. Externally-known unique code for the facility.']
  dc_name varchar [note: 'Official business name of the distribution center or warehouse facility.']
  distribution_co_located_manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'FK to manufacturing facility if co-located']
  distribution_facility_status varchar
  distribution_manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'FK to manufacturing facility']
  dock_doors_inbound varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors designated for inbound receiving operations. Impacts receiving throughput capacity and scheduling.']
  dock_doors_outbound varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors designated for outbound shipping operations. Impacts shipping throughput capacity and carrier scheduling.']
  dsd_hub_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this facility serves as a DSD hub for direct-to-retail delivery operations. DSD hubs bypass traditional distribution channels for faster store replenishment.']
  facility_code varchar
  facility_name varchar
  facility_purpose varchar [note: 'Purpose classification: distribution, cross-dock, hub']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution center based on temperature control and operational model. Ambient for room temperature, chilled for refrigerated, frozen for sub-zero, multi-temperature for mixed zones, DSD hub for Direct Store Delivery operations, cross-dock for flow-through distribution.']
  fsc_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified under FSC chain of custody standards for sustainable sourcing. Required for handling FSC-certified paper and wood-based products.']
  gmp_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified under Good Manufacturing Practice standards. Required for handling cosmetics, personal care, and certain consumer goods products.']
  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified to handle and store hazardous materials. Required for distribution of products with EPA or OSHA regulated substances.']
  inventory_rotation_method varchar [note: 'Primary inventory rotation method used at this facility. FIFO (First In First Out) for standard goods, FEFO (First Expired First Out) for perishables and date-sensitive products, LIFO (Last In First Out) for specific use cases.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the distribution center in decimal degrees. Used for geospatial analytics, route optimization, and logistics planning.']
  lease_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the facility lease agreement. Null for owned facilities. Used for lease renewal planning and facility strategy.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the distribution center in decimal degrees. Used for geospatial analytics, route optimization, and logistics planning.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution center record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  opened_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution center commenced operations. Used for facility age analysis and depreciation calculations.']
  operating_hours_weekday varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekdays in format HH:MM-HH:MM. Used for scheduling inbound deliveries, outbound shipments, and workforce planning.']
  operating_hours_weekend varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekends in format HH:MM-HH:MM. Used for scheduling weekend operations and carrier coordination.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the distribution center in its lifecycle. Active indicates full operations, inactive for temporarily closed, under construction for new facilities being built, decommissioned for permanently closed, seasonal for facilities operating only during peak periods, maintenance for temporary closure due to upgrades or repairs.']
  osa_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OSA performance percentage for stores served by this distribution center. OSA measures product availability at retail shelf level.']
  otif_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OTIF performance percentage for this distribution center. OTIF measures the percentage of orders delivered on time and in full, a key supply chain KPI.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of facility ownership model. Owned for company-owned facilities, leased for long-term leased properties, third-party logistics for 3PL-operated warehouses.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the distribution center address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  sap_plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA plant code representing this distribution center in the ERP system. Maps to the SAP MM and WM modules for material management and warehouse management.']
  sap_storage_location varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA storage location code within the plant. Represents the primary storage location identifier for inventory transactions in SAP WM module.']
  shifts_per_day varchar [note: 'Number of operational shifts per day at the facility. Used for labor planning, throughput capacity modeling, and operational cost analysis.']
  source_system_code varchar
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or regional administrative division where the facility is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  storage_capacity_pallet_positions varchar [note: 'Maximum number of pallet positions available for storage. Key metric for inventory capacity planning and space utilization analysis.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility has temperature-controlled storage zones. True for facilities with chilled or frozen capabilities, false for ambient-only.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the distribution center location. Used for scheduling, shift planning, and cross-facility coordination.']
  total_capacity_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total warehouse floor space capacity in square feet. Includes all storage, staging, and operational areas within the facility.']
  wms_site_code varchar [note: 'Unique site identifier in the Blue Yonder WMS system. Used for integration and synchronization between the lakehouse and the operational WMS platform.']

  Note: 'Physical distribution center or warehouse used for storing and shipping finished goods to customers. SSOT owner for distribution/logistics facility master. Distinct from manufacturing_facility which represents production plants.'
}

Table distribution.distribution_storage_location {
  distribution_storage_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for storage_location']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent distribution center facility where this storage location resides.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'Velocity-based classification of the location for slotting optimization: A (high-velocity/fast-moving), B (medium-velocity), C (low-velocity/slow-moving).']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Aisle designation within the warehouse layout for physical navigation and picking route optimization.']
  bay varchar [note: 'Bay or column position within the aisle for precise horizontal location reference.']
  bin_position varchar [note: 'Specific bin or slot position within the level for granular inventory placement and retrieval.']
  blocked_date date [note: 'Date when the storage location was blocked or made unavailable for operations.']
  blocked_flag boolean
  blocked_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the location is blocked or unavailable, such as maintenance, damage, safety hold, or quality quarantine.']
  capacity_units decimal(18,3)
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the storage location record was first created in the WMS system.']
  cycle_count_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for cycle counting inventory at this location to maintain inventory accuracy and compliance.']
  distribution_storage_location_level varchar [note: 'Vertical level or shelf position within the bay for height-based slotting and equipment compatibility.']
  distribution_storage_location_status varchar
  dsd_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location is designated for DSD operations where products bypass the DC and are delivered directly to retail stores.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the storage location became active and available for warehouse operations.']
  equipment_type_required varchar [note: 'Type of material handling equipment required to access this storage location for put-away and picking operations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the storage location is scheduled to be decommissioned or removed from active use. Null for indefinite locations.']
  fefo_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location supports FEFO inventory rotation logic for expiry-sensitive products (pharmaceuticals, food, cosmetics).']
  fifo_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location supports FIFO inventory rotation logic for age-based stock management.']
  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location is certified and equipped for storing hazardous materials per OSHA and EPA regulations.']
  height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical height dimension of the storage location in centimeters for vertical clearance and equipment reach validation.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent cycle count was performed at this storage location.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the storage location record was most recently updated in the WMS system.']
  length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical length dimension of the storage location in centimeters for dimensional slotting and equipment compatibility.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the storage location as defined in Blue Yonder WMS. Typically formatted as aisle-bay-level-bin (e.g., A01-B05-L03-P12).']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the storage location for operational reference.']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the storage location indicating availability for inventory placement and retrieval.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the storage location by operational function: bulk storage, active pick face, reserve inventory, staging area, dock door, or cross-dock zone.']
  mixed_lot_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location permits storage of multiple lot or batch numbers for the same SKU or requires lot segregation.']
  mixed_sku_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location permits storage of multiple SKUs simultaneously or requires single-SKU dedication.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp
  pick_face_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is designated as an active pick face for order fulfillment operations.']
  picking_strategy varchar [note: 'Order fulfillment picking method supported by this location: batch picking, wave picking, zone picking, discrete (single-order), or cluster picking.']
  putaway_strategy varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to assign incoming inventory to this location: directed (system-assigned), random, fixed (dedicated SKU), or dynamic (velocity-based).']
  replenishment_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for automated replenishment from reserve to pick locations, with lower numbers indicating higher priority.']
  source_system_code varchar
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control classification for the storage location to ensure product integrity and regulatory compliance (ambient, refrigerated 2-8°C, frozen <-18°C, controlled room temperature).']
  volume_capacity_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum volume capacity of the storage location in cubic meters for space utilization and slotting algorithms.']
  weight_capacity_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity of the storage location in kilograms for safe load management and slotting optimization.']
  width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical width dimension of the storage location in centimeters for dimensional slotting and equipment compatibility.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Logical zone classification within the distribution center for grouping locations by product category, velocity, or operational workflow (e.g., FAST-PICK, SLOW-MOVE, HAZMAT).']

  Note: 'Physical storage bin/slot/rack within a distribution facility (WMS-managed). SSOT for physical DC location layout. Distinct from inventory.inventory_storage_location which is the logical inventory holding location.'
}

Table distribution.inbound_receipt {
  inbound_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inbound receipt transaction. Primary key for this entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier that delivered the shipment to the distribution center.']
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Receiving operations incur expenses (labor, equipment) that are allocated to a cost center for expense tracking and budgeting.']
  inbound_distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center facility where the goods were received.']
  inbound_warehouse_distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific warehouse or storage facility within the distribution center where goods were received.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_facility. Business justification: Internal transfer receipt: inbound receipts from own plants need the source manufacturing facility to track internal logistics and cost allocation.']
  primary_inbound_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality control personnel who performed the inspection, used for accountability and audit trail.']
  purchase_order_id varchar
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Required for R&D material receipt tracking; links each inbound receipt to the RD project that requested the raw material for formulation trials.']
  receiving_clerk_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse employee who processed the inbound receipt, used for labor tracking and accountability.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or vendor who shipped the goods, representing the counterparty in this receipt transaction.']
  accepted_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of goods accepted into inventory after quality inspection, excluding rejected or damaged items.']
  actual_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the physical goods arrived and were checked in at the receiving dock, representing the principal business event for this transaction.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Advanced Shipping Notice document sent by the supplier prior to shipment arrival, enabling pre-receipt planning and dock scheduling.']
  case_count varchar [note: 'Number of cases or cartons received in this inbound shipment.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO standard container identifier for ocean freight shipments, following the BIC (Bureau International des Containers) format.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this receipt record was first created in the system, representing the audit trail start point.']
  discrepancy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether any discrepancies (quantity, quality, or documentation) were identified during the receiving process.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional details about any discrepancies identified during the receiving process.']
  discrepancy_reason varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of discrepancy identified during receiving, used for root cause analysis and supplier performance management.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Physical dock door location where the inbound shipment was unloaded, used for labor planning and dock utilization tracking.']
  expected_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of goods expected to be received based on the ASN or purchase order, used for discrepancy detection.']
  goods_receipt_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP Material Management (MM) goods receipt document number generated upon posting the receipt to inventory, linking WMS to ERP.']
  inbound_receipt_status varchar
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this receipt record was last updated, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp
  otif_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this receipt met both on-time delivery and complete quantity requirements, key supplier performance metric.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets received in this inbound shipment, used for dock capacity planning and labor allocation.']
  putaway_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when all received goods were moved from the receiving dock to their designated storage locations, completing the inbound process.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether formal quality inspection was required for this receipt based on product category, supplier risk profile, or regulatory requirements.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the quality inspection process for this receipt, tracking progression through quality control workflow.']
  receipt_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when all receiving activities (unloading, inspection, put-away) were completed and the receipt was closed in the WMS.']
  receipt_date date
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the inbound receipt, used for tracking and reference across systems and with suppliers.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inbound receipt transaction, tracking progression from scheduling through completion or exception handling.']
  receipt_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inbound receipt based on the source and nature of the goods being received.']
  received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual total quantity of goods physically received and counted during the receiving process.']
  rejected_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of goods rejected during receiving due to quality issues, damage, or non-conformance to specifications.']
  scheduled_receipt_date date [note: 'Planned date for the inbound shipment to arrive at the distribution center, based on ASN or supplier commitment.']
  seal_intact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the security seal was found intact upon arrival, critical for quality control and loss prevention.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal identifier applied to the trailer or container, verified upon receipt to ensure shipment integrity and prevent tampering.']
  source_system_code varchar
  temperature_check_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether temperature verification was required for this receipt due to cold chain or temperature-sensitive product requirements.']
  temperature_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the recorded temperature was within acceptable range per product specifications and cold chain requirements.']
  temperature_reading_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual temperature reading in Celsius recorded during receipt inspection for temperature-sensitive products, critical for cold chain compliance.']
  total_quantity decimal(18,4)
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the trailer or truck that delivered the shipment, used for tracking and carrier performance analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the quantities recorded in this receipt (Each, Case, Pallet, Kilogram, Pound, Liter, Gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|EA|KG|LB|LT|GL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the physical receipt of goods at a DC from suppliers, manufacturing plants, or inter-DC transfers. Records ASN reference, carrier, trailer/container ID, dock door, receipt date/time, received quantity by SKU/lot, temperature check results, and discrepancy flags. Integrates with SAP WM goods receipt and Blue Yonder WMS inbound processing. Drives inventory on-hand updates and FEFO/FIFO lot registration.'
}

Table distribution.inbound_receipt_line {
  inbound_receipt_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inbound receipt line. Primary key for this entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the warehouse associate who physically received and recorded this line. Used for accountability and performance tracking.']
  inbound_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent inbound receipt header transaction. Links this line to the overall receipt event.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Needed to associate each receipt line (specific SKU/lot) with the RD project using it, enabling traceability of trial material consumption.']
  regulatory_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.registration. Business justification: Regulatory registration verification during inbound receipt ensures each received SKU is registered in the jurisdiction, a standard compliance step.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Link receipt line to SKU master for traceability and quality inspection; required by receipt processing and compliance audit reports.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor who shipped the goods. Used for supplier performance tracking and procurement analytics.']
  asn_line_number varchar [note: 'Line number within the ASN document corresponding to this receipt line. Enables automated matching between ASN and physical receipt.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Advanced Shipping Notice document number sent by the supplier prior to shipment arrival. Used for pre-receipt planning and variance detection.']
  condition_code varchar [note: 'Quality condition assessment of the received goods. Determines whether product can be put away into available inventory or requires special handling.']
