  gps_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate captured at delivery location for route verification and geospatial analytics.']
  in_full_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complete ordered quantity was delivered without shortages. Component of OTIF performance measurement.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales invoice document generated for this DSD delivery. Links to financial billing and accounts receivable.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this delivery record was last updated in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.']
  merchandising_activity_type varchar [note: 'The type of in-store merchandising activity performed during the delivery visit. Supports retail execution and On Shelf Availability (OSA) objectives.']
  merchandising_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The total time in minutes spent on merchandising activities during the store visit. Used for labor productivity analysis.']
  merchandising_performed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether in-store merchandising activities (shelf stocking, rotation, display setup) were performed during this delivery visit.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp
  net_delivery_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The net monetary value of the delivery after subtracting return credits from gross delivery value. Represents actual secondary sales value.']
  on_time_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery arrived within the agreed delivery time window. Component of OTIF performance measurement.']
  otif_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery met On Time In Full (OTIF) performance criteria: arrived within delivery window and delivered complete order quantity.']
  pod_capture_method varchar [note: 'The method used to capture proof of delivery from the store recipient. Electronic signature and photo are preferred for audit trail.']
  pod_photo_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the photo evidence of delivery captured at the store. Links to document management system for audit purposes.']
  pod_signature_image_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the stored electronic signature image captured as proof of delivery. Links to document management system.']
  proof_of_delivery_flag boolean
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'The name of the store employee who received and acknowledged the delivery. Captured for proof of delivery and accountability.']
  return_credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of product returns credited to the store during this visit. Reduces net delivery value.']
  scheduled_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'The planned arrival timestamp at the retail store based on route optimization and store delivery window requirements.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this delivery record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  temperature_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temperature-sensitive products were maintained within required cold chain temperature ranges during delivery. Critical for food safety and quality compliance.']
  total_cases varchar
  total_cases_delivered decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total quantity of product cases delivered to the store during this visit. Aggregate measure across all SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) delivered.']
  total_cases_returned decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total quantity of product cases returned from the store during this visit due to damage, expiration, or other quality issues.']
  total_units_delivered decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total quantity of individual product units delivered to the store during this visit. Aggregate measure across all SKUs delivered.']
  total_units_returned decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total quantity of individual product units returned from the store during this visit due to damage, expiration, or other quality issues.']
  vehicle_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery vehicle used for this DSD delivery. Links to fleet asset master data.']
  visit_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the DSD delivery visit to the retail store occurred. Business event date for reporting and analytics.']

  Note: 'DSD delivery record from distribution/logistics perspective - proof of delivery, temperature compliance, physical goods movement. SSOT for delivery logistics. Distinct from sales.sales_dsd_delivery which tracks sales/financial aspects.'
}

Table distribution.dsd_delivery_line {
  dsd_delivery_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the DSD delivery line item. Primary key for this product.']
  distribution_dsd_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent DSD delivery header record. Links this line item to the overall store visit and delivery transaction.']
  distribution_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or distribution center location from which this product was sourced for delivery.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the DSD driver who delivered this line item. Used for driver performance tracking and accountability.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade promotion or promotional campaign associated with this delivery line, if applicable.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Tracks DSD pilot deliveries of new formulations, required for consumer test logistics and performance reporting.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery route on which this line was delivered. Used for route optimization and logistics analysis.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product master record for the SKU delivered or returned on this line.']
  cold_chain_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cold chain temperature requirements were maintained throughout delivery. True if compliant, False if temperature excursion occurred.']
  cost_of_goods_sold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost of goods sold for the delivered quantity. Used for margin analysis and profitability reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this delivery line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts on this line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  delivered_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of the SKU delivered to the store during this visit. Used for secondary sales capture and inventory replenishment tracking.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this specific line item was delivered or processed at the store. Used for OTIF performance tracking and delivery window compliance.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total discount or promotional allowance applied to this line. Used for trade promotion settlement and margin analysis.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Product expiration or best-before date. Used for FEFO inventory rotation and shelf-life management in stores.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number (GTIN-8, GTIN-12, GTIN-13, or GTIN-14) for the product. Enables cross-system product identification and retail scanning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this delivery line record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  line_amount decimal(18,2)
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the delivery document. Used for ordering and referencing specific line items within the delivery.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this delivery line. Values: DELIVERED (fully delivered), PARTIAL (partially delivered), REJECTED (rejected by store), RETURNED (returned to warehouse), CANCELLED (cancelled before delivery).']
  line_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value for this delivery line (delivered quantity × unit selling price). Represents secondary sales revenue at store level.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number for the delivered product. Critical for traceability, quality control, and recall management.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date the product was manufactured. Used for shelf-life calculation and quality tracking.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary value for this line after discounts and taxes (line_total_amount - discount_amount + tax_amount). Represents final revenue for this line.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this delivery line. May include special handling instructions, store feedback, or exception details.']
  oos_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store was out of stock for this SKU at the time of delivery. True if OOS condition detected, False otherwise. Critical for OSA metrics.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU originally ordered by the store or planned for delivery. Used for fill-rate and OTIF analysis.']
  planogram_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product was placed according to the store planogram. True if compliant, False if deviation occurred.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the delivery line was rejected by the store. Values: DAMAGED, WRONG_PRODUCT, QUALITY, UNAUTHORIZED, EXPIRED, OTHER.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why product was returned from the store. Values: DAMAGED (damaged goods), EXPIRED (past expiry date), OVERSTOCKED (excess inventory), QUALITY (quality issue), RECALL (product recall), UNSOLD (slow-moving), OTHER (other reason). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DAMAGED|EXPIRED|OVERSTOCKED|QUALITY|RECALL|UNSOLD|OTHER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text description of the return reason. Provides additional context beyond the standardized return reason code.']
  returned_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU returned from the store during this visit. Includes damaged goods, expired products, and unsold inventory.']
  shelf_placement_confirmed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DSD driver confirmed proper shelf placement and merchandising of the delivered product. True if confirmed, False otherwise.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to this delivery line. Calculated based on jurisdiction-specific tax rules.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control zone required for this product during storage and delivery. Values: AMBIENT (room temperature), CHILLED (refrigerated), FROZEN (frozen storage).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for quantities on this line. Common values: EA (each), CS (case), PK (pack), BX (box), PL (pallet), DZ (dozen), KG (kilogram), LB (pound), LT (liter), GL (gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PK|BX|PL|DZ|KG|LB|LT|GL — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,2)
  unit_selling_price decimal(15,4) [note: 'Selling price per unit for this SKU at the time of delivery. Used for revenue calculation and pricing analysis.']
  uom varchar
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code for the product. Standard barcode identifier used in North American retail.']
  vehicle_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery vehicle used to transport this line item. Used for fleet management and cold chain tracking.']

  Note: 'Line-level SKU detail for each product delivered or returned during a DSD store visit. Records SKU code, GTIN, lot number, expiry date, ordered quantity, delivered quantity, returned quantity, return reason code, unit selling price, line total value, and shelf placement confirmation. Enables secondary sales capture, OOS identification, and FEFO compliance tracking at the store-SKU level.'
}

Table distribution.otif_event {
  otif_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the OTIF performance measurement event. Primary key for the OTIF event record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transportation carrier responsible for delivery. Used for carrier performance scorecarding and accountability analysis.']
  delivery_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery execution record for Direct Store Delivery (DSD) or carrier delivery. Links to the actual delivery event.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center that originated the shipment. Used for DC-level OTIF performance accountability and scorecard reporting.']
  distribution_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outbound shipment being measured for OTIF performance. Links to the shipment transaction that this OTIF event evaluates.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Enables OTIF Incident Management report to identify the employee responsible for resolving each OTIF event, meeting internal SLA tracking.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific retail store location receiving the delivery. Applicable for Direct Store Delivery (DSD) and store-level OTIF tracking.']
  sales_order_id varchar
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer trade account receiving the shipment. Used for customer-specific OTIF scorecard and retailer compliance reporting.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual date the shipment was delivered to the customer location. Used to calculate on-time performance variance.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when delivery was completed and proof of delivery was captured. Used for time-window OTIF compliance.']
  committed_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date committed to the customer per the Service Level Agreement (SLA) or purchase order terms. Baseline for on-time performance measurement.']
  committed_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise delivery date and time committed to the customer, including time window for time-sensitive deliveries. Used for precise OTIF measurement when time windows apply.']
  committed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The total quantity committed to the customer per the sales order or purchase order. Baseline for in-full performance measurement. Measured in order unit of measure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this OTIF event record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and record creation tracking.']
  delivered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The actual quantity delivered to the customer. Used to calculate in-full performance and quantity variance. Measured in order unit of measure.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'The logistics channel used for delivery execution. Values include Direct Store Delivery (DSD), Less Than Truckload (LTL) carrier, Full Truckload (FTL) carrier, parcel, customer pickup, or third-party logistics (3PL).']
  delivery_variance_days varchar [note: 'The number of days variance between committed and actual delivery date. Negative values indicate early delivery, positive values indicate late delivery, zero indicates on-time.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTIF measurement is under dispute by the customer or internal stakeholders. True indicates active dispute, False indicates accepted measurement.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the OTIF measurement is disputed. Captures customer or internal objections to the performance assessment.']
  event_date date
  event_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the OTIF event record. Tracks progression from initial measurement through dispute resolution and final closure.']
  failure_category varchar [note: 'The primary root cause category for OTIF failures. Used for operational improvement analysis and accountability assignment. Values include DC pick error, inventory shortage, dock delay, carrier issue, customer refused, weather delay, system error, quality hold, damaged product, address issue, or no failure for successful deliveries. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dc_pick_error|inventory_shortage|dock_delay|carrier_issue|customer_refused|weather_delay|system_error|quality_hold|damaged_product|address_issue|no_failure — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  failure_reason_code varchar
  failure_reason_detail varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the root cause for OTIF failure. Free-text field capturing specific circumstances, corrective actions, and accountability notes.']
  in_full_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the full committed quantity was delivered. True indicates complete delivery, False indicates short shipment or over shipment outside tolerance.']
  in_full_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The acceptable variance percentage per customer SLA or business policy. Deliveries within this tolerance are considered in-full compliant.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this OTIF event record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  late_days varchar
  measurement_date date [note: 'The date when the OTIF performance was measured and recorded. Used for reporting period assignment and trend analysis.']
  on_time_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the shipment was delivered on or before the committed delivery date. True indicates on-time delivery, False indicates late delivery.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'The customer purchase order or sales order number associated with this shipment. Business identifier for traceability to the originating order.']
  otif_flag boolean
  otif_score boolean [note: 'Composite OTIF performance indicator. True only when both on_time_flag and in_full_flag are True. Primary KPI for DC operations accountability and retailer scorecard compliance.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the retailer penalty amount. Standard currency codes such as USD, EUR, GBP.']
  pod_signature_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an electronic or physical signature was captured at delivery. True indicates signature obtained, False indicates delivery without signature capture.']
  proof_of_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when proof of delivery was captured, typically via electronic signature, barcode scan, or delivery confirmation. Source of truth for actual delivery time.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for committed and delivered quantities. Common values include cases, pallets, eaches, or other standard units.']
  quantity_variance decimal(18,3) [note: 'The difference between delivered quantity and committed quantity. Negative values indicate short shipment, positive values indicate over shipment.']
  quantity_variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage variance between delivered and committed quantity, calculated as (delivered - committed) / committed * 100. Used to assess severity of in-full failures.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'The organizational function or external party accountable for the OTIF failure. Used for performance accountability and continuous improvement initiatives.']
  retailer_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The financial penalty or chargeback amount assessed by the retailer for OTIF non-compliance per the customer contract or retailer compliance program. Zero for compliant deliveries.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'The warehouse management system shipment number or bill of lading number. External business identifier for the shipment.']
  short_quantity decimal(18,3)
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'The service level tier committed to the customer. Defines the delivery speed and OTIF performance expectations per the customer agreement.']

  Note: 'OTIF (On Time In Full) performance measurement record for each outbound shipment or DSD delivery against committed service levels. Captures shipment/delivery reference, customer/account, committed vs actual delivery date, on-time flag, committed vs delivered quantity, in-full flag, composite OTIF score, root cause category for failures (DC pick error, inventory shortage, dock delay, carrier issue), and retailer penalty exposure amount. Primary KPI entity for DC operations accountability and retailer scorecard compliance.'
}

Table distribution.dock_appointment {
  dock_appointment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dock appointment record. Primary key for the dock appointment entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transportation carrier responsible for this appointment. Links to carrier master data for performance tracking and compliance monitoring.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Allows Dock Scheduling System to capture which employee scheduled the appointment, enabling workload planning and audit of appointment creation.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center where this dock appointment is scheduled. Links to the facility managing the dock operations.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Receiving appointments are scheduled per purchase order, enabling the dock team to allocate doors and resources for each inbound PO.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the carrier truck checked in at the distribution center gate. Used to calculate on-time arrival performance and carrier compliance.']
  actual_case_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of cases handled during the appointment. Used for variance analysis and operational performance tracking.']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when loading or unloading operations were completed and the truck departed. Used to calculate dock turnaround time and throughput metrics.']
  actual_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of pallets loaded or unloaded during the appointment. Compared against expected count to identify discrepancies.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when loading or unloading operations began at the dock door. Marks the beginning of active dock utilization.']
  actual_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual total weight of goods handled in kilograms. Verified through weigh station or scale integration.']
  appointment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique appointment reference number used for scheduling and tracking. Externally visible identifier shared with carriers and suppliers.']
  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dock appointment: scheduled (initial booking), confirmed (carrier acknowledged), checked_in (truck arrived at gate), in_progress (loading/unloading active), completed (finished), cancelled (appointment voided), no_show (carrier missed appointment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|confirmed|checked_in|in_progress|completed|cancelled|no_show — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  appointment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dock appointment purpose: inbound receipt (supplier delivery), outbound load (customer shipment), inter-DC transfer (internal movement), cross-dock (direct transfer), returns processing, or emergency delivery.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Advanced Shipping Notice identifier sent by supplier prior to shipment arrival. Used to pre-plan receiving operations and validate expected contents.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the appointment was cancelled. Captures business context for cancelled or no-show appointments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dock appointment record was first created in the system. Audit trail for appointment booking lifecycle.']
  cross_dock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a cross-dock appointment where goods are transferred directly from inbound to outbound without storage. True for cross-dock operations.']
  detention_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time in minutes that the carrier was detained beyond the scheduled appointment window. Used to calculate detention charges and identify operational bottlenecks.']
  dock_appointment_status varchar
  dock_door varchar
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Physical dock door identifier assigned to this appointment (e.g., D-101, DOOR-A5). Indicates the specific loading/unloading bay at the distribution center.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the driver assigned to this appointment. Used for gate check-in verification and security purposes.']
  driver_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the driver. Used for real-time communication regarding delays, gate instructions, or operational issues.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this appointment is part of a Direct Store Delivery operation where goods bypass the DC and go directly to retail stores. True for DSD shipments.']
  expected_case_count varchar [note: 'Anticipated number of cases to be handled during this appointment. Supports detailed capacity and labor planning for case-level operations.']
  expected_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Anticipated number of pallets to be loaded or unloaded during this appointment. Used for dock capacity planning and labor scheduling.']
  expected_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total expected weight of goods in kilograms for this appointment. Used for equipment planning and safety compliance.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'UN hazard classification code for hazardous materials (e.g., 3 for flammable liquids, 8 for corrosives). Required for HAZMAT compliance and safety protocols.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this appointment involves hazardous materials requiring special handling, documentation, and certified dock doors. True for HAZMAT shipments.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dock appointment record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to appointment details or status.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and special instructions for this appointment. May include gate directions, special handling requirements, or operational alerts.']
  otif_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the appointment met On Time In Full performance criteria: arrived within scheduled window and delivered expected quantities. True if OTIF compliant.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority classification for this appointment: standard (routine), high (expedited), urgent (same-day), or emergency (critical). Influences dock door assignment and resource allocation.']
  sales_order_number varchar [note: 'Sales order number associated with outbound load appointments. Links the dock appointment to customer orders being shipped.']
  scheduled_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned date for the carrier to arrive at the dock door. Used for day-level appointment planning and capacity management.']
  scheduled_date date
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the appointment window closes. Carrier should complete loading/unloading by this time to maintain schedule compliance.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the appointment window begins. Carrier is expected to arrive at or after this time.']
  scheduled_timestamp timestamp
  seal_intact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the security seal was intact upon arrival. True if seal is unbroken, false if compromised. Critical for security and quality control.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal identifier applied to the trailer. Verified at check-in to ensure cargo integrity and prevent tampering.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Outbound shipment identifier for load appointments. References the shipment being loaded onto the carrier truck.']
  source_system_code varchar
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this appointment involves temperature-sensitive goods requiring cold chain management. True for refrigerated or frozen shipments.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required temperature zone for goods in this appointment: ambient (room temperature), refrigerated (2-8°C), frozen (-18°C), or deep frozen (below -25°C).']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the trailer or container arriving for this appointment. Used for yard management and asset tracking.']

  Note: 'Scheduled dock door appointment record for inbound or outbound truck arrivals at a DC. Captures appointment date/time window, dock door assignment, appointment type (inbound receipt, outbound load, inter-DC transfer), carrier, trailer number, expected SKUs and quantities, appointment status (scheduled, confirmed, arrived, completed, no-show), and actual arrival/departure timestamps. Supports dock scheduling optimization and carrier compliance tracking.'
}

Table distribution.load_plan {
  load_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the outbound load plan record. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the carrier responsible for transporting this load.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center from which this load is being dispatched.']
  distribution_shipment_id varchar
  primary_loading_supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse supervisor who verified and approved the completed load before dispatch.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the picking wave that generated the orders included in this load plan. Links load planning to upstream warehouse execution.']
  actual_departure_datetime timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the loaded trailer departed the distribution center, used for OTIF performance measurement.']
  carrier_service_level varchar [note: 'Service level agreement tier for this shipment, determining transit time and handling requirements.']
  case_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cases or cartons included in this load plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this load plan record was first created in the WMS.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Dock door location at the distribution center where this load is staged and loaded.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this load is part of a Direct Store Delivery route, bypassing retailer distribution centers.']
  estimated_freight_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated transportation cost for this load based on carrier rates, distance, and service level.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying any exception or deviation from standard load planning process (e.g., overweight, incomplete orders, equipment failure).']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing any exceptions, special handling instructions, or deviations from the standard load plan.']
  freight_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated freight cost.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'DOT hazard class for hazardous materials in this load (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquids, Class 8 Corrosives).']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this load contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and DOT placarding.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this load plan record was last updated, tracking changes through the planning and execution lifecycle.']
  load_number varchar
  load_plan_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the load plan, typically generated by the WMS. Used for operational tracking and communication with carriers and warehouse staff.']
  load_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the load plan, tracking progression from planning through dispatch. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|confirmed|in_progress|loaded|sealed|dispatched|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  load_sequence_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy used to determine the order in which pallets are loaded into the trailer, typically aligned with delivery stop sequence.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the load based on shipment mode and delivery strategy. DSD indicates Direct Store Delivery.']
  loading_completion_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when physical loading of the trailer was completed and verified.']
  loading_start_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when physical loading of the trailer began at the dock door.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp
  order_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct outbound orders consolidated into this load plan.']
  otif_target_delivery_datetime timestamp [note: 'Target delivery date and time committed to the customer, used for OTIF performance measurement.']
  pallet_configuration varchar [note: 'Description of how pallets are arranged and stacked within the trailer, supporting optimal space utilization and load stability.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets included in this load plan.']
  plan_date date
  plan_number varchar
  planned_departure_datetime timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the loaded trailer is planned to depart the distribution center.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number applied to the trailer after loading to ensure tamper-evidence during transit.']
  source_system_code varchar
  stop_count varchar [note: 'Total number of delivery stops planned for this load, used for multi-stop route optimization.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this load requires temperature-controlled transportation (refrigerated or frozen).']
  temperature_range_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled loads during transit.']
  temperature_range_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled loads during transit.']
  total_volume_cbm decimal(18,4)
  total_volume_cubic_m decimal(12,3)
  total_volume_cubic_meters decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of the load in cubic meters, used for trailer utilization analysis and capacity planning.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the load in kilograms, including product, packaging, and pallets. Used for carrier billing and DOT compliance.']
  trailer_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the trailer or vehicle assigned to this load. May be a license plate, fleet number, or container number.']
  trailer_type varchar [note: 'Type of trailer or vehicle used for this load, determining capacity and product compatibility.']
  trailer_utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of trailer capacity utilized by this load, calculated based on weight, volume, or pallet positions.']
  utilization_percentage decimal(5,2)
  vehicle_type varchar

  Note: 'Outbound load plan record defining the assignment of outbound orders and shipments to a specific trailer/vehicle for dispatch from a DC. Captures load plan number, DC, carrier, trailer ID, planned departure datetime, total weight, total cube, pallet configuration, stop sequence, and load plan status (draft, confirmed, loaded, dispatched). Integrates with Blue Yonder WMS load planning and transportation management.'
}

Table distribution.returns_receipt {
  returns_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the returns receipt record. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the carrier that transported the returned goods to the distribution center.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center receiving the returned goods.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the lot or batch number of the returned goods, critical for traceability and recall management.']
  primary_returns_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the quality assurance employee who performed the condition inspection of the returned goods.']
  product_recall_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_recall. Business justification: Return processing for recalled items requires linking the return receipt to the product recall record for quarantine and reporting.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: Promotion Return Analysis links returned items to the originating promotion event to evaluate promotion effectiveness and refund liability.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Return processing matches returned goods to the original purchase order for credit issuance and inventory reconciliation.']
  recall_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the recall event if this return is recall-driven, linking to the recall management record.']
  receiving_clerk_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse employee who received and logged the returned goods at the distribution center.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail store location from which the goods were returned, if applicable.']
  return_order_id varchar
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being returned.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account or retail account that initiated the return (retailer, DSD customer, or consumer account).']
  case_count varchar [note: 'Number of cases included in the return receipt.']
  condition_assessment varchar [note: 'Quality inspection result indicating the physical and salability condition of the returned goods: resalable, damaged, expired, defective, or contaminated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this return receipt record was first created in the system.']
  credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary credit amount issued to the customer for the returned goods.']
  credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the credit memo issued to the customer account for the returned goods.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  destroy_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of returned units that must be destroyed due to damage, contamination, or recall requirements.']
  disposition_code varchar
  disposition_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the final disposition decision was executed (resale, destroy, rework, etc.).']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision on how the returned goods will be handled: resale to inventory, destroy, rework/repackage, donate, or return to vendor.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the returned product lot, critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory rotation and recall management.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) or UPC (Universal Product Code) of the returned product, used for global product identification.']
  inspection_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality and condition inspection of returned goods was completed.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this return receipt record was last updated.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional details, exceptions, or special handling instructions for the return receipt.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets included in the return receipt.']
  quantity_returned decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of units returned in this receipt.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the return is part of a product recall event (True) or not (False).']
  receipt_date date
  receipt_number varchar
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the returned goods were physically received at the distribution center. Principal business event timestamp.']
  resalable_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of returned units deemed resalable and eligible to be returned to inventory.']
  return_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known return authorization number issued to approve the return. Also known as RA# or RMA number.']
  return_date date
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the return: damaged goods, expired product, overstock, product recall, defective item, or wrong item shipped.']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text explanation of the return reason, providing additional context beyond the reason code.']
  return_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the return receipt: pending arrival, received at DC, inspected, processed for disposition, closed, or cancelled.']
  return_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the return source: retailer return, DSD (Direct Store Delivery) customer return, consumer return, recall-driven return, or internal transfer.']
  returned_quantity decimal(18,4)
  returns_receipt_status varchar
  rework_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of returned units that will be reworked, repackaged, or refurbished for resale.']
  scheduled_receipt_date date [note: 'Planned date for the return to arrive at the distribution center, as communicated in the return authorization.']
  source_system_code varchar
  temperature_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned goods were within acceptable temperature range during transit (True) or not (False).']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned goods require temperature-controlled handling (True) or not (False).']
  temperature_reading_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature reading in Celsius recorded upon receipt of temperature-sensitive returned goods.']
  total_quantity decimal(18,3)
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier tracking number for the return shipment, used to trace the inbound return delivery.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the returned quantity: EA (each), CS (case), PL (pallet), KG (kilogram), LB (pound), L (liter), GAL (gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|KG|LB|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  wms_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Transaction identifier from the Blue Yonder WMS system that processed this return receipt.']

  Note: 'Inbound returns processing record capturing goods returned from retailers, DSD customers, or consumers to a DC. Records return authorization number (RA#), originating customer/account, return reason code (damaged, expired, overstock, recall, consumer return), SKU, lot, quantity returned, condition assessment (resalable, destroy, rework), disposition decision, and credit memo reference. Supports reverse logistics, FEFO expiry management, and product recall execution.'
}

  distribution_offset_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the offset_allocation association']

  allocated_offset_amount decimal(18,4)

  allocation_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the allocation amount']
  allocation_date date [note: 'Date the offset amount was allocated to the facility']

  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2)
  allocation_period varchar
  allocation_period_end date [note: 'End date of the allocation period']
  allocation_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the allocation period']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the allocation']
  allocation_type varchar
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Person or role who approved the allocation']
  carbon_tonnes_offset decimal(18,4) [note: 'Tonnes of carbon offset allocated']
  certification_standard varchar [note: 'Certification standard (e.g., Gold Standard, VCS)']
  cost_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per offset unit']
  created_timestamp timestamp
  credit_quantity decimal(18,4)
  credit_quantity_allocated decimal(18,4)
  currency_code varchar
  distribution_offset_allocation_status varchar
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period of allocation']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year of allocation']

  offset_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'Number of offset units allocated']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the allocation period']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the allocation period']
  reason_code varchar
  reporting_period varchar
  source_system_code varchar
  total_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost of the offset allocation']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Verification status of offset']

  Note: 'Carbon offset allocation to distribution operations (warehousing, last-mile delivery). SSOT for distribution carbon offset tracking. Distinct from marketing.marketing_offset_allocation which allocates offsets to marketing activities.'
}

Table distribution.wave {
  wave_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for wave']
  distribution_facility_id varchar
  previous_wave_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on wave (previous_wave_id)']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse where the wave is executed.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real completion time when the wave finished.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real start time when picking began.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by planners about the wave.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp
  created_timestamp timestamp
  distribution_center_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the distribution center associated with the wave.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Primary equipment used for the wave (e.g., conveyor, robotic).']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the wave is marked as critical for service level commitments.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp
  on_time_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the wave completed within the scheduled window.']
  order_count varchar
  picker_count varchar [note: 'Number of warehouse staff assigned to execute the wave.']
  planned_start_timestamp timestamp
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority of the wave; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wave record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the wave record.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wave was released to the floor for execution.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned completion time for the wave.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start time for wave execution.']
  source_system_code varchar
  strategy varchar [note: 'Strategic approach applied to the wave (e.g., zone picking).']
  total_items varchar [note: 'Sum of all line‑item quantities across orders in the wave.']
  total_orders varchar [note: 'Count of distinct orders included in the wave.']
  total_quantity varchar [note: 'Aggregate quantity of units to be picked in the wave.']
  wave_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the wave used by warehouse staff.']
  wave_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code assigned to the wave for operational tracking.']
  wave_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the wave.']
  wave_type varchar [note: 'Category of the wave indicating the operational purpose (e.g., pick, replenish).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for wave. Referenced by wave_id.'
}

}

Table procurement.goods_receipt {
  goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to PO line']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to PO']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record creation timestamp']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Last modification timestamp']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'QC required flag']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Receipt date']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Receipt number']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Pending, accepted, rejected']
  received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Received quantity']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'UOM']

  Note: 'Receipt of goods from suppliers'
}

  supplier_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']

  invoice_date date [note: 'Invoice date']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice number']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Pending, approved, paid']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Last modification timestamp']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms']

  Note: 'Invoices from suppliers'
}

}

  Note: 'Supplier performance metrics'
}

  start_date date [note: 'Start date']

  Note: 'Vendor managed inventory agreements'
}

Table procurement.procurement_vmi_agreement2 {
  procurement_vmi_agreement2_id varchar [pk]
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Supplier party to the VMI agreement']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Business document number for the agreement']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the agreement']
  agreement_type_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the VMI agreement type.']
  agreement_type_description varchar
  agreement_type_name varchar
  auto_renew_flag boolean [note: 'Whether the agreement auto-renews']
  auto_replenish_flag boolean [note: 'Whether automatic replenishment is enabled']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency of billing under this agreement type']
  billing_trigger varchar [note: 'Event that triggers billing (consumption, shipment, receipt)']
  consignment_flag boolean [note: 'Whether stock is held on consignment']
  created_timestamp timestamp
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency for monetary terms']
  default_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Default lead time in days for this agreement type']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this agreement type becomes effective.']
  effective_from date
  effective_until date
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this agreement type expires.']
  is_active boolean
  is_consignment boolean [note: 'Whether this agreement type involves consignment inventory']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard lead time in days for this agreement type']
  max_order_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum order quantity allowed per replenishment cycle']
  max_stock_level decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum stock level allowed under the agreement']
  max_stock_threshold decimal(18,4)
  min_order_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum order quantity threshold for VMI replenishment']
  min_stock_level decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum stock level the supplier must maintain']
  min_stock_threshold decimal(18,4)
  ownership_model varchar [note: 'Inventory ownership model (consignment, owned, hybrid)']
  ownership_transfer_point varchar [note: 'Point at which inventory ownership transfers']
  penalty_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Whether penalty clauses apply to this agreement type']
  procurement_vmi_agreement2_description varchar
  procurement_vmi_agreement2_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the agreement type (e.g., active, inactive, deprecated).']
  reorder_point decimal(18,4) [note: 'Reorder trigger quantity']
  replenishment_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence of replenishment cycles']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'Method used for inventory replenishment under this agreement type']
  replenishment_model varchar
  requires_forecast_sharing boolean [note: 'Whether this type requires forecast data sharing']
  review_frequency varchar
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between inventory review cycles']
  safety_stock_method varchar [note: 'Method for calculating safety stock levels']
  service_level_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target service level percentage for this agreement type']
  type_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the VMI agreement type']
  type_name varchar [note: 'Name of the VMI agreement type']
  vmi_agreement_type varchar [note: 'Type/category of VMI agreement']
  vmi_agreement_type_code varchar
}

Table promotion.trade_promotion {
  trade_promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the trade promotion program. Primary key for the trade promotion entity.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category covered by this trade promotion. Links to the product category master entity.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand participating in this trade promotion. Links to the brand master entity.']
  owner_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business user responsible for planning, executing, and analyzing this trade promotion. Typically a trade marketing manager or category manager. Links to the user or employee master entity.']
  primary_trade_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business user who approved this trade promotion. Links to the user or employee master entity.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: REQUIRED: Supplier‑funded trade promotions need a link for budgeting and compliance; experts expect a sponsor supplier on each promotion.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail trade account (customer) participating in this promotion. Links to the trade account master entity.']
  accrual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total accrued trade spend liability for this promotion, representing funds reserved or committed but not yet paid to the retailer.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the trade promotion was formally approved by authorized business stakeholders and authorized for execution.']
  authorized_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total authorized trade spend budget allocated for this promotion, including all funding mechanisms (off-invoice, scan-back, display allowances, etc.). Expressed in the company\'s reporting currency.']
  baseline_volume_units decimal(15,2) [note: 'Expected sales volume in units without the promotion, used as the baseline for calculating incremental lift and ROI.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Retail channel classification where this promotion is executed. Grocery (supermarkets), mass merchandiser (Walmart/Target), drug (pharmacy chains), convenience (c-stores), club (warehouse clubs), ecommerce (online retail).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where this promotion is executed.']
  coupon_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotion includes manufacturer or retailer coupons (print, digital, or mobile). True if coupons are part of the promotion, false otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this trade promotion record was first created in the system. Represents the business event time of promotion planning initiation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this promotion record.']
  deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total deductions taken by the retailer against invoices for this promotion. Used for reconciliation and settlement tracking.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered to the retailer or consumer as part of this promotion. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15% off).']
  display_type varchar [note: 'Type of in-store merchandising display associated with this promotion. End-cap (end of aisle), free-standing (floor display), shelf-talker (shelf signage), pallet (bulk display), cooler (refrigerated display), none (no special display).']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the trade promotion concludes and promotional pricing/terms expire at retail.']
  expected_roi_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected return on investment for this promotion, calculated as (incremental profit / trade spend) * 100. Used for pre-event planning and approval.']
  feature_ad_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotion includes feature advertising in retailer circulars, flyers, or digital media. True if featured, false otherwise.']
  funding_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which trade funds are provided to the retailer. Off-invoice (deducted at purchase), scan-back (reimbursed based on POS sales), bill-back (invoiced after event), lump-sum (fixed payment), accrual (reserved funds).']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic coverage of the trade promotion. National (entire country), regional (multi-state or territory), local (city or metro area), store-specific (individual retail locations).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this trade promotion record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail purposes.']
  maximum_purchase_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of units a consumer can purchase at the promotional price. Null if no maximum limit applies.']
  minimum_purchase_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units a consumer must purchase to qualify for the promotional offer. Null if no minimum applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional business context, special terms, execution notes, or post-event observations related to this trade promotion.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Retailer pricing strategy context for this promotion. Hi-Lo (high-low promotional pricing with deep discounts), EDLP (everyday low price with minimal promotions), hybrid (combination approach).']
  promotion_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the trade promotion program for business user identification and reporting purposes.']
  promotion_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the trade promotion program, typically generated by the TPM system. Format: TPM-YYYYNNNN where YYYY is year and NNNN is sequence.']
  promotion_objective varchar [note: 'Primary business objective driving the trade promotion strategy. Volume growth (increase unit sales), market share gain (capture competitor share), new product launch (trial generation), competitive defense (respond to competitor activity), inventory clearance (reduce excess stock), seasonal event (holiday/event-driven).']
  promotion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the trade promotion. Planned (in design), approved (authorized for execution), active (currently running), closed (completed with results finalized), cancelled (terminated before completion), on-hold (temporarily suspended).']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trade promotion mechanism. Temporary price reduction (Hi-Lo), feature ad (retailer circular), display (end-cap/shelf), BOGO (buy-one-get-one), multi-buy (quantity discount), scan-back (post-sale rebate). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: temporary_price_reduction|feature_ad|display|bogo|multi_buy|scan_back|off_invoice|coupon|loyalty_reward|edlp|slotting_allowance|markdown_allowance — promote to reference product]']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Status of financial settlement and reconciliation for trade promotion deductions and accruals. Pending (awaiting settlement), in-progress (reconciliation underway), settled (fully reconciled and paid), disputed (discrepancies under review).']
  source_system_code varchar
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the trade promotion becomes active and promotional pricing/terms take effect at retail.']
  target_volume_units decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target sales volume in units expected to be achieved during the promotion period, including baseline and incremental lift.']
  tpm_system_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from the source Trade Promotion Management system (e.g., Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud TPM module). Used for system integration and traceability.']

  Note: 'Core master entity for trade promotion programs. Captures the full definition of a trade promotion including name, type (Hi-Lo, EDLP, display, coupon, scan-back, off-invoice), promotional period, owning brand/category, status (planned, active, closed, cancelled), total authorized trade spend budget, promotion objectives, promotion type classification (temporary price reduction, feature ad, display, BOGO, multi-buy, scan-back, coupon, loyalty reward), applicable channels, and integration reference to Salesforce TPM. Serves as the SSOT for all trade promotion definitions and the parent entity for all promotion events, spend allocations, and post-event analyses.'
}

Table promotion.promotion_event {
  promotion_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotion event. Primary key for this entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for integrated ROI reporting: each trade promotion event is executed as part of a marketing campaign, enabling campaign‑level performance analysis.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.capa. Business justification: Promotion event scheduling must consider any open CAPA for the product; linking ensures corrective actions are addressed before promotional activities.']
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for promotion spend accounting reports linking each event to the responsible cost center for budgeting and variance analysis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this promotional event for execution.']
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Event‑level sustainability reports require linking each promotion event to the ESG commitment it supports for impact tracking.']
  profit_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables profit‑center level reporting of promotion performance and ROI, a standard finance requirement.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific retail store, distribution center, or ship-to location where the promotional event is executed.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retailer or distributor account participating in this promotional event.']
  trade_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent trade promotion program under which this event is executed. A single program may have multiple events across different retailers or time windows.']
  accrual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The financial accrual amount reserved for this promotional event, representing the estimated liability for trade promotion obligations.']
  actual_trade_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The realized trade spend for this promotional event, representing the total investment actually incurred by the manufacturer.']
  actual_volume_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'The realized sales volume in units achieved during the promotional event, used for post-event analysis and lift measurement.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when this promotional event was approved for execution by the authorized business stakeholder.']
  baseline_volume_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'The expected sales volume in units without promotional activity, used as the baseline for calculating promotional lift.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'The date when this promotional event was cancelled, if applicable.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Textual explanation for why this promotional event was cancelled, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this promotional event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this promotional event record.']
  deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total deductions claimed by the retailer against invoices for this promotional event, representing retailer-initiated trade spend recovery.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the promotional event concludes execution at retail or distribution level.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the promotional event, including objectives, mechanics, and special conditions.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the promotional event for business user identification and reporting.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the promotion event, used for external communication and reporting.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotional event. Planned: event scheduled but not yet approved; Approved: event approved for execution; In Execution: event currently active; Completed: event finished; Cancelled: event terminated before completion.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotional event mechanism. Feature Ad: product featured in retailer circular; Display: in-store display placement; Temporary Price Reduction (TPR): short-term price discount; BOGO: Buy One Get One offer; Scan-Back: post-sale rebate based on scanned sales; Coupon: manufacturer or retailer coupon redemption.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'The primary source of funding for this promotional event. Manufacturer: fully funded by manufacturer; Retailer: fully funded by retailer; Cooperative: jointly funded by both parties.']
  geography_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic market where the event is executed.']
  gmroi_ratio decimal(8,2) [note: 'The gross margin return on investment for this promotional event, calculated as Gross Margin / Average Inventory Investment, measuring profitability efficiency.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this promotional event record was last modified in the system.']
  planned_trade_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The budgeted trade spend allocation for this promotional event, representing the total investment planned by the manufacturer.']
  planned_volume_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'The forecasted sales volume in units expected during the promotional event, used for planning and ROI estimation.']
  post_event_analysis_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether post-event analysis has been completed for this promotional event, including lift measurement and ROI calculation.']
  post_event_analysis_date date [note: 'The date when post-event analysis was completed for this promotional event.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'The pricing approach applied during this event. Hi-Lo: high-low promotional pricing with deep temporary discounts; EDLP: Everyday Low Price with minimal promotional variation; Hybrid: combination of both strategies.']
  promotional_lift_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage increase in sales volume attributable to the promotional event, calculated as (Actual Volume - Baseline Volume) / Baseline Volume * 100.']
  rebate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total rebate amount paid to the retailer for this promotional event, typically calculated post-event based on performance metrics.']
  roi_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The return on investment for this promotional event, calculated as (Incremental Profit - Trade Spend) / Trade Spend * 100, measuring promotional effectiveness.']
  salesforce_tpm_event_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier from Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Trade Promotion Management system for this event record, used for system integration and data lineage.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date when financial settlement for this promotional event was completed and all trade spend obligations were resolved.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'The current status of financial settlement for this promotional event. Pending: settlement not yet initiated; In Progress: settlement calculations underway; Settled: all financial obligations resolved; Disputed: settlement under dispute resolution.']
  source_system_code varchar
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the promotional event begins execution at retail or distribution level.']

  Note: 'Trade promotion event representing a specific promotional execution with a retailer (TPR, display, feature). SSOT for trade promotion events. Distinct from marketing.marketing_event which covers brand marketing events.'
}

Table promotion.event_sku {
  event_sku_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotion event SKU association record. Primary key for this entity.']
  channel_classification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution channel (e.g., grocery, mass, club, DTC, e-commerce) where this promotional SKU is offered.']
  label_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.label_version. Business justification: Promotion pricing must reference the latest approved label version for each SKU to meet labeling regulations.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: REQUIRED: Lot traceability for promotional SKUs ensures compliance and quality tracking of the specific production lot used.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent promotion event to which this SKU is associated.']
  regulatory_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.registration. Business justification: Compliance team validates each promoted SKU against its registration before launch; direct FK enables automated compliance checks.']
  retailer_funding_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retailer funding agreement or trade contract governing the promotional terms for this SKU.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product SKU participating in this promotion event.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: REQUIRED: Promotion Fulfillment Allocation report needs to map each promoted SKU to the specific inventory stock position supplying it.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retailer or trade account for which this promotional SKU pricing applies.']
  accrual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Accrued trade spend liability for this promotional SKU. Represents the estimated financial obligation based on planned volume and discount.']
  actual_promotional_volume_cases decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual volume of cases sold during the promotion period for this SKU. Captured post-event from POS or shipment data.']
  actual_promotional_volume_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual volume of consumer units sold during the promotion period for this SKU. Used for promotional lift and ROI analysis.']
  baseline_volume_estimate_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated volume of units that would have been sold without the promotion. Used to calculate incremental lift. Typically derived from historical sales trends.']
  baseline_volume_units decimal(15,2)
  compliance_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of promotional pricing compliance verification for this SKU. Ensures promotional pricing was executed as agreed with the retailer.']
  compliance_verified_date date [note: 'Date when promotional pricing compliance was verified for this SKU through retail execution audits or POS data validation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this promotion event SKU record was first created in the system.']
  deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual deduction amount claimed by the retailer for this promotional SKU. Used for trade spend reconciliation and settlement.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2)
  display_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of in-store display location where this promotional SKU is merchandised. Impacts visibility and promotional lift. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Shelf|End Cap|Free Standing Display|Pallet|Cooler|Checkout|Secondary Placement — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  feature_type varchar [note: 'Type of promotional feature or advertising support provided for this SKU during the promotion event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Circular Ad|Digital Ad|In-Store Signage|Shelf Talker|Coupon|Loyalty Offer|None — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number uniquely identifying the product SKU in the promotion. Supports EAN-8, EAN-13, UPC-A, UPC-E, and GTIN-14 formats.']
  incremental_lift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage lift in volume due to the promotion. Calculated as (incremental_lift_volume_units / baseline_volume_estimate_units) * 100.']
  incremental_lift_volume_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'Incremental volume of units sold above baseline due to the promotion. Calculated as actual_promotional_volume_units - baseline_volume_estimate_units.']
  is_featured_sku boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is a featured hero product in the promotion event. Featured SKUs typically receive premium merchandising and advertising support.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who last modified this promotion event SKU record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this promotion event SKU record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this promotional SKU.']
  planned_promotional_volume_cases decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted volume of cases expected to be sold during the promotion period for this SKU. Used for trade spend budgeting and supply planning.']
  planned_promotional_volume_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted volume of consumer units expected to be sold during the promotion period for this SKU.']
  planned_volume_units decimal(15,2)
  price_reduction_depth_percent decimal(18,2) [note: 'Percentage discount from regular shelf price to promoted price. Calculated as ((regular_shelf_price - promoted_price) / regular_shelf_price) * 100.']
  pricing_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the promotional pricing for this SKU. Tracks the workflow state from draft through execution to completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Pending Approval|Approved|Rejected|Active|Completed|Cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pricing_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the promotional pricing for this SKU was approved.']
  promoted_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The temporary promotional price offered to consumers during the promotion event. Expressed in the base currency of the business.']
  promoted_price_type decimal(18,2) [note: 'Type of promotional pricing mechanism applied to this SKU. Hi-Lo: temporary price reduction; EDLP: everyday low price; Multi-Buy: quantity discount; BOGO: buy-one-get-one; Scan-Back: retailer scanned rebate; Off-Invoice: deduction at invoice; Bill-Back: post-event settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Hi-Lo|EDLP|Multi-Buy|BOGO|Scan-Back|Off-Invoice|Bill-Back — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  promotion_effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when the promotional pricing for this SKU expires and regular pricing resumes.']
  promotion_effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when the promotional pricing for this SKU becomes effective at retail.']
  promotional_discount_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Absolute discount amount per unit (regular_shelf_price - promoted_price). Represents the per-unit trade spend investment.']
  promotional_gmroi decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross margin return on investment for this promotional SKU. Calculated as incremental_gross_margin / total_trade_spend_amount.']
  promotional_roi_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Return on investment for this promotional SKU. Calculated as (incremental_gross_profit - total_trade_spend_amount) / total_trade_spend_amount * 100.']
  rebate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Rebate amount paid to the retailer for this promotional SKU. Typically settled post-event based on actual sales performance.']
  regular_shelf_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The standard everyday shelf price (RSP/MSRP) for this SKU before promotional discount. Used to calculate price reduction depth.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when the financial settlement for this promotional SKU was completed with the retailer.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the financial settlement process for this promotional SKU with the retailer.']
  source_system_code varchar
  total_trade_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total trade spend investment for this SKU in this promotion event. Calculated as promotional_discount_per_unit * actual_promotional_volume_units.']
  upc varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code for the SKU participating in the promotion event. Standard 12-digit UPC-A format.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking individual SKUs (GTINs/UPCs) to a specific promotion event with full promotional pricing authority. Captures SKU-level promotional details including promoted price, promoted price type (Hi-Lo temporary price reduction, EDLP, multi-buy, BOGO, scan-back, off-invoice, bill-back), price reduction depth (%), regular shelf price (RSP/MSRP), effective date range, planned promotional volume (cases/units), actual promotional volume, baseline volume estimate, incremental lift volume, promotional discount amount per unit, retailer account, channel, and pricing approval status. Serves as the SSOT for SKU-level promotional pricing within trade events — distinct from standard list pricing owned by the product domain. Enables SKU-level trade spend, lift analysis, and promotional price compliance per event.'
}

Table promotion.trade_calendar {
  trade_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for trade_calendar']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category this promotional calendar covers. Links to the product category entity.']
  channel_classification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales or distribution channel this promotional calendar applies to (e.g., retail, wholesale, e-commerce). Links to the channel classification entity.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand this promotional calendar is associated with. Links to the brand master entity.']
  parent_calendar_trade_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent promotional calendar if this is a sub-calendar or regional variant. Links to another promotional calendar entity.']
  primary_trade_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand manager, category manager, or trade marketing manager who owns and is accountable for this promotional calendar. Links to the employee entity.']
  tertiary_trade_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this promotional calendar record. Links to the employee or system user entity.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer or trade account this promotional calendar is scoped to. Links to the trade account entity.']
  actual_event_count varchar [note: 'The actual number of promotional events executed or in-flight within this calendar period.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when this promotional calendar was formally approved by management or finance.']
  calendar_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code for the promotional calendar used in reporting and system integration (e.g., \'FY24Q1-RET\', \'CAL-2024-BRX\').']
  calendar_name varchar [note: 'Business name of the promotional calendar (e.g., \'FY2024 Q1 Retail Promo Calendar\', \'Annual Brand X Trade Plan\').']
  calendar_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotional calendar (draft, submitted, approved, locked, active, closed).']
  closed_date date [note: 'The date when this promotional calendar was closed after all events completed and post-event analysis was finalized.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where this promotional calendar is executed (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this promotional calendar record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the planned trade spend (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
