  tpm_system_reference_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this lift measurement record in the source TPM system (Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud).']
  validated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the lift measurement was validated and approved for use in ROI calculations.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing incremental volume lift measurements for a promotion event at the SKU-retailer-week level. Stores baseline volume (pre-promotion trend), actual promoted volume, incremental lift units, incremental lift percentage, lift source (POS scan data, Nielsen IQ panel, TradeEdge secondary sales), measurement methodology, confidence interval, and data vintage. Feeds TPO optimization models and post-event ROI calculations.'
}

Table promotion.tpo_scenario {
  tpo_scenario_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the TPO scenario. Primary key.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product category this TPO scenario focuses on, if scenario is category-specific.']
  channel_classification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution channel this TPO scenario applies to, if scenario is channel-specific.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary brand this TPO scenario focuses on, if scenario is brand-specific.']
  parent_scenario_tpo_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent TPO scenario if this scenario is a variant or iteration derived from another scenario.']
  primary_tpo_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade marketing analyst or manager who owns and is responsible for this TPO scenario.']
  tertiary_tpo_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this TPO scenario for execution or further analysis.']
  tpo_baseline_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the baseline or control TPO scenario against which this scenario is compared for incremental analysis.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific retail account this TPO scenario targets, if scenario is account-specific.']
  trade_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional calendar that defines the planning period and promotional events covered by this scenario.']
  trade_promotion_id varchar
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the TPO scenario was formally approved for execution or implementation.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the primary country this scenario applies to.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this TPO scenario record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this scenario.']
  evaluation_date date [note: 'Date when the TPO scenario was evaluated and optimization calculations were completed.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic coverage level of the TPO scenario: national, regional, local, or global.']
  is_baseline_scenario boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this scenario serves as the baseline control scenario for comparison purposes.']
  is_optimal boolean
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this TPO scenario record was most recently updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, assumptions, constraints, or context relevant to this TPO scenario.']
  optimization_objective varchar [note: 'Primary business goal the TPO scenario is designed to achieve: maximize volume, maximize ROI, maximize GMROI, minimize out-of-stock events, maximize profit, or balanced growth.']
  planning_horizon_end_date date [note: 'Last date of the planning period covered by this TPO scenario.']
  planning_horizon_start_date date [note: 'First date of the planning period covered by this TPO scenario.']
  predicted_roi_percentage decimal(8,4)
  predicted_spend_amount decimal(18,2)
  predicted_volume_units decimal(15,2)
  projected_baseline_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted gross revenue expected without promotional activity, serving as the baseline for incremental revenue calculation.']
  projected_baseline_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted sales volume in units expected without any promotional activity, serving as the baseline for lift calculation.']
  projected_gmroi decimal(10,2) [note: 'Expected gross margin return on investment, calculated as gross margin dollars generated per dollar of trade spend invested.']
  projected_gross_profit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted gross profit (revenue minus COGS) expected from this TPO scenario after accounting for trade spend.']
  projected_incremental_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional gross revenue expected to be generated by the promotional activities in this scenario, above the baseline.']
  projected_incremental_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional sales volume in units expected to be generated by the promotional activities defined in this TPO scenario, above the baseline.']
  projected_promotional_lift_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage increase in sales volume compared to baseline, calculated as (incremental volume / baseline volume) * 100.']
  projected_roi_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected return on investment expressed as a percentage, calculated as (incremental profit / total scenario spend) * 100.']
  projected_total_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total forecasted gross revenue including both baseline and incremental revenue for this scenario.']
  projected_total_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total forecasted sales volume in units including both baseline and incremental volume for this scenario.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal region code identifying the sales or geographic region this scenario covers.']
  scenario_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose, assumptions, and strategic intent of the TPO scenario.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name assigned to the TPO scenario for easy identification and reference by trade marketing teams.']
  scenario_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the TPO scenario, typically system-generated with a TPO prefix.']
  scenario_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the TPO scenario indicating its readiness for execution or decision-making.']
  scenario_type varchar
  source_system_code varchar
  total_scenario_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total trade promotion investment allocated across all promotions and accounts in this TPO scenario.']
  tpm_system_reference_code varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this TPO scenario to the source Trade Promotion Management system (e.g., Salesforce TPM, SAP TPM).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number tracking iterations and revisions of this TPO scenario.']
  volume_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for all volume metrics in this scenario (units, cases, pallets, kg, liters).']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a Trade Promotion Optimization (TPO) scenario — a modeled alternative promotion plan generated to optimize trade spend ROI. Captures scenario name, optimization objective (maximize volume, maximize ROI, maximize GMROI, minimize OOS), scenario status (draft, evaluated, approved, rejected), total scenario spend, projected incremental volume, projected ROI, comparison baseline scenario reference, and analyst owner. Enables what-if planning and scenario comparison for trade investment decisions.'
}

Table promotion.retailer_compliance {
  retailer_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the retailer compliance record. Primary key for this transactional compliance audit entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field sales representative or auditor who performed the compliance check. Enables accountability and audit trail for compliance assessments.']
  promotion_event_id varchar
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific retail store location where compliance was audited. Enables store-level compliance tracking and analysis.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retailer or trade account whose compliance is being measured. Identifies the party responsible for executing the promotional mechanics.']
  trade_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade promotion event being audited for compliance. Links this compliance record to the parent promotional campaign.']
  actual_retail_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual price observed at the retail location during the compliance audit. Compared against agreed promotional price to determine price compliance.']
  ad_feature_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the retailer included the promoted product in their feature advertisement or circular as agreed. True = compliant, False = non-compliant.']
  agreed_promotional_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The promotional price point that was contractually agreed between the manufacturer and retailer. Used as the benchmark for price compliance verification.']
  audit_date date
  audit_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify compliance. Field rep visit = in-person store check by manufacturer sales team, third-party audit = independent compliance service, POS data analysis = automated analysis of retailer sales data, photo verification = photographic evidence submitted digitally, retailer self-report = retailer-provided compliance attestation.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'The name of the field representative or third-party auditor who conducted the compliance check. Provides human-readable reference for audit accountability.']
  compliance_check_date date [note: 'The date when the compliance audit or field check was performed. Represents the business event timestamp for this compliance observation.']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2)
  compliance_score_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The overall compliance score expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00). Represents the degree to which the retailer met the agreed promotional execution terms. Used for funding adjustments and future negotiation leverage.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'The overall compliance determination for this audit record. Compliant = all mechanics met, non-compliant = one or more mechanics failed, partial compliant = some mechanics met, pending review = audit submitted but not yet adjudicated, disputed = retailer has challenged the compliance finding.']
  compliance_type varchar [note: 'The category of promotional mechanic being audited. Ad feature = retailer circular/flyer inclusion, display = in-store promotional display presence, price compliance = agreed promotional price point adherence, OSA = On Shelf Availability during promotion, POG placement = planogram compliance, multi-mechanic = combined audit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this compliance record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this compliance record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The system or channel from which this compliance record originated. SFA field audit = Salesforce mobile app, third-party service = external compliance vendor, POS integration = retailer point-of-sale data feed, retailer portal = retailer self-service submission, manual entry = back-office data entry.']
  data_source_reference_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier from the originating system (e.g., SFA visit ID, third-party audit ticket number, POS transaction batch ID). Enables traceability back to source system for dispute resolution.']
  display_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the retailer set up the agreed in-store promotional display (end cap, floor stand, shipper display). True = compliant, False = non-compliant.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the retailer has formally disputed this compliance assessment. True = dispute filed, False = no dispute. Disputed records feed deduction dispute resolution workflows.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the retailer for why they are disputing the compliance finding. Captures retailer perspective and supporting evidence for dispute resolution.']
  funding_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net adjustment to trade spend funding based on compliance performance. Positive values indicate additional funding earned through over-compliance; negative values indicate funding clawback due to non-compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this compliance record was last updated. Tracks changes due to dispute resolution, data corrections, or status updates.']
  non_compliance_category varchar [note: 'Standardized classification of the non-compliance root cause. Enables aggregated analysis of compliance failure patterns across retailers and promotions.']
  non_compliance_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the retailer failed to meet compliance requirements. Captures root cause details such as inventory shortage, operational error, pricing system failure, or deliberate non-execution.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text observations or context from the auditor. May include qualitative details about store conditions, retailer cooperation, or extenuating circumstances affecting compliance.']
  osa_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promoted product was in stock and available on shelf during the promotional period. True = compliant (in stock), False = non-compliant (out of stock).']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The financial penalty or funding adjustment applied due to non-compliance. May be deducted from retailer rebate or future funding allocations. Null if fully compliant.']
  photo_evidence_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to photographic evidence captured during the compliance audit. Supports visual verification of display presence, pricing signage, shelf placement, and ad feature inclusion.']
  pog_placement_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product was placed according to the agreed planogram specifications (shelf position, facings, adjacency). True = compliant, False = non-compliant.']
  price_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the retailer honored the agreed promotional price point (Hi-Lo or EDLP pricing strategy). True = compliant, False = non-compliant.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date when a disputed compliance record was resolved or when final compliance determination was made. Null if still pending or not disputed.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'The final outcome of a disputed compliance record. Upheld = original non-compliance finding stands, overturned = retailer dispute accepted and compliance restored, partial adjustment = compromise reached with reduced penalty, withdrawn = manufacturer withdrew the compliance claim.']
  source_system_code varchar

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing retailer execution compliance for a promotion event — whether the retailer delivered the agreed promotional mechanics (feature ad, display, price point, shelf placement per POG). Tracks compliance check date, compliance type (ad feature, display, price compliance, OSA), compliance score (%), non-compliance reason, penalty amount applied, auditor/field rep reference, and data source (SFA field audit, third-party compliance service). Feeds deduction dispute resolution and future funding negotiations.'
}

Table promotion.baseline_volume {
  baseline_volume_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the baseline volume record. Primary key for this entity.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the product category for which baseline volume is calculated.']
  channel_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the distribution channel for which the baseline volume is calculated (e.g., grocery, drug, mass, club, e-commerce).']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the brand associated with the SKU for which baseline volume is calculated.']
  primary_baseline_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the user who performed the manual override of the baseline estimate.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Baseline volume forecasts are derived from the R&D project\'s market analysis.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the SKU for which baseline volume is calculated.']
  tertiary_baseline_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the user who created this baseline volume record.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the retailer or trade account for which baseline volume is calculated.']
  analyst_override_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the baseline volume estimate was manually overridden by an analyst (True) or is system-generated (False).']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when the baseline volume estimate was approved for use in promotional lift calculations.']
  baseline_methodology varchar [note: 'The statistical methodology used to calculate the baseline volume estimate (e.g., moving average, regression, Nielsen IQ panel, Bayesian structural time series).']
  baseline_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period for which this baseline volume estimate applies.']
  baseline_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period for which this baseline volume estimate applies.']
  baseline_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated baseline revenue for the SKU-retailer-period combination at regular pricing, absent promotional activity.']
  baseline_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the baseline volume record (e.g., draft, approved, active, archived, superseded, rejected).']
  baseline_units decimal(15,2)
  calculation_method varchar
  calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the baseline volume calculation was performed.']
  cases decimal(18,3) [note: 'The estimated baseline sales volume in cases, representing expected sales absent any promotional activity.']
  confidence_interval_lower decimal(18,3) [note: 'The lower bound of the confidence interval for the baseline volume estimate, representing statistical uncertainty.']
  confidence_interval_upper decimal(18,3) [note: 'The upper bound of the confidence interval for the baseline volume estimate, representing statistical uncertainty.']
  confidence_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The confidence level percentage for the confidence interval (e.g., 95.00 for 95% confidence).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this baseline volume record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the baseline revenue amount.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The source system or data provider from which the baseline calculation data was obtained (e.g., POS, Nielsen IQ, IRI, internal sales, TradeEdge).']
  geography_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code or geographic region code for which the baseline volume applies.']
  historical_lookback_weeks varchar [note: 'The number of historical weeks of data used in the baseline volume calculation methodology.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this baseline volume record was last modified.']
  last_refresh_date date [note: 'The date when the baseline volume estimate was last refreshed or recalculated.']
  model_accuracy_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'A statistical measure of the baseline model accuracy, such as R-squared or Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), represented as a decimal value.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the baseline calculation model or algorithm used to generate this estimate.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or context related to the baseline volume calculation.']
  outlier_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether statistical outliers were excluded from the baseline calculation (True) or included (False).']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'The business justification or reason provided by the analyst for manually overriding the system-generated baseline estimate.']
  override_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the analyst override was applied to the baseline estimate.']
  period_end_date date
  period_start_date date
  promotional_periods_excluded_count varchar [note: 'The number of promotional periods that were excluded from the historical data when calculating the baseline volume.']
  seasonality_adjustment_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'The multiplicative factor applied to adjust for seasonal patterns in the baseline volume calculation.']
  source_system_code varchar
  tpm_system_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from the source Trade Promotion Management system (e.g., Salesforce TPM) for traceability.']
  trend_adjustment_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'The multiplicative factor applied to adjust for long-term trend patterns in the baseline volume calculation.']
  units decimal(18,3) [note: 'The estimated baseline sales volume in units, representing expected sales absent any promotional activity.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the statistical baseline volume estimate for a SKU-retailer-period combination, representing expected sales absent any promotional activity. Stores baseline volume (units/cases), baseline revenue, baseline methodology (moving average, regression, Nielsen IQ panel, Bayesian structural time series), data source, baseline period, confidence interval, last refresh date, analyst override flag, and model version. Serves as the reusable baseline denominator consumed by post_event_analysis for incremental lift calculation across all promotion events. Maintained independently of any single promotion event to enable consistent cross-event comparison and baseline trend monitoring.'
}

Table promotion.pos_redemption {
  pos_redemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the point-of-sale redemption transaction record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the cashier or associate who processed the redemption transaction.']
  loyalty_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the retailer or manufacturer loyalty account used during redemption.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this redemption to the specific promotion event that authorized the offer.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific retail store location where the redemption took place.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the shopper who redeemed the offer, if captured through loyalty program or digital account.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the SKU that was purchased with the promotional offer.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the retail trade account where the redemption occurred.']
  clearinghouse_name varchar [note: 'Name of the coupon clearinghouse that processed the manufacturer coupon redemption, if applicable.']
  clearinghouse_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Transaction identifier assigned by the coupon clearinghouse for tracking and settlement.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the redemption occurred.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this redemption record was first created in the data product.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the redemption value amount.']
  data_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the redemption data was received from the source system or data feed.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or data feed from which the redemption record was received.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the regular price through the promotional offer.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number identifying the product redeemed, may be GTIN-8, GTIN-12, GTIN-13, or GTIN-14 format.']
  handling_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Handling fee paid to the retailer or clearinghouse for processing the coupon redemption.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this redemption record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding the redemption transaction, validation, or settlement.']
  offer_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the specific promotional offer redeemed (coupon code, digital offer ID, loyalty reward code).']
  offer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotional offer type that was redeemed.']
  pos_terminal_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the POS terminal or register where the redemption was processed.']
  promoted_unit_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final unit price paid by the consumer after the promotional discount was applied.']
  redeemed_units decimal(15,2)
  redemption_amount decimal(18,2)
  redemption_channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel through which the redemption occurred.']
  redemption_date date [note: 'The date when the promotional offer was redeemed at point of sale.']
  redemption_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Number of units of the SKU purchased with the promotional offer applied.']
  redemption_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the redemption record in the settlement workflow.']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the redemption occurred at the point of sale, including time zone information.']
  redemption_transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the redemption transaction, typically sourced from retailer POS system or coupon clearinghouse.']
  redemption_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the discount or savings provided through the redemption.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market code where the redemption took place.']
  regular_unit_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Regular shelf price per unit before the promotional discount was applied.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the redemption was rejected during validation, if applicable.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount paid by the manufacturer to the retailer or clearinghouse for this redemption.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when the redemption was financially settled between manufacturer and retailer or clearinghouse.']
  source_system_code varchar
  transaction_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total transaction amount for the basket or order containing the redeemed offer.']
  unit_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Discount amount applied per unit of the SKU redeemed.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code scanned at point of sale for the redeemed product.']
  validation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the redemption has been validated against promotion rules and eligibility criteria.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the redemption record was validated or rejected.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing point-of-sale redemption data for consumer-facing promotional offers (coupons, scan-back discounts, loyalty rewards, digital offers). Tracks redemption date, retailer store, SKU, redemption quantity, redemption value, offer type (manufacturer coupon, retailer coupon, digital offer, loyalty reward), redemption channel (in-store, e-commerce, DTC), and data source (EDI 844/849, retailer POS feed, coupon clearinghouse). Enables consumer promotion ROI and redemption rate tracking.'
}

Table promotion.consumer_offer {
  consumer_offer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the consumer-facing promotional offer. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the broader marketing campaign that this consumer offer supports. Links to the marketing campaign entity. Null if not part of a campaign.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category associated with this consumer offer. Links to the product category master data entity.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand associated with this consumer offer. Links to the brand master data entity.']
  primary_consumer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this consumer offer. Links to the user or employee master data entity. Null if not yet approved.']
  promotion_event_id varchar
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Consumer offers for new product launches are created under the umbrella of the related R&D project.']
  target_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to consumer.consumer_segment. Business justification: Required for Offer‑to‑Segment targeting report; marketers need to join offers with consumer segments to measure lift and compliance.']
  tertiary_consumer_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this consumer offer record. Links to the user or employee master data entity.']
  trade_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent trade promotion program that this consumer offer is part of. Links to the trade promotion entity. Null if offer is standalone.']
  actual_cost_incurred decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total cost incurred from all redemptions of this offer to date. Used for budget variance analysis and financial reconciliation.']
  actual_redemption_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of times this offer has been redeemed by consumers to date. Updated in near real-time from POS (Point of Sale) and e-commerce systems.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the consumer offer in the governance workflow. Pending = awaiting review; Approved = authorized for activation; Rejected = not approved.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the consumer offer was approved. Null if not yet approved.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Barcode value associated with the consumer offer for scanning at POS (Point of Sale). May be UPC (Universal Product Code), EAN (European Article Number), or proprietary coupon barcode format.']
  consumer_offer_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the consumer offer including terms, conditions, and value proposition. Used for consumer communication and internal reference.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the offer is valid (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this consumer offer record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the discount value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to qualifying purchase. Null if discount is fixed-value based. Expressed as decimal (e.g., 15.00 for 15% off).']
  discount_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary discount amount provided by the offer in the specified currency. Null if discount is percentage-based.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel through which the consumer offer is distributed. FSI = Free Standing Insert (newspaper); Digital = online/web; In-Store = physical retail location; DTC App = Direct-to-Consumer mobile application; Email = email marketing; Social Media = social platforms; POS = Point of Sale display. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fsi|digital|in_store|dtc_app|email|social_media|pos — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  end_date date
  estimated_cost_per_redemption decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected cost to the business for each redemption of this offer, including discount value and administrative costs. Used for budget planning and ROI (Return on Investment) analysis.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for funding the consumer offer discount. Brand = manufacturer-funded; Trade = trade promotion budget; Co-op = cooperative funding between manufacturer and retailer; Retailer = retailer-funded.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach of the consumer offer. National = available across entire country; Regional = specific regions or states; Local = specific cities or markets; Store-Specific = individual retail locations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this consumer offer record was last modified in the system.']
  maximum_redemption_per_customer varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single customer can redeem this offer during the validity period. Null indicates unlimited redemptions.']
  minimum_purchase_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum purchase value required to qualify for the offer. Null if no minimum purchase requirement exists.']
  minimum_purchase_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of qualifying units or SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) that must be purchased to redeem the offer. Null if no quantity requirement exists.']
  offer_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the consumer offer for redemption tracking and identification. Used by consumers at point of sale or digital checkout.']
  offer_name varchar [note: 'Marketing name or title of the consumer offer as displayed to consumers in promotional materials and digital channels.']
  offer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consumer offer. Draft = under development; Approved = ready for activation; Active = currently redeemable; Paused = temporarily suspended; Expired = validity period ended; Cancelled = terminated before expiration.']
  offer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consumer offer mechanism. Coupon = physical or digital coupon; Digital Offer = app-based or online promotion; Loyalty Reward = points-based redemption; Instant Rebate = immediate price reduction; Free Goods = complimentary product with purchase; BOGO = Buy One Get One; Bundle Discount = multi-product savings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: coupon|digital_offer|loyalty_reward|instant_rebate|free_goods|bogo|bundle_discount — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  offer_value_amount decimal(18,2)
  offer_value_percentage decimal(5,2)
  qualifying_sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SKU identifiers or product codes that qualify for this offer. May reference specific products, product families, or categories. Null if offer applies to all products.']
  redemption_channel varchar [note: 'Channel where the consumer can redeem the offer. In-Store = physical retail; Online = e-commerce website; Mobile App = DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) mobile application; Call Center = phone order; Any = omnichannel redemption allowed.']
  source_system_code varchar
  stackable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this offer can be combined with other offers or promotions. True = can be stacked; False = exclusive offer.']
  start_date date
  terms_and_conditions varchar [note: 'Full legal terms and conditions governing the use and redemption of this consumer offer. Includes restrictions, exclusions, and compliance requirements.']
  total_budget_allocated decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budget amount allocated for this consumer offer across all redemptions. Used for financial planning and spend tracking.']
  total_redemption_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum total number of redemptions allowed across all customers for this offer. Null indicates no aggregate limit.']
  transferable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the offer can be transferred or shared between consumers. True = transferable; False = non-transferable, tied to original recipient.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date when the consumer offer becomes active and redeemable. Consumers cannot redeem the offer before this date.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'End date when the consumer offer expires and is no longer redeemable. Consumers cannot redeem the offer after this date.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining consumer-facing promotional offers (coupons, digital offers, loyalty rewards, instant rebates, free goods with purchase) that are part of a trade promotion or brand marketing program. Captures offer code, offer type, offer description, discount value/percentage, qualifying SKUs, minimum purchase requirement, offer validity period, distribution channel (FSI, digital, in-store, DTC app), maximum redemption limit, and offer status. Distinct from trade funding — this entity owns the consumer-facing offer definition.'
}

Table promotion.flight_allocation {
  flight_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the promotion_flight_allocation association']
  campaign_flight_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the campaign_flight']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the promotion_event']
  trade_account_id varchar
  trade_promotion_id varchar
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual spend against this allocation']
  actual_volume_units decimal [note: 'Realized sales volume for the promotion_event under this campaign_flight']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2)
  allocated_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Allocated budget amount']
  allocated_budget_amount decimal(18,2)
  allocated_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Spend allocated to this promotion_event within the specific campaign_flight']
  allocated_volume_units decimal(15,2)
  allocation_amount decimal(18,2)
  allocation_date date [note: 'Date of allocation']
  allocation_end_date date
  allocation_percentage varchar
  allocation_start_date date
  allocation_status varchar
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Type of allocation (fixed, percentage, volume-based)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the allocation was approved']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of allocation']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Person who approved the allocation']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date the allocation was approved']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget amount allocated to this flight']
  channel_type varchar
  created_timestamp timestamp
  currency_code varchar
  effective_date date [note: 'Effective date']
  end_date date
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date']
  flight_end_date date
  flight_start_date date
  incremental_volume_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated incremental volume from this allocation']
  is_active boolean
  is_approved boolean [note: 'Whether the allocation has been approved']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was last updated']
  lift_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected or actual volume lift percentage']
  notes varchar
  planned_volume_units decimal [note: 'Forecasted sales volume for the promotion_event under this campaign_flight']
  roi_estimate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated return on investment']
  roi_ratio decimal(10,4) [note: 'Return on investment ratio for the allocation']
  source_system_code varchar
  start_date date
  updated_timestamp timestamp
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Variance between planned and actual']
  variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Variance percentage']

  Note: 'This association captures the allocation of a promotion_event to a campaign_flight, including spend and volume metrics that are specific to the event‑flight pairing. Each record links one promotion_event to one campaign_flight and stores attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship.. Existence Justification: A promotion_event represents a specific trade promotion execution at a retailer/store, while a campaign_flight represents a media flight for a marketing campaign. In practice, a single promotion_event can be linked to multiple campaign_flights (e.g., the same in‑store promotion is supported by TV, digital, and print flights), and a single campaign_flight can include many promotion_events across different retailers. The business tracks allocation of spend and volume per promotion_event‑flight pair, which is managed by planners as a distinct record.'
}

Table marketing.marketing_brand {
  marketing_brand_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the brand record. Primary key for the brand master data product.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Brand‑level financial performance is tracked via a dedicated GL account for revenue and expense aggregation.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: BRAND MANAGER role is an employee; required for brand performance reporting and accountability.']
  parent_brand_marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent brand in the brand hierarchy. Null for masterbrand level. Used to establish brand family relationships and roll-up reporting.']
  primary_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Brand Supplier Management: each brand designates a primary supplier for sourcing, required for supplier performance and compliance reporting.']
  annual_marketing_budget decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total allocated marketing investment for the brand for the fiscal year, including media spend, trade promotion, consumer promotion, and agency fees.']
  annual_revenue_target decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned annual revenue goal for the brand in the base currency. Used for budget planning, performance tracking, and brand portfolio prioritization.']
  architecture_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the brand within the corporate brand architecture hierarchy. Masterbrand represents the corporate umbrella, subbrand is a distinct offering under the masterbrand, endorsed carries both corporate and product brand, ingredient brand is a component brand, private label is retailer-owned, and house brand is a proprietary store brand.']
  awareness_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of target consumers who recognize or recall the brand when prompted or unprompted. Measured through consumer surveys and market research panels.']
  brand_category varchar [note: 'Primary product category or market segment in which the brand competes. Examples include personal care, household cleaning, health and wellness, beauty, food and beverage.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the brand for operational reference across systems. Used in SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) hierarchies, trade promotion systems, and financial reporting.']
  brand_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the brand\'s purpose, value proposition, and positioning in the market. Used for internal reference and marketing brief development.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'The official registered name of the brand as it appears on product packaging, marketing materials, and consumer-facing communications.']
  brand_owner varchar
  brand_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the brand. Active indicates current market presence with ongoing marketing investment, inactive is temporarily paused, pending launch is approved but not yet in market, under review is strategic assessment phase, sunset is planned phase-out, and archived is historical record only.']
  brand_type varchar
  consideration_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of target consumers who would consider purchasing the brand when making a category purchase decision. Key indicator of brand relevance and competitive positioning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the brand record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date when the brand was officially discontinued or withdrawn from active marketing. Null for active brands. Used for portfolio rationalization reporting and historical analysis.']
  equity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite metric measuring the overall strength and value of the brand based on consumer awareness, perceived quality, brand associations, and loyalty. Typically scaled 0-100 with higher scores indicating stronger brand equity.']
  geographic_market_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach of the brand\'s distribution and marketing activities. Global indicates worldwide presence, regional covers multiple countries in a region, national is single-country, and local is sub-national markets.']
  guidelines_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the comprehensive brand guidelines document containing logo usage, color specifications, typography, tone of voice, and visual identity standards.']
  is_licensed_brand boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the brand is operated under a licensing agreement from another entity. True indicates licensed brand, false indicates owned brand.']
  is_private_label boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the brand is a private label or store brand owned by a retail customer rather than the manufacturer. True indicates private label, false indicates manufacturer-owned brand.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when the brand was first introduced to the market. Used for brand age calculations, anniversary campaigns, and lifecycle analysis.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the brand in its market lifecycle. Development indicates pre-launch planning, launch is initial market introduction, growth is rapid expansion phase, maturity is stable market presence, decline is decreasing market share, revitalization is strategic renewal effort, and discontinued indicates end of active marketing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: development|launch|growth|maturity|decline|revitalization|discontinued — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  logo_asset_url decimal(18,2) [note: 'URL or file path to the official brand logo asset in the digital asset management system. Used for marketing materials, packaging design, and digital campaigns.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the brand record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  nps_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Net Promoter Score measuring consumer willingness to recommend the brand to others. Calculated as percentage of promoters minus percentage of detractors, ranging from -100 to +100.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Legal entity or business unit that owns the intellectual property rights to the brand. May differ from the manufacturing or distribution entity.']
  positioning_statement varchar [note: 'Concise statement defining the brand\'s unique value proposition, competitive differentiation, and emotional benefit to the target consumer. Foundation for all marketing communications.']
  preference_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of target consumers who prefer this brand over competitive alternatives. Indicates brand loyalty and pricing power potential.']
  primary_color_hex varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal color code for the primary brand color used in packaging, marketing materials, and digital assets. Ensures consistent brand identity across touchpoints.']
  primary_distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Main channel through which the brand reaches consumers. Examples include mass retail, specialty retail, e-commerce, DTC (Direct to Consumer), professional/salon, or foodservice.']
  product_brand_id varchar [note: 'FK to authoritative product.product_brand; resolves SSOT duplicate']
  som_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Brand\'s percentage of total category sales volume or value. Primary metric for competitive market position and growth tracking.']
  sov_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Brand\'s share of total advertising and promotional activity within the category, measured across paid media, earned media, and owned channels. Key indicator of marketing investment relative to competitors.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Specific subcategory within the broader brand category. Provides granular classification for competitive analysis and shelf placement strategy.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Third-party sustainability certifications or eco-labels associated with the brand, such as FSC (Forest Stewardship Council), RSPO (Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil), or B Corp. Multiple certifications separated by semicolons.']
  target_consumer_segment varchar [note: 'Primary demographic and psychographic profile of the intended consumer audience for the brand. Includes age range, income level, lifestyle attributes, and purchase behaviors.']
  target_som_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Strategic target for the brand\'s market share. Used for annual planning, performance evaluation, and resource allocation decisions.']
  target_sov_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Strategic target for the brand\'s share of voice in the category. Used for media planning and budget allocation decisions. Typically set to exceed current SOM (Share of Market) to drive growth.']
  trademark_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current trademark registration expires and requires renewal. Critical for maintaining legal protection of brand assets.']
  trademark_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic territories or countries where the brand trademark is registered and legally protected. May include multiple jurisdictions separated by semicolons.']
  trademark_registration_date date [note: 'Date when the trademark was officially registered with the relevant trademark authority. Used for renewal tracking and IP portfolio management.']
  trademark_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number assigned by the trademark authority for the brand name, logo, or other protected brand elements. Used for legal protection and IP (Intellectual Property) management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp
  valuation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial value of the brand as an intangible asset. Calculated using income, market, or cost approaches for financial reporting and M&A (Mergers and Acquisitions) purposes.']

  Note: 'Brand entity from marketing perspective - brand equity, positioning, campaigns, and consumer perception. SSOT for brand marketing strategy. Distinct from product.product_brand which is the product portfolio/PLM brand assignment.'
}

Table marketing.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing campaign. Primary key.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'FK to marketing.agency']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or user who approved this campaign for execution. Links to workforce/user master data.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product category this campaign targets. Links to the category hierarchy.']
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Campaign expense allocation requires charging campaign spend to a specific cost center for budgeting.']
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Campaigns are often created to support specific ESG commitments; linking enables compliance tracking.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Campaign costs are posted to a specific GL account to support detailed expense reporting.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary brand being promoted in this campaign. Links to the brand master data.']
  primary_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Campaign planning and ROI attribution need a primary SKU reference to allocate budget and measure lift per product.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Campaign planning aligns with R&D product development projects to synchronize launch timing and messaging.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Campaigns are owned by a sales rep responsible for execution and alignment with sales activities.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Campaign Sponsorship Procurement: marketing campaigns often have a sponsoring supplier; linking enables sponsor spend tracking and ROI calculation.']
  target_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the predefined consumer segment this campaign is designed to reach. Links to consumer segmentation master data.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.trade_account. Business justification: Campaigns are often targeted to specific key trade accounts; the marketing team needs to link each campaign to the account for promotion planning and performance reporting.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The actual date the campaign concluded. May differ from planned end date due to extensions, early termination, or performance-based adjustments.']
  actual_media_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual total media spend incurred for this campaign, in the company\'s reporting currency. Includes all paid media costs across channels.']
  actual_production_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual cost incurred for creative production, including all content creation, design, video production, and asset development expenses.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The actual date the campaign went live and began execution. May differ from planned start date due to operational adjustments.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this campaign was formally approved for execution by authorized stakeholders, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2)
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify and track the campaign across systems, media buys, and analytics platforms.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the campaign\'s purpose, strategy, key messages, and expected outcomes.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'The official name or title of the marketing campaign as used in internal planning and external communications.']
  campaign_objective varchar
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the campaign: draft (initial planning), planned (scheduled), approved (ready for execution), active (currently running), paused (temporarily suspended), completed (finished), or cancelled (terminated before completion). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|planned|approved|active|paused|completed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campaign by execution approach: ATL (Above The Line - mass media), BTL (Below The Line - targeted), digital (online channels), shopper (in-store/retail), influencer (social media partnerships), or integrated (multi-channel).']
  channel_mix varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of marketing channels used in the campaign (e.g., TV, digital display, social media, print, radio, OOH, in-store).']
  country_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where the campaign is executed (e.g., USA,CAN,MEX).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this campaign record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  creative_concept varchar [note: 'High-level description of the creative theme, messaging strategy, and visual identity used in the campaign.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this campaign record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  end_date date
  geography_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach of the campaign: global (worldwide), regional (multi-country region), national (single country), or local (city/metro area).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the campaign is currently active (True) or inactive (False). Active campaigns are either running or scheduled to run.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this campaign record was last updated or modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  objective varchar [note: 'Primary business goal of the campaign: awareness (brand visibility), consideration (product evaluation), conversion (purchase), loyalty (retention), launch (new product introduction), or relaunch (product refresh).']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'The originally scheduled date for campaign conclusion as defined during planning phase.']
  planned_media_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The budgeted total media spend allocated to this campaign during the planning phase, in the company\'s reporting currency.']
  planned_production_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The budgeted cost for creative production, including content creation, design, video production, and asset development.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'The originally scheduled date for campaign launch as defined during planning phase.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this campaign in the source operational system, used for traceability and reconciliation.']
  start_date date
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Textual description of the intended consumer segment for this campaign, including demographic, psychographic, and behavioral characteristics.']
  total_budget decimal(18,2)

  Note: 'Master record for each marketing campaign executed by Consumer Goods. Captures campaign name, type (ATL, BTL, digital, shopper, influencer, integrated), objective, target audience, brand association, channel mix, planned vs. actual media spend, campaign start/end dates, creative concept, campaign status, and owning brand/category. Sourced from Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud and internal campaign management systems.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_flight {
  campaign_flight_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign flight record. Primary key.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'FK to marketing.agency']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent marketing campaign. A single campaign may have multiple flights across different channels or geographies.']
  creative_asset_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reference to the creative asset used in this flight. Links to creative asset management system for creative performance analysis.']
  flight_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Flight manager (employee) oversees execution; essential for flight performance review and responsibility logs.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand being promoted in this flight. Enables brand-level campaign performance aggregation.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.retail_store. Business justification: Flight schedules are executed at individual retail stores; linking flight to store enables store‑level media execution tracking and OTIF reporting.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when the flight media execution ended. Captures real execution timeline for post-campaign analysis.']
  actual_impressions varchar [note: 'Actual number of impressions delivered during the flight. Core metric for reach and frequency analysis.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the flight media execution began. Used for variance analysis against planned schedule.']
  allocated_budget decimal(18,2)
  attribution_model varchar [note: 'Attribution methodology used to assign conversion credit to this flight. Critical for multi-touch attribution analysis.']
  budget_allocated decimal(18,2)
  budget_allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated to this flight. Represents the planned media spend for this specific flight execution.']
  budget_spent decimal(18,2)
  budget_spent_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual amount spent on this flight. Used for budget variance analysis and Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) calculation.']
  campaign_flight_status varchar
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary media channel for this flight execution. Supports omnichannel campaign measurement across digital, TV, out-of-home, print, social media, and search.']
  clicks varchar [note: 'Total number of clicks generated by the flight. Primary engagement metric for digital and search campaigns.']
  conversion_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Conversion rate expressed as a decimal (conversions divided by clicks or impressions). Measures campaign effectiveness in driving desired actions.']
  conversions varchar [note: 'Total number of conversions attributed to this flight. Conversion definition varies by campaign objective (purchase, sign-up, download, etc.).']
  cpa decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average cost per conversion for this flight. Critical metric for performance marketing and Return on Investment (ROI) analysis.']
  cpc decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average cost per click for this flight. Key efficiency metric for paid search and digital campaigns.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this flight record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  ctr decimal(5,4) [note: 'Click-through rate expressed as a decimal (clicks divided by impressions). Key performance indicator for digital campaign effectiveness.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this flight record.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or platform from which performance data was captured (e.g., Meta API, Google Ads API, agency post-buy report, Nielsen IQ). Used for data lineage and quality assessment.']
  end_date date
  flight_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the campaign flight for business user identification and reporting.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the flight within the campaign. Used for external reporting and agency coordination.']
  flight_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign flight.']
  frequency decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average number of times an individual was exposed to the campaign (impressions divided by reach). Measures message repetition.']
  grp decimal(10,2) [note: 'Gross Rating Points for traditional media (TV, radio, OOH). Measures total audience delivery as a percentage of target population multiplied by frequency.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this flight record was last updated. Supports change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  market_geography varchar [note: 'Geographic market or region where the flight is executed. Supports market-level performance tracking and regional campaign optimization.']
  measurement_date date [note: 'Date on which performance metrics were measured or reported. Supports daily granular performance tracking at the campaign-channel-market-date grain.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the flight execution, performance anomalies, or special circumstances. Supports qualitative context for quantitative performance data.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'Specific placement or ad format type within the channel (e.g., banner, video, native, sponsored post, display, search text ad). Enables placement-level optimization.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Specific advertising platform or network (e.g., Meta, Google Ads, TikTok, programmatic DSP, linear TV network). Enables platform-specific performance analysis.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Product category or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) group being promoted. Supports category-level marketing effectiveness analysis.']
  reach varchar [note: 'Estimated number of unique individuals exposed to the campaign flight. Used for audience coverage analysis.']
  roas decimal(10,4) [note: 'Return on Ad Spend expressed as a ratio (revenue generated divided by ad spend). Primary metric for campaign profitability assessment.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date for the flight media run. Defines the flight duration for budget allocation and performance measurement.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for the flight media run. Used for campaign planning and scheduling.']
  start_date date
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the target audience segment for this flight (e.g., demographic, psychographic, behavioral criteria). Supports audience-specific performance analysis.']
  target_impressions varchar [note: 'Planned number of impressions for this flight. Used for media planning and performance target setting.']
  video_completion_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of video views that were completed (watched to end). Measures creative engagement quality.']
  video_views varchar [note: 'Total number of video views for video creative flights. Applicable to digital video, social, and TV campaigns.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of campaign execution at the flight level, capturing performance metrics across ALL channels (digital, TV, OOH, print, social, search, programmatic) at the campaign-channel-market-date grain. Defines scheduled media run dates, channel, market/geography, budget allocation, and flight-level performance targets. Captures planned and actual impressions, clicks, CTR, video views, view-through rate, conversions, CPC, CPA, ROAS, reach, frequency, GRPs (traditional), and platform-specific metrics (Meta, Google, TikTok, programmatic DSP). A single campaign may have multiple flights across different channels or geographies. Sourced from digital ad platform APIs, agency post-buy reports, and media monitoring systems. Serves as the unified granular performance record for omnichannel campaign measurement.'
}

Table marketing.media_plan {
  media_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the media plan record. Primary key.']
  agency_id varchar
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign associated with this media plan.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand for which this media plan is created.']
  planner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Media planner employee owns the plan; needed for budget approval audit and planner workload tracking.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Media plans are built for product launches that stem from a particular R&D project, ensuring budget aligns with development timeline.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved this media plan.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when this media plan was formally approved for execution.']
  budget_allocation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total campaign or brand budget allocated to this media plan line.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this media plan record was first created in the system.']
  creative_format varchar [note: 'Format of the creative asset planned for this media (e.g., 30-second spot, banner ad, full-page print, billboard).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the planned spend amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Time segment of day for broadcast media (e.g., Prime Time, Early Morning, Late Night, Daytime).']
  end_date date
  flight_end_date date [note: 'End date of the media flight or execution period for this plan.']
  flight_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the media flight or execution period for this plan.']
  kpi_target varchar [note: 'Target Key Performance Indicator metric and value for measuring media plan success (e.g., Reach 70%, CTR 2.5%, ROAS 3.0).']
  market varchar [note: 'Geographic market or Designated Market Area (DMA) targeted by this media plan (e.g., USA, New York DMA, UK).']
  media_channel varchar [note: 'Primary media channel category for this plan line (TV, digital, out-of-home, print, social, search, programmatic, radio, cinema, podcast). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tv|digital|ooh|print|social|search|programmatic|radio|cinema|podcast — promote to reference product]']
  media_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the media owner or publisher (e.g., NBC, Google, Meta, Clear Channel).']
  media_plan_status varchar
  media_subchannel varchar [note: 'Detailed subchannel or platform within the primary media channel (e.g., Facebook, Google Display, National TV, Local Radio).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this media plan record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes, comments, or special instructions related to this media plan.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Primary marketing objective for this media plan (awareness, consideration, conversion, retention, trial).']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code for the media plan used for external reference and tracking.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or title of the media plan document.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the media plan indicating its approval and execution state.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the media plan by planning horizon and purpose.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this media plan document to track revisions and iterations.']
  planned_cpm decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned cost per thousand impressions for this media investment.']
  planned_cpp decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned cost per rating point for broadcast media investments.']
  planned_frequency decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned average number of times the target audience will be exposed to the media message.']
  planned_grp decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned Gross Rating Points representing the total reach multiplied by frequency for the target audience.']
  planned_impressions varchar [note: 'Total number of planned impressions (ad exposures) for this media plan.']
  planned_reach_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned percentage of the target audience reached at least once during the planning period.']
  planned_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total planned media spend amount for this media plan in the base currency.']
  planning_period varchar
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the period covered by this media plan.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the period covered by this media plan.']
  start_date date
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the target audience demographic or segment for this media plan (e.g., Adults 25-54, Moms with Kids, HHI 75K+).']
  total_budget decimal(18,2)

  Note: 'Formal media plan document capturing planned media investments across channels (TV, digital, OOH, print, social, search, programmatic) for a brand or campaign. Includes planned impressions, GRPs, reach, frequency, CPM, total planned spend, agency, media owner, market, and planning period. Serves as the authoritative pre-buy record for media investment decisions.'
}

Table marketing.media_spend {
  media_spend_id decimal(18,2) [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the media spend transaction record. Primary key for the media spend data product.']
  campaign_flight_id varchar
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign associated with this media spend transaction.']
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Media spend invoices are posted against cost centers to track advertising costs per organizational unit.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or system that performed the reconciliation of this media spend record.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Accounting of media spend requires a GL account reference for journal entry creation.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand for which this media spend was incurred.']
  media_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the media plan that authorized this spend.']
  agency_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The agency service fee or commission charged for managing the media buy.']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'The name of the media agency that managed the media buy and invoiced the spend.']
  buy_type varchar [note: 'The method by which the media inventory was purchased (e.g., Programmatic, Direct, Auction, Guaranteed).']
  channel varchar
  clicks varchar
  clicks_delivered varchar [note: 'The total number of clicks generated by the media placement for this spend transaction.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the media spend was incurred (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  cpc_actual decimal(10,4) [note: 'The actual cost per click (CPC) achieved for this media spend, calculated as working spend divided by clicks delivered.']
  cpm_actual decimal(10,4) [note: 'The actual cost per thousand impressions (CPM) achieved for this media spend, calculated as (working spend / impressions) * 1000.']
  cpv_actual decimal(10,4) [note: 'The actual cost per video view (CPV) achieved for this media spend, calculated as working spend divided by video views delivered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this media spend record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the spend amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  geography_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic scope of the media placement (e.g., National, Regional, Local, Global).']
  impressions varchar
  impressions_delivered varchar [note: 'The total number of ad impressions delivered for this spend transaction.']
  invoice_number decimal(18,2) [note: 'The invoice number from the media owner or agency billing system for this spend transaction.']
  invoiced_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount invoiced by the media owner or agency for this media spend transaction.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this media spend record is currently active and valid (True) or has been voided or superseded (False).']
  market_name varchar [note: 'The name of the specific market or Designated Market Area (DMA) where the media was placed (e.g., New York, Los Angeles, London).']
  media_channel varchar [note: 'The media channel through which the advertising was delivered (e.g., TV, Digital Display, Social Media, Search). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TV|Radio|Print|Digital Display|Social Media|Search|Video|Out-of-Home|Programmatic|Influencer|Podcast|Streaming|Email|Mobile|Affiliate|Sponsorship|Other — 17 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  media_owner_name varchar [note: 'The name of the media owner or publisher that provided the advertising inventory (e.g., Google, Meta, NBC Universal).']
  media_subchannel varchar [note: 'The specific subchannel or platform within the media channel (e.g., Facebook, Google Ads, CNN, Spotify).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this media spend record was last modified or updated.']
  non_working_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of invoiced spend allocated to agency fees, production costs, and other non-media expenses (non-working media).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this media spend transaction, including reconciliation notes or dispute details.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the invoice for this media spend was paid.']
  payment_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'The payment status of the invoice associated with this media spend transaction.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'The type of media placement or ad format (e.g., Banner, Video Pre-Roll, Native, Sponsored Post, 30-Second Spot).']
  planned_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The originally planned or committed spend amount from the media plan for this channel and period.']
  platform varchar
  production_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost of creative production, ad serving, and other production-related expenses included in this spend transaction.']
  reconciled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this media spend record was reconciled against the media plan and delivery metrics.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'The current reconciliation status of this spend transaction against the media plan and delivery metrics.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this media spend record in the source system.']
  spend_amount decimal(18,2)
  spend_date date [note: 'The date on which the media spend was incurred or invoiced.']
  spend_period decimal(18,2) [note: 'The fiscal or calendar period (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-03) to which this spend is allocated for reporting purposes.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'The demographic or psychographic target audience for this media placement (e.g., Adults 25-54, Moms with Kids, High-Income Households).']
  variance_to_plan_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The difference between invoiced spend and planned spend (invoiced minus planned), indicating over- or under-spend.']
  video_views_delivered varchar [note: 'The total number of video views or completions delivered for video media placements.']
  working_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of invoiced spend that directly purchased media inventory (working media), excluding agency fees and production costs.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of actual media spend incurred per channel, campaign, brand, and time period. Captures invoiced spend, committed spend, variance to plan, media owner, agency fees, working vs. non-working spend classification, and cost-per-metric actuals (CPM, CPC, CPV). Sourced from agency billing systems and reconciled against media plan commitments.'
}

Table marketing.creative_asset {
  creative_asset_id decimal(18,2) [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the creative asset record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign this creative asset is associated with.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual or team responsible for the creative asset content and lifecycle management.']
  label_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.label_version. Business justification: Creative assets used in label design must be tied to the specific label version for regulatory approval traceability.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand associated with this creative asset.']
  parent_asset_creative_asset_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Identifier of the parent creative asset if this asset is a derivative, localized version, or variant of another asset.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Creative assets (images, packaging) are created per SKU; linking enables asset tracking, compliance, and reuse.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when the creative asset was withdrawn or archived from active use.']
  actual_publish_date date [note: 'Actual date when the creative asset was published or went live.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the creative asset in the review workflow (draft, pending review, approved, rejected, expired).']
  asset_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Unique business identifier or code assigned to the creative asset for tracking and reference purposes.']
  asset_format decimal(18,2)
  asset_name decimal(18,2) [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the creative asset (e.g., \'Spring Campaign Hero Banner\', \'Product Launch Video 30s\').']
  asset_type decimal(18,2) [note: 'Classification of the creative asset by media type (video, image, audio, document, HTML, social post, email template). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: video|image|audio|document|html|social_post|email_template — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Notes or comments from compliance and legal review regarding the creative asset.']
