  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory and legal compliance review for the creative asset (not required, pending, approved, rejected).']
  content_category varchar [note: 'Business category or use case of the creative asset (TV commercial, digital banner, social media, print ad, packaging visual, influencer brief, blog post, email content, landing page, infographic). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tv_commercial|digital_banner|social_media|print_ad|packaging_visual|influencer_brief|blog_post|email_content|landing_page|infographic — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  content_format varchar [note: 'Specific file format or content format of the asset (e.g., MP4, JPEG, PNG, PDF, GIF, HTML5).']
  content_status varchar [note: 'Current publishing lifecycle status of the creative asset (planned, scheduled, published, archived, withdrawn).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the creative asset record was first created in the system.']
  creative_agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external creative agency or partner that produced the creative asset.']
  creative_asset_description decimal(18,2) [note: 'Detailed textual description of the creative asset content, purpose, and key messaging.']
  creative_concept varchar [note: 'High-level creative concept or theme of the asset (e.g., \'Family Moments\', \'Sustainability Focus\', \'Product Innovation\').']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the production cost amount.']
  dam_reference_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or reference code in the Digital Asset Management system where the asset is stored.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration of the creative asset in seconds (applicable to video and audio assets).']
  effective_date date
  expiry_date date
  file_reference varchar
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the creative asset file in bytes.']
  file_url varchar
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic regions or markets where the creative asset is approved for use (e.g., \'Global\', \'USA\', \'EMEA\').']
  height_pixels varchar [note: 'Height dimension of the creative asset in pixels (applicable to images, videos, and display ads).']
  is_master_version boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the master or primary version of the creative asset (True) or a derivative/variant (False).']
  keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords or tags associated with the creative asset for search and discovery purposes.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the primary language of the creative asset content.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the creative asset record was last modified or updated.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Planned date for the creative asset to be withdrawn or archived from active use.']
  planned_publish_date date [note: 'Planned or scheduled date for the creative asset to be published or go live.']
  production_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to produce the creative asset.']
  publishing_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel or platform where the creative asset is published or distributed (e.g., \'Facebook\', \'Instagram\', \'YouTube\', \'Website\', \'Email\', \'TV\').']
  rights_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the usage rights or licensing for the creative asset expire.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the creative asset in the source system.']
  storage_location_url varchar [note: 'Full URL or file path where the creative asset is stored in the DAM or content repository.']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'Primary target audience or consumer segment for the creative asset (e.g., \'Millennials\', \'Parents with young children\', \'Health-conscious consumers\').']
  thumbnail_url varchar [note: 'URL of the thumbnail or preview image for the creative asset.']
  usage_rights varchar [note: 'Description of usage rights, licensing terms, or restrictions governing how the creative asset may be used (e.g., \'Global rights\', \'North America only\', \'Digital channels only\').']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number or identifier for the creative asset (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'v2.1\', \'Final\').']
  width_pixels varchar [note: 'Width dimension of the creative asset in pixels (applicable to images, videos, and display ads).']

  Note: 'Master record for each creative asset and content item (TV commercial, digital banner, social post, print ad, packaging visual, influencer brief, video, blog post, email template) used in marketing campaigns or published on owned/earned channels. Captures asset name, format, dimensions, file type, brand, campaign association, creative concept, approval status, rights/usage restrictions, expiry date, DAM reference ID, and content publishing metadata (planned publish date, actual publish date, publishing channel, content status, content type, target audience segment, content owner). Serves as the unified creative and content management record supporting brand compliance, creative reuse tracking, DAM integration, and coordinated omnichannel content calendar planning. No separate content calendar product exists — all scheduling and publishing metadata lives here.'
}

Table marketing.influencer {
  influencer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the influencer or content creator partner. Primary key.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.agency. Business justification: Influencers are often managed through a marketing agency; adding agency_id captures that relationship without creating a bidirectional link.']
  campaign_id varchar
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing team member or user who onboarded this influencer into the system.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: An influencer is typically contracted by a specific brand; adding brand_id creates the parent relationship and enables brand‑level reporting.']
  agent_contact varchar [note: 'Contact email or phone number for the influencer\'s agent or manager.']
  agent_name varchar [note: 'Name of the influencer\'s agent, manager, or talent representation, if applicable.']
  audience_age_range varchar [note: 'Dominant age demographic of the influencer\'s audience, used for campaign targeting and brand alignment.']
  audience_gender_split varchar [note: 'Gender distribution of the influencer\'s audience (e.g., 60% Female, 40% Male). Used for campaign targeting.']
  audience_geography varchar [note: 'Primary geographic markets where the influencer\'s audience is concentrated, expressed as comma-separated ISO 3-letter country codes (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR).']
  average_post_impressions varchar [note: 'Average total impressions (views) per post, calculated from historical campaign performance data.']
  average_post_reach varchar [note: 'Average number of unique users reached per post, calculated from historical campaign performance data.']
  brand_safety_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Brand safety assessment score (0.00 to 5.00) evaluating the influencer\'s content for alignment with brand values, absence of controversial content, and reputational risk. Higher scores indicate safer partnerships.']
  content_category varchar [note: 'Primary content category or niche that the influencer specializes in, used for brand and campaign alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Beauty|Fashion|Lifestyle|Fitness|Food|Travel|Parenting|Tech|Gaming|Home — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiration or termination date of the current contract. Null for open-ended or retainer agreements.']
  contract_reference varchar
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the current or most recent contract with the influencer.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current contractual relationship status with the influencer. Active indicates an ongoing partnership; Inactive indicates no current contract but available for future engagement.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Type of contractual agreement governing the influencer partnership. Retainer and Ambassador indicate long-term relationships; One-Time and Campaign-Based are transactional.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this influencer record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for influencer payments (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for business communication and contract correspondence with the influencer.']
  engagement_rate decimal(8,6)
  engagement_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average engagement rate (likes, comments, shares divided by followers) expressed as a percentage. Key metric for influencer effectiveness.']
  exclusivity_category varchar [note: 'Product category or categories covered by the exclusivity clause (e.g., Beauty, Personal Care, Household). Null if no exclusivity.']
  exclusivity_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the influencer is under an exclusivity agreement preventing them from promoting competing brands.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2)
  fee_per_post decimal(18,2)
  follower_count varchar [note: 'Total number of followers across the primary platform at the time of last update. Used for tier classification and reach estimation.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Legal or professional full name of the influencer or content creator.']
  handle varchar [note: 'Primary social media handle or username of the influencer (e.g., @username). Used as the public identifier across platforms.']
  influencer_category varchar
  influencer_name varchar
  influencer_tier varchar [note: 'Classification tier based on follower count: Nano (<10K), Micro (10K-100K), Macro (100K-1M), Mega (1M+), Celebrity (established public figure).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this influencer record is currently active and available for campaign engagement. False indicates archived or inactive status.']
  last_campaign_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent campaign engagement with this influencer. Used for recency analysis and re-engagement planning.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this influencer record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, preferences, special requirements, or historical observations about the influencer partnership.']
  payment_terms decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the influencer for campaign engagements.']
  performance_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Internal performance rating (0.00 to 5.00) based on historical campaign ROI (Return on Investment), content quality, professionalism, and deliverable timeliness.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the influencer or their management team.']
  platform varchar
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for business communication with the influencer or their representation.']
  primary_platform varchar [note: 'The primary social media platform where the influencer has the largest following or engagement.']
  secondary_platforms varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of additional social media platforms where the influencer maintains an active presence.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this influencer record in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  tier varchar
  total_campaigns_participated decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative count of campaigns the influencer has participated in with Consumer Goods. Used for partnership history and performance tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record for influencer and content creator partners engaged by Consumer Goods, including full engagement/activation history. Captures influencer handle, platform(s), follower count, engagement rate, audience demographics, content category, tier (nano, micro, macro, mega), brand safety score, contract status, preferred contact, and historical campaign participation. Engagement detail records capture: engagement type (sponsored post, product seeding, brand ambassador, event appearance), campaign association, contracted deliverables, content submission dates, approval status, agreed fee, actual fee paid, platform, post URLs, reach, impressions, engagement metrics, and content performance. Serves as the SSOT for influencer partner identity, roster management, and all activation history. No separate engagement product exists — all engagement transactional data lives here as detail records.'
}

Table marketing.consumer_segment {
  consumer_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing consumer segment. Primary key.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar
  primary_consumer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing team member or business unit responsible for managing and activating this segment.']
  age_range_lower varchar [note: 'Lower bound of the age range for consumers in this segment (in years). Null if age is not a defining characteristic.']
  age_range_upper varchar [note: 'Upper bound of the age range for consumers in this segment (in years). Null if age is not a defining characteristic.']
  average_basket_size_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average transaction or basket size in USD for consumers in this segment, used for campaign ROI modeling.']
  brand_affinity varchar [note: 'Brands or brand categories that this segment shows strong preference or loyalty toward. Used for brand-specific campaign targeting.']
  category_affinity varchar [note: 'Product categories that this segment shows high purchase propensity or engagement with.']
  cltv_estimate_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated Customer Lifetime Value in USD for consumers in this segment, used for prioritization and investment decisions.']
  consumer_segment_description varchar
  content_preference varchar [note: 'Types of marketing content that resonate with this segment (e.g., educational, promotional, user-generated, influencer, video).']
  country_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this segment is applicable (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was first created in the system.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Primary data source or vendor used to define and validate this segment (e.g., Nielsen IQ, internal CRM, third-party data provider).']
  digital_engagement_level varchar [note: 'Level of digital channel engagement and activity for consumers in this segment (social media, email, mobile app, e-commerce).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition is scheduled to be retired or replaced. Null for open-ended segments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition became active and available for campaign targeting.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach or focus of this consumer segment (global, regional, national, or local).']
  income_bracket varchar [note: 'Income level classification for consumers in this segment, used for targeting and media planning.']
  innovation_adoption_profile varchar [note: 'Consumer adoption curve classification for new product launches and innovations (based on Rogers Diffusion of Innovations theory).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this segment is currently active and available for use in campaign planning and targeting.']
  key_characteristics varchar [note: 'Defining behavioral, demographic, or psychographic characteristics that distinguish this segment from others.']
  last_refresh_date date [note: 'Date when the segment definition, size estimate, or characteristics were last updated or recalculated.']
  messaging_tone_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred messaging tone and style for communications to this segment (e.g., aspirational, practical, humorous, authoritative).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was last modified or updated.']
  nps_score_average decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average Net Promoter Score for consumers in this segment, indicating brand loyalty and advocacy potential.']
  price_sensitivity_level decimal(18,2) [note: 'Degree of price sensitivity and responsiveness to promotions and discounts for consumers in this segment.']
  promotion_responsiveness varchar [note: 'Level of responsiveness to trade promotions, coupons, and special offers for consumers in this segment.']
  purchase_frequency_profile varchar [note: 'Typical purchase frequency behavior of consumers in this segment for relevant product categories.']
  refresh_frequency varchar [note: 'Planned frequency for updating segment membership and characteristics to maintain accuracy.']
  region_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regional codes or identifiers where this segment is applicable (e.g., state, province, metro area codes).']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the consumer segment used across marketing systems and campaign planning tools.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the consumer segment including defining characteristics, behaviors, and strategic rationale.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the consumer segment used in campaign planning and reporting.']
  segment_size_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated number of consumers or households that belong to this segment based on the most recent segmentation analysis.']
  segment_size_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total addressable consumer base that this segment represents.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consumer segment indicating whether it is actively used in campaign targeting.']
  segment_type varchar
  segmentation_methodology varchar [note: 'Detailed methodology, algorithm, or analytical approach used to create this segment (e.g., RFM analysis, cluster analysis, decision tree).']
  segmentation_model_type varchar [note: 'The methodology or model used to define this consumer segment (behavioral, psychographic, demographic, geographic, needs-based, or hybrid).']
  size_estimate varchar
  social_media_platform_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred social media platforms for consumers in this segment (e.g., Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, Twitter). Comma-separated list.']
  strategic_priority_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic importance ranking of this segment for marketing investment and campaign focus (tier 1 being highest priority).']
  sustainability_consciousness_level varchar [note: 'Level of environmental and sustainability awareness and preference for eco-friendly products within this segment.']
  target_channel_affinity varchar [note: 'Preferred marketing and sales channels for reaching this segment (e.g., digital, retail, DTC, social media, email). Comma-separated list of channel preferences.']

  Note: 'Reference master for consumer segmentation definitions used in targeting and campaign planning. Captures segment name, segmentation model (behavioral, psychographic, demographic, needs-based), segment size estimate, key characteristics, target channel affinity, brand affinity, and strategic priority tier. Used to align campaign targeting across digital, retail, and DTC channels. Distinct from consumer profile (owned by consumer domain).'
}

Table marketing.market_research_study {
  market_research_study_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the market research study or syndicated data subscription. Primary key.']
  agency_id varchar
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the product category being researched in this study.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the employee or marketing manager who is the business owner and primary stakeholder for this market research study.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the brand that is the primary focus of this market research study.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Market research studies are commissioned for specific R&D projects to validate concepts and guide product strategy.']
  analysis_completion_date date [note: 'The date when the research analysis and reporting were finalized and delivered.']
  average_basket_size decimal(10,2) [note: 'The average number of units or volume purchased per transaction by panel households during the measurement period.']
  brand_awareness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of respondents who demonstrated awareness of the brand, either aided or unaided, as measured in the brand health tracker.']
  brand_consideration_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of respondents who would consider purchasing the brand, as measured in the brand health tracker.']
  brand_preference_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of respondents who prefer the brand over competitors, as measured in the brand health tracker.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'The business unit or division that commissioned and is responsible for the market research study (e.g., Skincare, Haircare, Home Care).']
  buyer_demographics varchar [note: 'Summary of the demographic profile of buyers identified in the consumer panel, including age, gender, income, household composition, and other relevant characteristics.']
  cost_amount decimal(18,2)
  country_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where the research study is conducted (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the market research study record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the study investment amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  data_provider_name varchar [note: 'The name of the syndicated data provider for ongoing panel subscriptions or continuous measurement services.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system or platform from which the market research study data and metadata were extracted (e.g., Nielsen IQ, Kantar Portal, internal research repository).']
  end_date date
  fieldwork_end_date date [note: 'The date when data collection or fieldwork for the research study was completed.']
  fieldwork_start_date date [note: 'The date when data collection or fieldwork for the research study began.']
  household_penetration_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of households in the target market that have purchased the brand or category during the measurement period, as reported by consumer panel data.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the market research study record is currently active and relevant for reporting and analysis.']
  key_findings_summary varchar [note: 'Executive summary of the key insights, findings, and actionable recommendations from the market research study.']
  market varchar
  market_research_study_status varchar
  methodology varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the research methodology employed, including sampling approach, data collection method (online survey, in-person interview, focus group, panel tracking), and analytical techniques.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the market research study record was last modified or updated.']
  nps_score decimal(18,6) [note: 'The Net Promoter Score calculated from the research study, measuring customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend the brand on a scale from -100 to +100.']
  purchase_frequency decimal(10,2) [note: 'The average number of purchase occasions per buying household during the measurement period, as reported by consumer panel data.']
  repeat_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of buyers who made repeat purchases of the brand or product during the measurement period, indicating loyalty and retention.']
  research_agency_name varchar [note: 'The name of the external market research agency or syndicated data provider conducting or supplying the study (e.g., Nielsen IQ, Kantar, Circana, Ipsos).']
  research_objective varchar [note: 'The primary business objective or research question that the study was designed to address (e.g., measure brand health, test new product concept, understand usage barriers, track competitive dynamics).']
  research_type varchar [note: 'The type or methodology of the market research study. Includes quantitative surveys, qualitative focus groups, usage and attitude (U&A) studies, concept testing, packaging tests, Net Promoter Score (NPS) surveys, brand health trackers, and consumer panel subscriptions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: quantitative|qualitative|usage_and_attitude|concept_test|pack_test|nps|brand_health_tracker|consumer_panel — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'The total number of respondents or households included in the research study sample.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the market research study in the source system or research agency platform.']
  start_date date
  study_code varchar [note: 'The unique business identifier or project code assigned to the market research study for tracking and reference purposes.']
  study_investment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total financial investment or cost incurred for commissioning and conducting the market research study, including agency fees, fieldwork costs, and data licensing.']
  study_name varchar [note: 'The official name or title of the market research study or syndicated data subscription.']
  study_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the market research study. Tracks progression from planning through fieldwork, analysis, and completion.']
  study_type varchar
  subscription_end_date date [note: 'The end date or renewal date of the syndicated data subscription or continuous panel tracking service.']
  subscription_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the syndicated data subscription or continuous panel tracking service.']
  subscription_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the research study is a one-time project or part of an ongoing syndicated data subscription with periodic updates.']
  switching_behavior_index decimal(10,2) [note: 'A metric indicating the rate at which consumers switch between brands within the category, as measured by consumer panel data.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the target consumer segment or demographic profile for the research study (e.g., women 25-45, households with children, premium beauty buyers).']
  target_market varchar [note: 'The geographic market or region where the research study is being conducted (e.g., North America, Western Europe, APAC).']
  vendor_name varchar

  Note: 'Master record for each market research study or syndicated data subscription commissioned or conducted by Consumer Goods. Captures study name, research type (quantitative, qualitative, U&A, concept test, pack test, NPS, brand health tracker, consumer panel), research agency/data provider (Nielsen IQ, Kantar, Circana, Ipsos), fieldwork dates, target market, sample size, methodology, status, key findings summary, and periodic panel metrics (household penetration, purchase frequency, basket size, repeat rate, switching behavior, buyer demographics). Serves as the SSOT for all primary and syndicated research investment, output tracking, and consumer panel insight management. Enables research ROI tracking and insight activation across brand strategy.'
}

Table marketing.brand_health_tracker {
  brand_health_tracker_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the brand health tracking record.']
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Brand health metrics are overseen by a brand health analyst; needed for audit, KPI ownership, and resource planning.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product category for which brand health is tracked.']
  primary_competitor_brand_marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary competing brand used for benchmarking.']
  aided_awareness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of consumers who recognize the brand when prompted with a list of brands in the category.']
  awareness_percent decimal(5,2)
  awareness_score decimal(5,4)
  brand_equity_index decimal(7,2) [note: 'Composite index score measuring overall brand strength combining awareness, consideration, preference, and loyalty metrics.']
  brand_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total advertising spend for this brand during the measurement period.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Distribution channel context for the brand health measurement.']
  competitor_som_value_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Primary competitor share of market by value for benchmarking purposes.']
  competitor_sov_total_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Primary competitor total share of voice for benchmarking purposes.']
  consideration_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of consumers who would consider purchasing the brand.']
  consideration_percent decimal(5,2)
  consideration_score decimal(5,4)
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this brand health tracking record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts in this record.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Primary data source provider for this brand health tracking record.']
  innovation_perception_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Consumer perception score for brand innovation attribute on a scale of 1-10.']
  market varchar
  market_geography varchar [note: 'Geographic market where brand health is measured (country, region, or city).']
  measurement_date date
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the brand health metrics were measured or compiled.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this brand health tracking record was last modified.']
  net_sentiment_score decimal(6,2) [note: 'Net sentiment score calculated as positive percentage minus negative percentage, ranging from -100 to +100.']
  nps_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Net Promoter Score measuring likelihood of consumers to recommend the brand, calculated as percentage of promoters minus percentage of detractors.']
  preference_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of consumers who prefer this brand over competitors in the category.']
  preference_score decimal(5,4)
  purchase_intent_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of consumers who intend to purchase the brand in the next purchase cycle.']
  purchase_intent_score decimal(5,4)
  quality_perception_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Consumer perception score for product quality attribute on a scale of 1-10.']
  segment varchar
  share_of_conversation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Brand share of total social media conversation volume within the category.']
  social_mention_volume varchar [note: 'Total number of social media mentions for the brand during the tracking period.']
  social_sentiment_negative_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of social media mentions with negative sentiment during the tracking period.']
  social_sentiment_neutral_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of social media mentions with neutral sentiment during the tracking period.']
  social_sentiment_positive_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of social media mentions with positive sentiment during the tracking period.']
  som_value_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Brand share of total category sales measured by value (revenue) for the period and geography.']
  som_volume_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Brand share of total category sales measured by volume (units) for the period and geography.']
  sov_digital_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Share of voice for digital advertising (display, search, video) as percentage of total category digital spend.']
  sov_ooh_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Share of voice for out-of-home advertising (billboards, transit, etc.) as percentage of total category OOH spend.']
  sov_social_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Share of voice for social media advertising as percentage of total category social spend.']
  sov_total_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total share of voice across all media channels as percentage of total category advertising spend.']
  sov_tv_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Share of voice for television advertising as percentage of total category TV spend.']
  spontaneous_awareness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of consumers who mention the brand unprompted when asked about the category.']
  sustainability_perception_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Consumer perception score for environmental and social responsibility attribute on a scale of 1-10.']
  total_category_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total advertising spend across all brands in the category for the measurement period.']
  tracking_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the brand health measurement period.']
  tracking_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the brand health measurement period.']
  trust_perception_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Consumer perception score for brand trust and reliability attribute on a scale of 1-10.']
  value_perception_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Consumer perception score for value-for-money attribute on a scale of 1-10.']

  Note: 'Consolidated periodic brand performance scorecard capturing all consumer perception, market position, competitive share, media share, and social sentiment metrics for a brand in a specific market and time period. Captures awareness (spontaneous, aided), consideration, preference, purchase intent, NPS, brand equity index, SOV% (Share of Voice by channel — TV, digital, OOH, social), SOM% (value share, volume share by category/channel/geography), total category spend, competitor benchmarks, social sentiment scores (positive/neutral/negative breakdown), mention volume, share of conversation, platform distribution, net sentiment score, and key image attribute scores. Sourced from Nielsen Ad Intel/Kantar media monitoring (SOV), Nielsen IQ Retail Measurement/Circana (SOM), proprietary brand tracking studies (awareness/equity), and social listening platforms (sentiment). Serves as the single authoritative brand scorecard enabling longitudinal brand health monitoring, SOV vs. SOM gap analysis, competitive benchmarking, market share trend tracking, and sentiment trend analysis.'
}

Table marketing.sov_measurement {
  sov_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Share of Voice measurement record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing analyst or user who created or validated the Share of Voice measurement record.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand being measured for Share of Voice.']
  product_category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category for which Share of Voice is measured.']
  brand_rank varchar [note: 'Rank of the brand by Share of Voice within the category (1 = highest SOV).']
  brand_som_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The brand\'s Share of Market (sales-based market share) during the same measurement period, used for SOV vs. SOM gap analysis.']
  brand_sov_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The brand\'s Share of Voice as a percentage of total category advertising spend during the measurement period.']
  brand_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total advertising spend by the brand during the measurement period.']
  channel varchar
  competitor_brand varchar
  competitor_count varchar [note: 'Number of competitor brands included in the category Share of Voice calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the Share of Voice measurement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for spend amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or vendor providing the Share of Voice measurement data (e.g., Nielsen Ad Intel, Kantar Media, Comscore).']
  frequency decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average number of times the target audience was exposed to the brand\'s advertising during the measurement period.']
  grp decimal(10,2) [note: 'Gross Rating Points achieved by the brand during the measurement period, representing reach multiplied by frequency.']
  impressions_count varchar [note: 'Total number of advertising impressions delivered by the brand during the measurement period.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Share of Voice measurement record is currently active (True) or archived (False).']
  market varchar
  market_geography varchar [note: 'Geographic market where the Share of Voice is measured (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  measurement_date date [note: 'Date when the Share of Voice measurement was calculated and recorded.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate Share of Voice (e.g., spend-based, impression-based, GRP-based).']
  measurement_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the Share of Voice measurement period.']
  measurement_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the Share of Voice measurement period.']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Share of Voice measurement record (Preliminary, Final, Revised, Archived).']
  media_channel varchar [note: 'Media channel through which advertising spend is measured (TV, Digital, Radio, Print, Out-of-Home, Social Media).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the Share of Voice measurement record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or commentary regarding the Share of Voice measurement, including data quality issues, anomalies, or contextual information.']
  reach_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the target audience reached by the brand\'s advertising during the measurement period.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the Share of Voice measurement record in the source system.']
  sov_measurement_category varchar
  sov_percent decimal(5,2)
  sov_percentage decimal(8,4)
  sov_som_gap decimal(5,2) [note: 'Difference between brand SOV percentage and brand SOM percentage (SOV - SOM). Positive gap indicates over-investment; negative gap indicates under-investment.']
  top_competitor_brand_name varchar [note: 'Name of the competitor brand with the highest Share of Voice in the category during the measurement period.']
  top_competitor_sov_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Share of Voice percentage of the top competitor brand in the category.']
  total_category_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total advertising spend across all brands in the category during the measurement period.']

  Note: 'Periodic Share of Voice (SOV) measurement record capturing a brand\'s advertising share relative to total category spend across media channels, market, and time period. Captures brand SOV%, competitor SOV%, total category spend, brand spend, channel (TV, digital, OOH, social), data source (Nielsen Ad Intel, Kantar), and measurement period. Enables SOV vs. SOM gap analysis.'
}

Table marketing.digital_performance {
  digital_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the digital campaign performance record. Primary key for this transactional performance measurement entity.']
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Digital performance metrics are compiled by a marketing analyst; link enables attribution of analysis effort and audit.']
  campaign_flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific campaign flight or wave. Links this performance record to the flight-level execution plan.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent marketing campaign. Links this performance record to the campaign master data.']
  creative_asset_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Identifier for the specific creative asset used in this campaign execution. Links performance to creative master data for creative effectiveness analysis.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand being promoted in this campaign. Links performance to brand master data for brand-level reporting.']
  ad_format varchar [note: 'The specific creative format of the digital ad (e.g., carousel, single image, video, story, banner, interstitial, native). Describes the presentation style of the creative asset.']
  attribution_model varchar [note: 'The attribution methodology used to assign conversion credit to this touchpoint. Defines how conversions are credited across the customer journey.']
  attribution_window_days varchar [note: 'The number of days after ad interaction during which conversions are attributed to this campaign. Defines the lookback period for conversion tracking.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The digital marketing channel type. Categorizes the advertising medium (social, search, display, video, programmatic, native, email). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Social|Search|Display|Video|Programmatic|Native|Email — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  click_through_rate decimal(8,6)
  clicks varchar [note: 'Total number of clicks on the ad. Measures user engagement and intent to interact with the campaign.']
  conversion_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Conversion rate expressed as a decimal (conversions divided by clicks). Measures the effectiveness of the ad in driving desired actions.']
  conversions varchar [note: 'Total number of conversions attributed to this campaign on this date and channel. Represents completed desired actions (purchase, sign-up, download, etc.) as defined by the campaign objective.']
  cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total media spend for this campaign on this date and channel. Represents the actual cost incurred for ad delivery.']
  cpa decimal(18,4) [note: 'Average cost per conversion (total cost divided by conversions). Measures the efficiency of conversion-focused campaigns.']
  cpc decimal(18,4) [note: 'Average cost per click (total cost divided by clicks). Key efficiency metric for click-based campaigns.']
  cpm decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per thousand impressions (total cost divided by impressions, multiplied by 1000). Standard metric for awareness campaigns.']
  created_timestamp timestamp
  ctr decimal(10,6) [note: 'Click-through rate expressed as a decimal (clicks divided by impressions). Key engagement metric indicating ad relevance and creative effectiveness.']
  ctr_percent decimal(7,4)
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency campaign performance tracking.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The source system or API from which this performance data was ingested (e.g., Meta Ads API, Google Ads API, TikTok Ads Manager). Tracks data lineage and integration source.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The device category on which the ad was served and performance was measured. Segments performance by device form factor.']
  frequency decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average number of times each unique user saw the ad (impressions divided by reach). Measures ad exposure intensity per user.']
  geography varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic market where the ad was served. Enables market-level performance analysis.']
  impressions varchar [note: 'Total number of times the ad was displayed to users. Represents the reach volume of the campaign on this date and channel.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance record was ingested into the lakehouse from the source platform API. Supports data freshness monitoring and audit trail.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this performance record is currently active and should be included in reporting. Supports soft-delete and data quality management.']
  measurement_date date [note: 'The date on which this digital performance was measured and recorded. Represents the business event timestamp for this transactional record at campaign-channel-date grain.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'The specific placement or inventory type within the platform (e.g., feed, stories, in-stream, search results, display network). Identifies where the ad appeared within the platform ecosystem.']
  platform varchar [note: 'The digital advertising platform where the campaign was executed. Identifies the ad network or publisher platform (e.g., Meta, Google, TikTok, programmatic DSP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Meta|Google|TikTok|Programmatic DSP|YouTube|LinkedIn|Twitter — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  product_category varchar [note: 'The product category being promoted in this campaign. Enables category-level performance analysis and cross-category comparison.']
  reach varchar [note: 'Total number of unique users who saw the ad at least once. Measures unduplicated audience size exposed to the campaign.']
  revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue attributed to conversions from this campaign on this date and channel. Used to calculate ROAS and campaign profitability.']
  revenue_attributed decimal(18,2)
  roas decimal(18,4) [note: 'Return on ad spend expressed as a ratio (revenue generated divided by cost). Key profitability metric for performance marketing campaigns.']
  spend decimal(18,2)
  spend_amount decimal(18,2)
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the target audience segment for this campaign execution. Defines the demographic, behavioral, or interest-based targeting criteria applied.']
  video_completion_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Percentage of video views that were watched to completion. Expressed as a decimal. Measures video creative engagement quality.']
  video_quartile_25_pct decimal(10,6) [note: 'Percentage of video views that reached the 25% completion point. Expressed as a decimal. Tracks early engagement drop-off.']
  video_quartile_50_pct decimal(10,6) [note: 'Percentage of video views that reached the 50% completion point (midpoint). Expressed as a decimal. Measures sustained viewer interest.']
  video_quartile_75_pct decimal(10,6) [note: 'Percentage of video views that reached the 75% completion point. Expressed as a decimal. Indicates strong creative engagement.']
  video_views varchar [note: 'Total number of video views for video ad formats. Counts views meeting the platforms minimum view threshold (typically 3 seconds or more).']
  view_through_conversions varchar [note: 'Number of conversions attributed to users who viewed but did not click the ad, then converted within the attribution window. Measures passive ad influence.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of digital campaign performance metrics captured at the campaign-channel-date grain. Captures impressions, clicks, CTR, video views, view-through rate, conversions, cost-per-click (CPC), cost-per-acquisition (CPA), ROAS (Return on Ad Spend), reach, frequency, and platform (Meta, Google, TikTok, programmatic DSP). Sourced from digital ad platforms via API integration.'
}

Table marketing.attribution {
  attribution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for attribution']
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Attribution models are maintained by an analyst; linking provides accountability and traceability for model changes.']
  attribution_product_sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product purchased or converted in the attributed event.']
  attribution_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Attribution models assign conversion revenue to specific SKUs; FK ensures accurate SKU‑level performance metrics.']
  campaign_flight_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific campaign flight (time-bound execution) receiving attribution credit.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign receiving attribution credit for this conversion.']
  creative_asset_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Identifier of the creative asset (ad, banner, video) shown at this touchpoint.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand associated with the attributed conversion.']
  shopper_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the consumer who completed the conversion or purchase event.']
  attributed_clicks varchar
  attributed_conversions decimal(10,4) [note: 'Fractional conversion count credited to this touchpoint (e.g., 0.25 conversions for 25% attribution weight).']
  attributed_impressions varchar
  attributed_revenue decimal(18,2)
  attributed_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue amount credited to this touchpoint based on the attribution weight.']
  attribution_status varchar [note: 'Status of the attribution record (final, provisional, recalculated, voided).']
  calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the attribution calculation was performed.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Marketing channel that received attribution credit (paid search, organic search, display, social media, email, affiliate, direct, referral, video, mobile app, TV, radio, print, outdoor, in-store). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paid_search|organic_search|display|social_media|email|affiliate|direct|referral|video|mobile_app|tv|radio|print|outdoor|in_store — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  conversion_event_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the conversion event (purchase, sign-up, download) being attributed.']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the conversion event occurred.']
  conversion_type varchar [note: 'Type of conversion event being attributed (purchase, add-to-cart, sign-up, download, lead, subscription, trial, engagement). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: purchase|add_to_cart|sign_up|download|lead|subscription|trial|engagement — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cost_per_acquisition decimal(18,2)
  cpa decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost per acquisition for this attributed touchpoint (media spend attributed / attributed conversions).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this attribution record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for attributed revenue amount.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or platform that provided the attribution data (Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, internal MTA platform, etc.).']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used by the consumer at this touchpoint (desktop, mobile, tablet, smart TV, wearable, other).']
  geography varchar [note: 'Three-letter country code where the conversion occurred.']
  is_direct_conversion boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this was a direct conversion with no prior touchpoints (True/False).']
  is_view_through boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this touchpoint was a view-through impression (ad viewed but not clicked) (True/False).']
  lookback_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the attribution lookback window used to identify contributing touchpoints.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Consumer market segment targeted by the attributed campaign.']
  measurement_date date
  measurement_period_end date
  measurement_period_start date
  media_spend_attributed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of media spend allocated to this touchpoint based on attribution weight.']
  model varchar
  model_type varchar [note: 'Type of attribution model applied to assign credit (last-touch, first-touch, linear, time-decay, position-based, data-driven MTA).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this attribution record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about this attribution record.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Digital platform or publisher where the touchpoint occurred (Google Ads, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, etc.).']
  roas decimal(10,4) [note: 'Return on Ad Spend calculated for this attributed touchpoint (attributed revenue / media spend attributed).']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit code of the product involved in the conversion event.']
  time_to_conversion_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of hours elapsed between this touchpoint and the final conversion event.']
  total_touchpoints decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total number of marketing touchpoints in the consumer journey leading to this conversion.']
  touchpoint_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Position of this touchpoint in the consumer journey sequence (1 = first touch, N = last touch).']
  touchpoint_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this specific marketing touchpoint occurred.']
  weight decimal(5,4) [note: 'Fractional credit assigned to this touchpoint by the attribution model (0.0000 to 1.0000, sum across all touchpoints = 1.0).']

  Note: 'Marketing attribution record linking consumer purchase or conversion events to contributing marketing touchpoints (campaign, channel, creative) using a defined attribution model (last-touch, first-touch, linear, data-driven, MTA). Captures attributed revenue, attributed conversions, attribution model type, touchpoint sequence, channel contribution weight, and brand. Enables ROI measurement across the marketing mix.'
}

Table marketing.social_listening_record {
  social_listening_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the social listening record.']
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Social listening reports are authored by a social media analyst; required for report sign‑off and workload tracking.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product category being monitored.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand being monitored in this social listening record.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific product being monitored, if applicable.']
  competitive_brand_mention_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether competitive brands were mentioned alongside the monitored brand.']
  country_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where mentions originated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this social listening record was first created in the system.']
  crisis_alert_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the mention volume or sentiment triggered a brand crisis alert threshold.']
  data_collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the social listening data was collected from the source platform.']
  data_source_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the social listening platform or vendor that provided the data (e.g., Brandwatch, Sprinklr, Talkwalker).']
  engagement_count varchar [note: 'Total engagement actions (likes, shares, comments, retweets) across all mentions.']
  facebook_mention_count varchar [note: 'Number of mentions originating from Facebook platform.']
  geography_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the social listening monitoring.']
  impressions_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated total number of times the mentions were displayed.']
  influencer_mention_count varchar [note: 'Number of mentions originating from identified influencer accounts.']
  instagram_mention_count varchar [note: 'Number of mentions originating from Instagram platform.']
  language_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 639-1 language codes detected in the mentions.']
  mention_count varchar
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this social listening record was last modified.']
  monitoring_topic varchar [note: 'The specific topic, keyword, or hashtag being monitored in social media channels.']
  negative_mention_count varchar [note: 'Number of mentions classified as negative sentiment.']
  negative_mentions varchar
  net_sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated net sentiment score representing overall sentiment balance, typically ranging from -100 to +100.']
  neutral_mention_count varchar [note: 'Number of mentions classified as neutral sentiment.']
  neutral_mentions varchar
  observation_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the time period for which social listening data was collected.']
  observation_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the time period for which social listening data was collected.']
  platform varchar
  positive_mention_count varchar [note: 'Number of mentions classified as positive sentiment.']
  positive_mentions varchar
  reach varchar
  reach_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated number of unique users who were exposed to the mentions.']
  record_date date
  recorded_date date
  reddit_mention_count varchar [note: 'Number of mentions originating from Reddit platform.']
  sentiment_analysis_model varchar [note: 'Name or version of the sentiment analysis algorithm or model used to classify mentions.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,4)
  share_of_conversation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total category or competitive conversation captured by this brand or product.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this record in the source system.']
  tiktok_mention_count varchar [note: 'Number of mentions originating from TikTok platform.']
  top_theme_1 varchar [note: 'The most frequently discussed theme or topic within the mentions.']
  top_theme_2 varchar [note: 'The second most frequently discussed theme or topic within the mentions.']
  top_theme_3 varchar [note: 'The third most frequently discussed theme or topic within the mentions.']
  topic_keywords varchar
  total_mention_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total number of social media mentions captured during the observation period.']
  twitter_mention_count varchar [note: 'Number of mentions originating from Twitter/X platform.']
  youtube_mention_count varchar [note: 'Number of mentions originating from YouTube platform.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of social media listening and sentiment monitoring results for a brand, product, or topic at a given time period. Captures mention volume, sentiment breakdown (positive, neutral, negative), share of conversation, top themes, platform distribution (Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok, Reddit, YouTube), net sentiment score, and data source. Enables consumer sentiment trend analysis and brand reputation monitoring.'
}

Table marketing.nielsen_panel_insight {
  nielsen_panel_insight_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Nielsen panel insight record. Primary key for this data product.']
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Nielsen insights are analyzed by a market analyst; link supports audit of insight generation and analyst KPI.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the product category to which this panel insight applies.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the brand to which this panel insight applies.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific SKU to which this panel insight applies. Nullable for category or brand-level insights.']
  average_basket_size decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average quantity (units or volume) purchased per transaction by buying households during the panel period.']
  average_price_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Average price paid per unit or volume by panel households during the panel period.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'Name of the brand measured in this panel insight.']
  brand_switching_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage of households that switched from this brand to a competitor brand during the panel period. Expressed as a decimal.']
  buyer_age_group varchar [note: 'Age group classification of the primary buyer in panel households.']
  buyer_household_size varchar [note: 'Number of people in the buying household.']
  buyer_income_bracket varchar [note: 'Annual household income bracket of the primary buyer in panel households.']
  buyer_presence_of_children boolean [note: 'Indicates whether children are present in the buying household.']
  buyer_profile_segment varchar [note: 'Demographic or psychographic segment classification of the primary buyer households (e.g., Millennials with Kids, Affluent Seniors, Budget-Conscious Families).']
  buying_households_count varchar [note: 'Number of panel households that purchased the category, brand, or SKU during the panel period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this panel insight record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the total spend amount (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP, EUR).']
  data_extract_date date [note: 'Date when this panel insight data was extracted from the Nielsen IQ source system.']
  data_source varchar
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system from which this panel insight was extracted (e.g., Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel, Nielsen Homescan).']
  dollar_sales decimal(18,2)
  gtin varchar [note: 'The GTIN (UPC, EAN) of the SKU measured in this panel insight. Nullable for category or brand-level insights.']
  household_penetration_pct decimal(8,4)
  household_penetration_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage of panel households that purchased the category, brand, or SKU during the panel period. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2345 = 23.45%).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this panel insight record is currently active and valid for use in analytics and reporting.']
  loyalty_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Nielsen IQ loyalty index score measuring the strength of repeat purchase behavior for this brand or SKU. Higher values indicate stronger loyalty.']
  market varchar
  market_geography varchar [note: 'Geographic market or region covered by this panel insight (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR, DEU).']
  market_share_pct decimal(8,4)
  market_share_percent decimal(5,2)
  measurement_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the level of aggregation for this panel insight: category-level, brand-level, or SKU-level.']
  measurement_period_end date
  measurement_period_start date
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this panel insight record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  nielsen_panel_insight_category varchar
  panel_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the Nielsen consumer panel from which this insight was derived.']
  panel_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the panel measurement period for which this insight was captured.']
  panel_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the panel measurement period for which this insight was captured.']
  panel_type varchar
  period varchar
  product_description varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or label of the product measured in this panel insight.']
  purchase_frequency decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average number of purchase occasions per buying household during the panel period.']
  repeat_purchase_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage of buying households that made more than one purchase during the panel period. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.6500 = 65%).']
  share_of_category_spend decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage of total category spend captured by this brand or SKU within the panel. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1250 = 12.5%).']
  share_of_category_volume decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage of total category volume (units) captured by this brand or SKU within the panel. Expressed as a decimal.']
  total_panel_households decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total number of households in the Nielsen panel for this geography and period.']
  total_purchase_occasions decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total number of purchase transactions recorded for the category, brand, or SKU during the panel period.']
  total_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount spent by panel households on the category, brand, or SKU during the panel period.']
  total_units_purchased decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity (units or volume) of the category, brand, or SKU purchased by panel households during the panel period.']
  unit_sales varchar

  Note: 'Periodic consumer panel insight record sourced from Nielsen IQ panel data. Captures category, brand, SKU-level household penetration, purchase frequency, basket size, repeat rate, switching behavior, buyer profile demographics, and panel period. Serves as the authoritative source for Nielsen IQ consumer panel metrics used in brand and category strategy decisions.'
}

Table marketing.agency {
  agency_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing agency partner. Primary key for the agency master record.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'P6: Connect procurement.agency by adding column supplier_id (BIGINT) with FK to procurement.supplier.supplier_id because agencies are external vendors and should link to the supplier master. P6: connect_table: procurement.agency** - add column ']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the agency\'s primary business address (street address, building number).']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the agency\'s primary business address (suite, floor, building name). Nullable.']
  agency_code varchar [note: 'Internal short code or identifier used to reference the agency in operational systems and reporting.']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the marketing agency partner.']
  agency_status varchar
  agency_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the agency based on core service offering: creative (brand and campaign creative development), media (media planning and buying), digital (digital marketing and social media), pr (public relations and communications), shopper (shopper marketing and retail execution), research (market research and consumer insights).']
  annual_retainer_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annual retainer fee amount committed under the current contract. Null if engagement is project-based only.']
  assigned_brands varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Consumer Goods brand names or brand identifiers that this agency is contracted to support.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the agency\'s primary business address.']
  contact_email varchar
  contact_name varchar
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled expiration or termination date of the current agency contract. Null for open-ended agreements.']
  contract_reference varchar
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the current agency contract or master service agreement.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the agency contract: active (contract in force), pending (contract under negotiation or awaiting signature), suspended (temporarily paused), terminated (ended before expiration), expired (contract term ended).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the agency\'s primary business address (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this agency record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts associated with this agency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet DUNS number for the agency, used for vendor identification and credit assessment.']
  engagement_model varchar [note: 'Basis of the commercial relationship: retainer (ongoing monthly/annual fee), project (discrete project-based fees), hybrid (combination of retainer and project fees), performance (fee tied to performance metrics).']
  fee_structure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Description of the agency\'s fee arrangement, including rate type (hourly, monthly retainer, project-based, commission percentage, performance-based) and any tiered or volume-based pricing.']
  holding_group varchar [note: 'Name of the parent holding company or network to which the agency belongs (e.g., WPP, Omnicom, Publicis, IPG, Dentsu). Null if the agency is independent.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agency record is currently active and available for assignment to campaigns and projects. False indicates the agency relationship has been terminated or is inactive.']
  is_minority_owned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agency is certified as a minority-owned business enterprise (MBE) for supplier diversity tracking and reporting.']
  is_preferred_vendor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agency is designated as a preferred or strategic vendor with priority consideration for new projects and campaigns.']
  is_woman_owned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agency is certified as a woman-owned business enterprise (WBE) for supplier diversity tracking and reporting.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal performance review or scorecard assessment for this agency.']
  markets_served varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic markets or regions where the agency provides services to Consumer Goods (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC, LATAM, or specific country codes).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this agency record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or context about the agency relationship, capabilities, or historical performance.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the agency was formally onboarded and added to the approved agency roster for Consumer Goods.']
  payment_terms decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the agency (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, milestone-based, advance payment). Defines when invoices are due.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Most recent performance evaluation rating assigned to the agency based on quality of work, timeliness, collaboration, and business impact. Used for agency benchmarking and roster optimization.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the agency\'s primary business address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary agency contact for operational communication and coordination.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary point of contact at the agency for day-to-day engagement and escalation.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Business phone number of the primary agency contact.']
  retainer_amount decimal(18,2)
  scope_of_services varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the services provided by the agency under the current engagement (e.g., brand strategy, creative production, media planning, digital campaign execution, influencer management, market research).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this agency record in the source operational system, used for traceability and reconciliation.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the agency\'s primary business address.']
  tax_number decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax identification number or employer identification number (EIN) of the agency for tax reporting and compliance purposes.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the agency relationship was formally terminated or the agency was removed from the active roster. Null if the agency is still active.']

  Note: 'Master record for marketing agency partners engaged by Consumer Goods. Captures agency name, agency type (creative, media, digital, PR, shopper, research, influencer), holding group, scope of services, retainer vs. project basis, primary contact, contract status, performance rating, markets served, assigned brands, and fee structure. Serves as the SSOT for agency partner identity and roster management within the marketing domain. Enables agency performance benchmarking and spend consolidation analysis.'
}

Table marketing.marketing_budget {
  marketing_budget_id decimal(18,2) [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing budget allocation record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category to which this budget allocation is assigned. Null for brand-level or corporate-level budgets.']
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2)
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Budget allocations are often earmarked to meet ESG commitments; linking enables financial ESG tracking.']
  finance_budget_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'FK to authoritative finance.finance_budget; resolves SSOT duplicate']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand to which this budget allocation is assigned. Null for multi-brand or corporate-level budgets.']
  primary_marketing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing leader or business owner responsible for managing and executing this budget allocation.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative amount of budget that has been actually spent (invoiced and paid) as of the measurement date.']
  agency_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the budget allocated to agency fees and retainers for creative, media, and strategic services.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this budget allocation was formally approved by the authorized approver or budget committee.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2)
  budget_category varchar
  budget_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Externally-known unique code for this marketing budget allocation, used for tracking and reference across planning systems.']
  budget_context varchar [note: 'Marketing-specific budget context']
  budget_name decimal(18,2) [note: 'Human-readable name or title for this marketing budget allocation, typically describing the scope or purpose.']
  budget_period decimal(18,2)
  budget_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget allocation in the approval and execution workflow.']
  budget_type decimal(18,2) [note: 'Classification of the budget allocation by planning horizon and purpose (annual, quarterly, campaign-specific, project-based, tactical, or strategic).']
  budget_year decimal(18,2)
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'The type of marketing campaign or activity this budget supports (awareness, consideration, conversion, retention, product launch, seasonal, promotional). Null for non-campaign budgets. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: awareness|consideration|conversion|retention|launch|seasonal|promotional — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  channel varchar [note: 'The primary marketing channel or media type for which this budget is allocated (digital, TV, print, radio, out-of-home, social media, search, display, video, retail, events). Null for multi-channel budgets. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: digital|tv|print|radio|ooh|social|search|display|video|retail|events — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2)
  committed_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative amount of budget that has been committed through purchase orders, contracts, or reservations but not yet invoiced or paid.']
  contingency_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the budget held in reserve for unforeseen opportunities or adjustments during the budget period.']
  country_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU) to which this budget applies. Null for global budgets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this budget record was first created in the system, capturing the initial planning event.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all budget amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this budget allocation expires and no further spending can be committed. Null for open-ended budgets.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this budget allocation becomes effective and spending can be committed against it.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year, represented as an integer 1-12. Null for annual or quarterly budgets.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter within the fiscal year to which this budget allocation applies (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4). Null for annual budgets.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this budget allocation applies, represented as a four-digit year (e.g., 2024).']
  geography_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic scope of the marketing investment covered by this budget (global, regional, national, local).']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code under which this marketing budget spend is recorded in the financial system.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this budget allocation is currently active and available for spending (True) or has been deactivated or superseded (False).']
  marketing_budget_status varchar
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this budget record was last modified or updated, reflecting the most recent change to budget allocation or spend tracking.']
  non_working_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the total budget allocated to non-working expenses (production, creative development, agency fees, research) that do not directly reach consumers.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments providing additional context, rationale, or special instructions for this budget allocation.']
  production_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the budget allocated specifically to creative production costs (video production, photography, design, content creation).']
  research_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the budget allocated to market research, consumer insights, brand tracking, and measurement activities.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this budget record in the source operational system, used for traceability and reconciliation.']
  spent_amount decimal(18,2)
  total_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total approved marketing investment amount for this budget allocation, including all working and non-working components.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The difference between the total budget amount and the actual spend amount (positive indicates under-spend, negative indicates over-spend).']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The variance amount expressed as a percentage of the total budget amount, indicating budget utilization performance.']
  working_media_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the total budget allocated to working media spend (paid media placements, impressions, airtime) that directly reaches consumers.']

  Note: 'Marketing department budget for campaigns, media, and brand activities. SSOT for marketing spend planning. Distinct from finance.finance_budget which is the corporate-level financial budget.'
}

Table marketing.market_share_record {
  market_share_record_id decimal(18,6) [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the market share measurement record.']
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Market share reports are produced by an analyst; linking enables responsibility tracking and report version control.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category within which market share is calculated.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand for which market share is measured.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific SKU for which market share is measured. Nullable when measurement is at brand or category level.']
  acv_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total category sales volume represented by stores carrying the brand or SKU (weighted distribution).']
  brand_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total sales value (revenue) for the brand or SKU during the measurement period.']
  brand_volume_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total sales volume (units) for the brand or SKU during the measurement period.']
