  category_total_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total sales value (revenue) for the entire category during the measurement period.']
  category_total_volume_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total sales volume (units) for the entire category during the measurement period.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Retail channel or trade class within which market share is measured (e.g., total market, food, drug, mass, club, convenience, ecommerce). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: total_market|food|drug|mass|club|convenience|ecommerce — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Data quality or confidence level assigned to this measurement (high, medium, low) based on sample size or data completeness.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first created in the data platform.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts in this record.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source of the market share data (e.g., Nielsen IQ Retail Measurement, IRI/Circana, internal sales data, other syndicated source).']
  data_source_report_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or reference number from the source system report.']
  geography_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the specific geographic area (e.g., country code, state code, metro code) for the measurement.']
  geography_level varchar [note: 'Geographic granularity at which market share is measured (national, regional, state, metro, local).']
  geography_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the geographic area for the measurement.']
  is_projected boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this record contains projected/estimated data (true) or actual reported data (false).']
  market varchar
  market_definition varchar [note: 'Description of the market scope and boundaries used for the share calculation (e.g., total category, specific segment, competitive set).']
  market_share_percent decimal(5,2)
  market_share_record_category varchar
  market_share_value_pct decimal(8,4)
  market_share_volume_pct decimal(8,4)
  measurement_date date [note: 'Date when the market share measurement was captured or reported by the data source.']
  measurement_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the time period for which market share is measured.']
  measurement_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the time period for which market share is measured (e.g., week, month, quarter).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was last modified in the data platform.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, commentary, or context about this market share measurement (e.g., data anomalies, market events, methodology changes).']
  period varchar
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the measurement period (weekly, monthly, quarterly, yearly).']
  rank_position varchar [note: 'Competitive rank position of the brand or SKU within the category for this period (1 = market leader).']
  share_point_change decimal(5,2) [note: 'Change in value share percentage points compared to the previous comparable period (positive = gain, negative = loss).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this record in the source system.']
  tdp_count varchar [note: 'Total Distribution Points representing the weighted percentage of stores carrying the brand or SKU.']
  value_share_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Market share expressed as a percentage of total category sales value (revenue). Also known as dollar share or revenue share.']
  volume_share_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Market share expressed as a percentage of total category sales volume (units). Also known as unit share.']
  volume_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for volume quantities (e.g., units, cases, liters, kilograms).']
  year_over_year_change_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage change in value share compared to the same period in the prior year.']

  Note: 'Periodic market share (SOM) measurement record capturing a brand\'s or SKU\'s value and volume share within a defined category, channel, and geography at a given time period. Captures value share %, volume share %, category total value, brand value, data source (Nielsen IQ Retail Measurement, IRI/Circana), market definition, and period. Enables SOM trend tracking and competitive benchmarking.'
}

Table marketing.sponsorship {
  sponsorship_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sponsorship agreement record. Primary key.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product category or business unit associated with this sponsorship for portfolio management and reporting.']
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Sponsorship contracts include ESG objectives; linking enables monitoring of sustainability outcomes.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand associated with this sponsorship agreement.']
  primary_sponsorship_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or executive who approved this sponsorship agreement.']
  activation_rights varchar [note: 'Detailed description of marketing activation rights granted under the sponsorship, including logo usage, signage, hospitality, content creation, social media, and promotional activities.']
  agency_partner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external agency or consultant managing sponsorship activation and measurement.']
  annual_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual monetary value of the sponsorship agreement, calculated as total contract value divided by contract duration in years.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this sponsorship agreement was formally approved by authorized stakeholders.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Reference URL or file path to the signed sponsorship contract document stored in the document management system.']
  contract_duration_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'The length of the sponsorship contract expressed in years, including fractional years for partial-year agreements.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'The date when the sponsorship agreement expires or terminates.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'The date when the sponsorship agreement becomes effective and rights begin.']
  contract_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the sponsorship contract over its full term.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this sponsorship record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this sponsorship record.']
  end_date date
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sponsorship includes category exclusivity rights preventing competitors from sponsoring the same property.']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the scope of exclusivity rights, including product categories, geographic territories, and competitive restrictions.']
  fee decimal(18,2)
  fee_amount decimal(18,2)
  hospitality_entitlements varchar [note: 'Description of hospitality benefits included in the sponsorship, such as VIP tickets, suite access, meet-and-greet opportunities, and event passes.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sponsorship record is currently active and valid for reporting and analytics.']
  measurement_framework varchar [note: 'Description of the measurement methodology and key performance indicators (KPIs) used to evaluate sponsorship effectiveness and ROI.']
  media_rights varchar [note: 'Description of media and broadcast rights included in the sponsorship, such as commercial time, broadcast mentions, digital content rights, and streaming access.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this sponsorship record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context about the sponsorship agreement, activation plans, or special considerations.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Primary marketing objective or strategic goal for this sponsorship investment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: brand_awareness|brand_positioning|consumer_engagement|market_entry|loyalty_building|product_launch|corporate_social_responsibility — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  partner_name varchar
  payment_schedule decimal(18,2) [note: 'Description of the payment terms and schedule for the sponsorship contract, including installment amounts and due dates.']
  performance_obligations varchar [note: 'Contractual performance obligations and deliverables required from the sponsored property, including appearances, content creation, and promotional activities.']
  property_name varchar [note: 'The name of the sponsored property (e.g., team name, event name, celebrity name, venue name).']
  property_type varchar [note: 'The category of property being sponsored (e.g., sports team, entertainment event, festival, celebrity, influencer). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sports_team|sports_league|sports_event|entertainment_event|music_festival|cultural_event|celebrity|influencer|venue|broadcast_property|esports — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsorship contract includes an option for renewal at the end of the current term.']
  renewal_terms varchar [note: 'Description of the renewal terms, conditions, and pricing structure if renewal option exists.']
  roi_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target return on investment percentage for this sponsorship, used for performance evaluation and portfolio optimization.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this sponsorship record in the source operational system.']
  sponsorship_code varchar [note: 'Internal business identifier or code used to reference this sponsorship in financial and marketing systems.']
  sponsorship_name varchar [note: 'The official name or title of the sponsorship agreement as recognized in marketing materials and contracts.']
  sponsorship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sponsorship agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|negotiation|active|suspended|expired|terminated|renewed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sponsorship_type varchar
  start_date date
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the target consumer audience or demographic segment this sponsorship is intended to reach.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Description of contract termination conditions, notice periods, and penalties for early termination by either party.']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic territory or market scope covered by the sponsorship agreement, using three-letter ISO country codes or regional descriptors.']
  tier varchar [note: 'The level or tier of sponsorship (e.g., title sponsor, presenting sponsor, official sponsor, associate sponsor) indicating prominence and rights.']

  Note: 'Master record for brand sponsorship agreements with sports, entertainment, events, or cultural properties. Captures sponsorship name, property type (sports team, event, festival, celebrity), brand, sponsorship tier, contract value, activation rights, exclusivity terms, territory, contract start/end dates, renewal option, and performance obligations. Enables sponsorship portfolio management and ROI tracking.'
}

Table marketing.marketing_event {
  marketing_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing event record. Primary key.']
  agency_id varchar [note: 'FK to marketing.agency']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the event plan.']
  campaign_id varchar
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Events have sustainability plans tied to ESG commitments; FK supports event ESG reporting.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand sponsoring or owning this marketing event.']
  product_category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category featured in this event.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'FK to promotion.promotion_event when this marketing event is trade-linked; resolves SSOT duplicate']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail account or customer associated with retailer-focused events.']
  actual_attendance varchar [note: 'Actual number of attendees who participated in the event.']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual cost incurred for executing the event.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when the event concluded.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the event started or took place.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the event venue.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, floor, building) for the event venue.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event plan was approved for execution.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total planned budget allocated for the event.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the event venue is located.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the event takes place.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for budget and cost amounts.']
  end_date date
  event_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code for the event used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  event_context varchar [note: 'Marketing-specific event context']
  event_date date
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the event purpose, activities, and key highlights.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the marketing event (e.g., \'Spring Product Launch 2024\', \'Summer Sampling Tour\').']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the marketing event.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the marketing event by its primary purpose and format.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event record is currently active in the system.']
  lead_count varchar [note: 'Number of qualified leads or prospects generated from the event.']
  location varchar
  market_geography varchar [note: 'Geographic market or region this event targets (e.g., \'Northeast US\', \'Western Europe\').']
  marketing_event_status varchar
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this event record was last modified or updated.']
  nps_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Net Promoter Score collected from event attendees measuring likelihood to recommend.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Primary business objective or goal of the marketing event.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Overall assessment of the event outcome relative to objectives and targets.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled end date for the event.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled start date for the event.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the event venue location.']
  registration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether attendee registration was required for the event.']
  sample_units_distributed varchar [note: 'Total number of product sample units distributed during the event.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this event record in the source operational system.']
  start_date date
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the event venue is located.']
  target_attendance varchar [note: 'Planned or target number of attendees for the event.']
  venue_name varchar [note: 'Name of the physical or virtual venue where the event takes place.']
  venue_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the venue or location type for the event.']

  Note: 'Marketing event such as product launches, brand activations, sponsorships, and experiential marketing. SSOT for marketing event management. Distinct from promotion.promotion_event which is a trade promotion execution event.'
}

Table marketing.brand_assignment {
  brand_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the brand_assignment association']
  campaign_id varchar
  employee_id varchar
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the brand']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the trade account']
  allocation_percent decimal(5,2)
  allocation_percentage decimal(18,2)
  annual_volume_target decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual volume target for the brand at this account']
  approval_status varchar
  approved_by varchar
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date of brand assignment']
  assignment_end_date date
  assignment_reason varchar
  assignment_role varchar
  assignment_start_date date
  assignment_type varchar
  brand_assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the brand at the trade account']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Channel code for the brand assignment']
  created_timestamp timestamp
  distribution_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for distribution']
  distribution_tier varchar
  effective_date date
  effective_from varchar
  effective_until varchar
  end_date date
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates exclusive brand rights']
  expiry_date date
  is_active boolean
  is_primary boolean
  is_primary_brand boolean
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment was last updated']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date the brand was launched for the trade account']
  notes varchar
  primary_brand_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if this brand is the primary brand for the trade account\'s shelf planning']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking of brand']
  region_code varchar
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Revenue share percentage for this assignment']
  target_shelf_share_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target shelf share percentage for the brand at this account']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Territory code for assignment']
  updated_timestamp timestamp

  Note: 'This association captures the operational relationship between trade accounts and brands. It records when a brand is launched at a specific trade account, its current status, and whether it is the primary brand for shelf planning.. Existence Justification: Brands are allocated to trade accounts for shelf planning and promotional purposes. Each trade account can carry multiple brands, and each brand can be sold through multiple trade accounts. The assignment is actively managed and includes attributes such as launch date, status, and a primary‑brand flag.'
}

Table marketing.brand_distribution_allocation {

Existence Justification: Brands are actively allocated to supply network nodes with defined allocation percentages, primary flags, and effective date ranges. The planning team manages these allocations, and a single brand can be stocked at many nodes while a node can carry many brands.'
}

Existence Justification: A marketing campaign allocates portions of inventory at specific locations to support promotional activities. Each allocation is defined by a percentage and a time window, and the same inventory can be allocated to multiple campaigns over time, while a campaign can allocate to many stock positions.'

Existence Justification: In Consumer Goods, a marketing campaign often requires regulatory submissions to obtain claim approvals. A single campaign may depend on several submissions (e.g., different claims or regions) and a single submission can be used to support multiple campaigns.'

Table finance.cost_center {
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2) [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cost center. Primary key for the cost center master record in SAP S/4HANA CO module.']
  company_code_id varchar
  controlling_area_id varchar
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand budgeting process allocates cost‑center budgets to each brand for expense control and reporting.']
  primary_cost_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the manager accountable for this cost center\'s budget and performance. Used for approval workflows and variance analysis reporting.']
  profit_center_id decimal(18,2)
  activity_type_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this cost center performs activity-based costing. True if the center provides measurable activities (e.g., machine hours, labor hours) that are allocated to cost objects.']
  allocation_method decimal(18,2) [note: 'Method used for allocating costs from this cost center to other cost objects (products, profit centers). Critical for COGS calculation and overhead absorption.']
  budget_profile_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget control profile defining budget planning rules, tolerance limits, and approval workflows for this cost center. Determines budget availability check behavior.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Business area classification for cross-company code reporting. Represents a distinct line of business or product category (e.g., Personal Care, Home Care, Beauty) for consolidated financial analysis.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity company code in SAP S/4HANA FI module. Links cost center to the legal entity for statutory financial reporting and SOX compliance.']
  cost_center_category decimal(18,2) [note: 'Accounting classification indicating whether costs are direct (attributable to products), indirect (supporting operations), overhead (administrative), or capital (asset-related). Critical for COGS calculation and financial reporting.']
  cost_center_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Business identifier for the cost center. Alphanumeric code used across SAP S/4HANA FI/CO modules for financial accountability tracking. Typically follows organizational naming conventions (e.g., plant code + department code).']
  cost_center_description decimal(18,2) [note: 'Extended description providing additional context about the cost center\'s purpose, scope, and responsibilities within the organization.']
  cost_center_group decimal(18,2) [note: 'Logical grouping of cost centers for consolidated reporting and analysis. Enables flexible aggregation beyond standard hierarchy (e.g., all brand marketing centers, all R&D centers).']
  cost_center_name decimal(18,2) [note: 'Full descriptive name of the cost center. Human-readable label identifying the organizational unit (e.g., \'Brand Marketing - North America\', \'Manufacturing Plant 01 - Production Line A\').']
  cost_center_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center. Active centers accept postings; inactive centers are closed; blocked centers are temporarily suspended; planned centers are approved but not yet operational.']
  cost_center_type decimal(18,2) [note: 'Classification of the cost center by functional area. Determines the nature of costs tracked and reporting hierarchy. Used for COGS allocation and EBITDA segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: production|sales|marketing|distribution|administration|research_development|quality_assurance|procurement|finance|shared_services — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the cost center operates. Supports multi-country financial consolidation and regulatory reporting.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created the cost center master record. Audit trail for SOX compliance and data governance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cost center master record was created in the system. Audit trail for SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for cost center transactions. Defines the local currency for budget planning and actual cost recording.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Department or functional unit code within the organization. Provides granular classification for workforce planning and departmental cost analysis.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'Functional area classification for cost-of-sales accounting. Distinguishes between manufacturing, sales, marketing, R&D, and administrative functions for COGS and operating expense allocation.']
  hierarchy_area varchar [note: 'Standard hierarchy assignment for organizational reporting. Defines the cost center\'s position in the corporate hierarchy tree for roll-up reporting and budget consolidation.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified the cost center master record. Audit trail for change tracking and SOX compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cost center master record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking and SOX compliance.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Geographic location or site code for the cost center. Used for regional cost analysis and location-based reporting.']
  lock_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the cost center is locked for actual postings. Used during period-end close and organizational changes to prevent unintended transactions.']
  parent_cost_center_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Code of the parent cost center in the organizational hierarchy. Enables hierarchical roll-up of costs for management reporting and budget aggregation.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or distribution center code associated with this cost center. Links production and logistics cost centers to physical facilities for COGS allocation.']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the cost center master record. Incremented with each modification to support change history tracking and optimistic locking.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region classification (e.g., NOAM, EMEA, APAC, LATAM) for regional financial reporting and performance benchmarking.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source ERP system from which this cost center record originated. Supports multi-system landscapes and data lineage tracking.']
  statistical_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is a statistical cost center used only for informational reporting without actual cost postings. Used for tracking non-financial metrics.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the cost center is valid and can accept cost postings. Supports time-dependent organizational changes and fiscal year transitions.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date until which the cost center is valid. Nullable for open-ended cost centers. Used for organizational restructuring and cost center retirement.']

  Note: 'Master record for organizational cost centers used in SAP S/4HANA CO module. Defines the financial accountability units within the CPG enterprise — brand units, manufacturing plants, regional sales offices, and shared service centers. Each cost center carries a hierarchy path, responsible manager, controlling area, profit center assignment, currency, and active period. Serves as the foundational dimension for all cost accounting, COGS allocation, and EBITDA reporting.'
}

Table finance.profit_center {
  profit_center_id decimal(18,2) [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the profit center. Primary key for this entity representing an autonomous business segment within the consumer goods enterprise.']
  company_code_id varchar
  controlling_area_id varchar
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Profit Center Manager needed for profit‑center performance reports and accountability; finance tracks manager as employee.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Profitability reporting ties each brand to a profit center to calculate brand‑level P&L.']
  parent_profit_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reference to the parent profit center in a hierarchical profit center structure. Enables roll-up reporting and EBITDA decomposition across organizational levels.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the profit center\'s physical or administrative location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or department information. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'Brand identifier for brand-based profit centers. Enables brand-level P&L reporting and brand portfolio management.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Business area code representing a cross-company-code organizational unit for segment reporting. Enables consolidated financial statements by business segment.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Sales channel classification for channel-based profit centers: retail, wholesale, e-commerce, direct-to-consumer (DTC), foodservice, or export.']
  city varchar [note: 'City name for the profit center\'s location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity company code to which this profit center belongs. Used for statutory financial reporting and consolidation.']
  consolidation_unit_code varchar [note: 'Code of the consolidation unit for group reporting. Links profit center to the financial consolidation hierarchy for IFRS/GAAP reporting.']
  cost_center_group_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Code of the cost center group associated with this profit center. Links profit center accounting to cost center accounting for overhead allocation and COGS calculation.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the profit center\'s location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this profit center record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the profit center\'s local reporting currency. Used for revenue and cost tracking before consolidation.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'General email address for the profit center for business correspondence. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the profit center if applicable. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'Functional area code representing the business function (e.g., production, sales, administration) for cost-of-sales accounting and functional P&L reporting.']
  geographic_region_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or region code representing the primary geographic location of the profit center for regional segment reporting.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of this profit center within the organizational hierarchy. Level 1 represents top-level segments, with increasing numbers for deeper levels.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this profit center record. Audit trail for change accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this profit center record was last updated. Audit trail for record modifications.']
  lock_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the profit center is locked for transaction postings. Used during period-end close and audit processes.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the profit center. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  plan_version varchar
  planning_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this profit center participates in budgeting and financial planning processes.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the profit center\'s location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  product_category_code varchar [note: 'Product category or product line code associated with this profit center. Used for product-based segment reporting and portfolio analysis.']
  profit_center_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Business identifier code for the profit center used in financial reporting and segment analysis. Externally-known unique code aligned with SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA master data.']
  profit_center_description decimal(18,2) [note: 'Detailed textual description of the profit center\'s business scope, responsibilities, and strategic objectives.']
  profit_center_name decimal(18,2) [note: 'Full business name of the profit center representing the product line, brand, or geographic business unit.']
  profit_center_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the profit center indicating whether it is operational, temporarily suspended, or permanently closed.']
  profit_center_type decimal(18,2) [note: 'Classification of the profit center by organizational dimension: product line, brand, geographic region, sales channel, or business unit. Determines the segment reporting structure.']
  responsible_person_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the profit center manager for financial reporting notifications and escalations.']
  responsible_person_name varchar [note: 'Name of the business leader or manager accountable for the profit center\'s financial performance and operational results.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Segment code for external segment reporting per IFRS 8 and US GAAP ASC 280. Represents reportable operating segments for investor disclosures.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated name or acronym for the profit center used in reports and dashboards for space-constrained displays.']
  source_system_code varchar
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this profit center is subject to SOX financial controls and audit requirements for publicly traded companies.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'State or province code for the profit center\'s location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax jurisdiction code for the profit center\'s location. Used for tax reporting and compliance with local tax authorities.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Effective start date from which this profit center is active and available for transaction postings. Supports time-dependent organizational structures.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Effective end date after which this profit center is no longer active. Null value indicates an open-ended validity period.']
  valuation_view varchar [note: 'Valuation perspective for profit center accounting: legal (statutory), group (IFRS/GAAP consolidation), profit center (internal transfer pricing), or management (operational view).']

  Note: 'Master record for profit centers representing autonomous business segments within the consumer goods enterprise — product lines, brands, or geographic business units. Tracks revenue, cost, and profitability at a granular level below the legal entity. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA (Profit Center Accounting) and Oracle Cloud Financials for segment reporting and EBITDA decomposition.'
}

Table finance.gl_account {
  gl_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the general ledger account. Primary key for the GL account master data.']
  account_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: GL account ownership required for audit trails and SOX compliance; reports list responsible employee per account.']
  carbon_offset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_offset. Business justification: Accounting for carbon offset purchases uses dedicated GL accounts; linking enables accurate carbon credit financial reporting.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chart of accounts framework to which this account belongs. Links to the chart_of_accounts master data.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the company code (legal entity) to which this GL account belongs. Links to the company_code master data.']
  controlling_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the controlling area for cost accounting and management reporting. Links to the controlling_area master data.']
  account_category varchar
  account_group varchar [note: 'The account group classification that controls field status, number ranges, and posting rules in the ERP system.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'The full descriptive name of the GL account as it appears in financial reports and statements.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique account number in the chart of accounts. This is the business identifier used in financial postings and reporting.']
  account_short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated name or label for the GL account used in condensed reports and system displays.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the GL account: active (in use), inactive (not currently used but retained), pending approval (awaiting activation), or archived (historical only).']
  account_type varchar [note: 'The fundamental accounting classification of the account: asset, liability, equity, revenue, expense, or statistical (non-monetary).']
  alternative_account_number varchar
  balance_amount decimal(18,2)
  balance_sheet_account varchar
  balance_sheet_classification decimal(18,2) [note: 'For balance sheet accounts, indicates whether the account is a current asset, non-current asset, current liability, non-current liability, or equity. Not applicable for P&L accounts.']
  blocked_for_posting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is currently blocked for all postings. Used to prevent transactions to accounts that are under review, closed, or being restructured.']
  budget_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether budget availability checking is active for this account. When enabled, postings are validated against approved budgets.']
  cash_flow_category varchar [note: 'For accounts that impact cash flow, indicates the cash flow statement category: operating activities, investing activities, or financing activities. Not applicable for non-cash accounts.']
  consolidation_account_number varchar [note: 'The corresponding account number in the group consolidation chart of accounts. Used for mapping local accounts to group reporting structures.']
  cost_center_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a cost center assignment is mandatory when posting to this account. Used for internal cost allocation and management reporting.']
  cost_element_category decimal(18,2) [note: 'For accounts that are cost elements, indicates whether it is a primary cost element (direct posting from FI) or secondary cost element (internal allocation only). Not applicable if not a cost element.']
  cost_element_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is also defined as a cost element in the Controlling (CO) module for cost center and internal order postings.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person who created this GL account record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this GL account record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the account\'s local currency. For multi-currency accounts, this is the primary reporting currency.']
  debit_credit_indicator decimal(18,2) [note: 'Indicates whether the account normally carries a debit balance or credit balance in the general ledger.']
  deletion_flag boolean
  field_status_group varchar [note: 'The field status group that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed when posting to this account.']
  financial_statement_category varchar [note: 'The primary financial statement where this account appears: balance sheet, income statement (P&L), cash flow statement, retained earnings, or statistical.']
  financial_statement_line_item varchar [note: 'The specific line item or section within the financial statement where this account balance is reported (e.g., Current Assets, Cost of Goods Sold, Operating Expenses).']
  gl_account_description varchar
  gl_account_group varchar
  gl_account_name varchar
  gl_account_number varchar
  gl_account_status varchar
  gl_account_type varchar
  intercompany_clearing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is used for intercompany transactions that require clearing and elimination in consolidated financial statements.']
  interest_indicator varchar
  is_open_item_managed boolean
  is_reconciliation_account boolean
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person who last modified this GL account record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this GL account record was last modified.']
  line_item_display_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether individual line item details are stored and can be displayed for this account. Required for detailed transaction analysis and audit trails.']
  open_item_management_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account uses open item management for tracking and clearing individual line items (e.g., AR, AP, bank clearing accounts).']
  pl_account varchar
  pl_category varchar [note: 'For income statement accounts, indicates the P&L category: gross revenue, net revenue, COGS, gross margin, operating expense (SG&A, marketing, trade spend), EBITDA, EBIT, or net income. Not applicable for balance sheet accounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gross_revenue|net_revenue|cogs|gross_margin|operating_expense|ebitda|ebit|net_income|not_applicable — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  planning_level varchar [note: 'The organizational level at which budget planning and forecasting is performed for this account (e.g., company code, profit center, cost center).']
  posting_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether direct postings are allowed to this account. Some accounts (e.g., summary or control accounts) may only accept postings from automated processes.']
  posting_block_flag boolean
  profit_center_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a profit center assignment is mandatory when posting to this account. Used for segment reporting and profitability analysis.']
  reconciliation_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a reconciliation account (subledger control account) that summarizes detailed transactions from subsidiary ledgers such as AR, AP, or inventory.']
  reconciliation_account_type varchar
  retained_earnings_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the retained earnings account where net income/loss is transferred during year-end closing.']
  sort_key varchar [note: 'The default sort key used for organizing and displaying line items within this account (e.g., posting date, document number, amount).']
  source_system_code varchar
  sox_control_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is subject to SOX internal control requirements and enhanced audit procedures.']
  tax_category decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tax treatment category for this account (e.g., taxable revenue, non-taxable revenue, input tax, output tax, tax-exempt). Used for VAT, sales tax, and GST reporting.']
  tax_code_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tax codes can be entered when posting to this account. Typically true for revenue and expense accounts, false for balance sheet accounts.']
  tax_relevant_flag boolean
  tolerance_group varchar
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this GL account is valid and available for posting. Used for time-dependent account structures.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this GL account is valid. After this date, the account is no longer available for posting. Null indicates no end date.']

  Note: 'General ledger chart of accounts master for the consumer goods enterprise. Defines every account in the COA including P&L accounts (revenue, COGS, trade spend, marketing, SG&A), balance sheet accounts (inventory, AR, AP, fixed assets), and statistical accounts. Stores account type, financial statement category, tax category, reconciliation account flag, currency, and controlling relevance. Source of truth for all financial postings in SAP S/4HANA FI and Oracle Cloud Financials.'
}

Table finance.ledger {
  ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the financial ledger record. Primary key for the ledger entity.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.chart_of_accounts']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.company_code']
  controlling_area_id varchar
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Ledger stewardship is tracked for consolidation control and audit; responsible employee must be recorded.']
  accounting_principle varchar [note: 'The accounting standard or principle applied in this ledger, such as International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), United States Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (US GAAP), local country-specific GAAP, tax accounting rules, or management accounting conventions.']
  accounting_standard varchar
  audit_trail_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether comprehensive audit trail logging is enabled for all postings and changes to this ledger, supporting regulatory compliance and forensic analysis.']
  base_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the base currency in which this ledger maintains balances (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  consolidation_ledger_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger is used for group consolidation purposes, aggregating financial data from multiple legal entities for consolidated financial statements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp recording when this ledger record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for ledger lifecycle management.']
  currency_code varchar
  currency_type varchar [note: 'Defines the currency perspective maintained in this ledger. Local currency represents the operating entity\'s functional currency, group currency is the consolidated reporting currency, hard currency is a stable reference currency (e.g., USD, EUR), and index currency supports inflation-adjusted reporting.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this ledger ceases to be active and no longer accepts financial postings. Null for ledgers with indefinite validity.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this ledger becomes active and begins accepting financial postings. Supports temporal validity for ledger lifecycle management.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Code identifying the fiscal year calendar structure used by this ledger, defining the number of posting periods, special periods, and year-end closing rules. Common in SAP S/4HANA FI to support diverse fiscal calendars across global entities.']
  is_extension_ledger boolean
  is_leading_ledger boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the leading ledger (primary book of record) for the company code. Only one ledger per company code can be designated as the leading ledger.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this ledger record. Supports change tracking and audit trail for ledger configuration.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp recording when this ledger record was last modified. Enables change tracking and supports audit trail requirements for financial system configuration.']
  ledger_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the ledger within the enterprise. Used as the business identifier for the ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI and Oracle Cloud Financials.']
  ledger_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the ledger\'s purpose, scope, and usage within the enterprise financial reporting architecture. Provides context for finance users and auditors.']
  ledger_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the ledger for human-readable identification and reporting purposes.']
  ledger_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the ledger. Active ledgers accept postings, inactive ledgers are configured but not yet in use, closed ledgers are archived and no longer accept postings, and suspended ledgers are temporarily disabled.']
  ledger_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ledger indicating its role in the financial reporting architecture. Leading ledger is the primary book of record, parallel ledgers support alternative accounting principles (e.g., local GAAP), and extension ledgers provide management reporting views.']
  posting_period_variant varchar [note: 'Code defining the posting period structure for this ledger, including the number of regular periods (typically 12 monthly periods) and special periods for year-end adjustments and closing entries.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years that financial data in this ledger must be retained to comply with statutory, tax, and regulatory record-keeping requirements. Common retention periods range from 7 to 10 years for financial records.']
  source_system_code varchar
  sox_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger maintains controls and audit trails compliant with Sarbanes-Oxley Act Section 404 requirements for internal controls over financial reporting.']
  supports_cost_of_sales_accounting decimal(18,2) [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger supports cost of sales (COGS) accounting method, which matches costs directly to revenue in the period of sale, as opposed to period-based cost accounting.']
  supports_profit_center_accounting decimal(18,2) [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger maintains profit center accounting data for internal management reporting and profitability analysis by organizational unit.']
  supports_segment_reporting boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger supports segment reporting as required by IFRS 8 Operating Segments. Segment reporting enables financial analysis by business unit, product line, or geographic region.']
  underlying_ledger_code varchar
  valid_from_date date
  valid_to_date date
  valuation_approach varchar
  valuation_view varchar [note: 'Defines the valuation perspective applied in this ledger. Legal valuation follows statutory requirements, group valuation applies consolidated group policies, profit center valuation supports internal management reporting, and cost center valuation tracks cost allocation.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created this ledger record in the system. Supports audit trail and accountability for ledger configuration changes.']

  Note: 'Financial ledger master defining the accounting ledgers maintained in the enterprise — leading ledger (IFRS/GAAP), parallel ledgers for local GAAP, and extension ledgers for management reporting. Each ledger record captures ledger type, accounting principle, currency type, fiscal year variant, and posting period variant. Supports multi-GAAP reporting requirements common in global CPG companies operating across multiple regulatory jurisdictions.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the journal entry record. Primary key for the journal entry header.']
  company_code_id varchar
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Identifier of the cost center to which costs or revenues from this journal entry are allocated. Used for management accounting and EBITDA reporting.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Journal entries reference GL accounts by code; adding gl_account_id FK normalizes the relationship and removes the redundant code column.']
  journal_approved_by_employee_id varchar
  journal_created_by_employee_id varchar
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Journal entries belong to a specific ledger; adding ledger_id FK links each entry to its ledger without removing existing ledger_group (kept for reporting).']
  primary_journal_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who posted or created this journal entry. Critical for SOX compliance and audit trail. Confidential business information.']
  profit_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Identifier of the profit center for segment reporting and profitability analysis. Used for EBITDA calculation by business unit.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: R&D expense tracking mandates associating journal entries with the originating RD project for cost accounting and project profitability reporting.']
  reversed_journal_entry_id varchar
  approval_status varchar
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the journal entry was approved. Part of the SOX-compliant audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp
  baseline_payment_date date [note: 'The baseline date used for calculating payment terms and due dates for vendor invoices and customer receivables. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  batch_input_session varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch input session if this journal entry was created through automated batch processing. Used for tracking mass data uploads and interface postings.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the business area or division for cross-company code reporting. Used for consolidated financial statements.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'The date on which this open item was cleared. Used for DSO and DIO calculation. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the clearing document if this open item has been cleared (paid or offset). Used for accounts payable and receivable reconciliation.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Four-character alphanumeric code identifying the legal entity or business unit within the enterprise. Represents the smallest organizational unit for which a complete self-contained set of accounts can be drawn up for purposes of external reporting.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Organizational unit in management accounting that represents a closed system for cost accounting. Used for internal management reporting.']
  created_by_user varchar
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this journal entry record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the document currency in which the journal entry amounts are denominated. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date printed on the source document (invoice, receipt, payment voucher). May differ from posting date. Used for document control and audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to the accounting document within the company code and fiscal year. This is the externally visible business key for the journal entry.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification code indicating the nature and source of the journal entry. Examples: SA (General Ledger Document), AB (Accounting Document), KR (Vendor Invoice), DR (Customer Invoice), DZ (Customer Payment), KA (Vendor Payment), KG (Vendor Credit Memo), KN (Customer Credit Memo), KZ (Payment Request), RE (Invoice Receipt), RV (Invoice Reversal), WA (Goods Issue), WE (Goods Receipt), ZP (Payment Posting). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SA|AB|KR|DR|DZ|KA|KG|KN|KZ|RE|RV|WA|WE|ZP — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  entry_date date [note: 'The date on which the journal entry was created or entered into the system. Used for audit trail and process monitoring. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  exchange_rate decimal(15,5) [note: 'The exchange rate used to convert document currency amounts to local currency. Expressed as local currency per one unit of document currency. Null if document currency equals local currency.']
  exchange_rate_type decimal(18,6) [note: 'Code indicating the type of exchange rate used for currency translation. Examples: M (Average Rate), B (Bank Buying Rate), G (Bank Selling Rate), P (Posting Date Rate).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Numeric period within the fiscal year (typically 1-12 for monthly periods, or 1-13 for year-end adjustments). Determines the reporting period for financial statements.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit year representing the fiscal year to which this journal entry is assigned. Used for period-based financial reporting and year-end closing.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'Code classifying the journal entry by functional area (e.g., Production, Sales, Administration, R&D). Used for cost of goods sold (COGS) allocation and functional expense reporting.']
  header_text varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional information or explanation about the journal entry. Used for documentation and audit purposes.']
  intercompany_flag boolean
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry involves intercompany transactions between different company codes within the enterprise. True if intercompany, False otherwise.']
  journal_entry_number varchar
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp
  ledger_group varchar [note: 'Two-character code identifying the ledger or accounting principle under which this entry is recorded. Examples: 0L (Leading Ledger - IFRS), 2L (Local GAAP), 3L (Management Reporting). Supports parallel accounting for multiple reporting standards.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the company code local currency. All amounts are translated to this currency for statutory reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this journal entry record was last modified. Part of the audit trail for tracking changes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  payment_terms_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Code representing the payment terms applicable to this document (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30). Used for cash flow forecasting and DSO tracking.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger. This is the effective date for financial reporting and determines the fiscal period assignment. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the journal entry. Values: draft (entry created but not posted), posted (entry successfully posted to GL), parked (entry saved for later posting), cancelled (entry voided before posting), reversed (entry reversed by a subsequent document).']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference number from the source document (invoice number, purchase order number, payment reference). Used for cross-referencing and reconciliation.']
  reversal_date date
  reversal_flag boolean
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a previous entry. True if this is a reversal document, False otherwise.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for reversing the original document. Examples: 01 (Reversal in Current Period), 02 (Reversal in Next Period), 03 (Correction), 04 (Accrual Reversal), 05 (Error Correction), 06 (Period-End Adjustment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|02|03|04|05|06|07|08|09 — promote to reference product]']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the original journal entry that is being reversed by this entry. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this journal entry originated. Examples: SAP_FI (SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting), SAP_CO (SAP Controlling), ORACLE_GL (Oracle Cloud General Ledger), ORACLE_AP (Oracle Accounts Payable), ORACLE_AR (Oracle Accounts Receivable), SFDC (Salesforce), WMS (Warehouse Management System), MES (Manufacturing Execution System). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAP_FI|SAP_CO|ORACLE_GL|ORACLE_AP|ORACLE_AR|SFDC|WMS|MES — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is subject to SOX internal control testing and audit procedures. True if SOX-controlled, False otherwise.']
  tax_reporting_date date [note: 'The date relevant for tax reporting purposes. May differ from posting date based on tax jurisdiction rules. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  total_credit_amount decimal(18,2)
  total_debit_amount decimal(18,2)
  trading_partner_code varchar
  trading_partner_company_code varchar [note: 'Company code of the intercompany trading partner if this is an intercompany transaction. Used for consolidation and elimination entries. Null if not an intercompany transaction.']
  transaction_code varchar [note: 'SAP transaction code or Oracle program identifier used to create this journal entry. Examples: FB01 (Post Document), FB50 (G/L Account Posting), MIRO (Enter Invoice), F-28 (Post Incoming Payments). Used for audit trail and process analysis.']

  Note: 'Core general ledger journal entry header capturing every financial posting in the consumer goods enterprise. Records document type (vendor invoice, customer payment, goods issue, payroll posting, accrual, reversal), posting date, fiscal period, company code, ledger, currency, exchange rate, reference document, and SOX-compliant posting user and approval chain. The authoritative transactional record for all financial activity sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI and Oracle Cloud Financials.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry_line {
  journal_entry_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the journal entry line item. Primary key for granular cost allocation, COGS decomposition, and SOX-compliant audit trail tracing at the line level.']
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2)
  cost_object_cost_center_id decimal(18,2)
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Journal entry lines store GL account code; FK to gl_account enables proper account lookup and eliminates duplicate code.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent journal entry header document. Links this line to the overall journal entry batch and posting context.']
  partner_cost_center_id varchar
  partner_profit_center_id varchar
  profit_center_id decimal(18,2)
  amount decimal(18,2)
  amount_company_code_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount converted to the local currency of the company code. Used for statutory reporting and local GAAP compliance.']
  amount_group_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount converted to the group reporting currency. Enables consolidated financial reporting across all subsidiaries.']
  amount_local_currency varchar
  amount_transaction_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of this line item in the original transaction currency. Preserves the original posting amount before currency conversion.']
  assignment_field varchar [note: 'Free-text assignment field for additional reference information such as invoice number, purchase order, or payment reference. Enhances traceability.']
  assignment_reference varchar
  baseline_date date
  baseline_payment_date date [note: 'Baseline date for payment terms calculation. Used to compute due dates and cash discount periods.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Business area classification for cross-company code segment reporting. Enables consolidated financial statements by business segment.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date on which this open item was cleared or settled. Null if the item remains open.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the clearing entry that settled this open item. Null if the item remains open.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity identifier representing the company or subsidiary for which this line is posted. Enables consolidated and statutory reporting.']
  company_code_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the company code local currency. Typically the statutory reporting currency of the legal entity.']
  cost_object_type varchar
  created_timestamp timestamp
  currency_code varchar
  customer_account_number varchar [note: 'Customer account number for revenue and receivables postings. Links financial transactions to customer master data.']
  debit_credit_indicator decimal(18,2) [note: 'Indicates whether this line is a debit (D) or credit (C) posting. Fundamental to double-entry bookkeeping and account balance calculation.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is due for payables or expected for receivables. Critical for DSO and cash flow management.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert transaction currency to company code or group currency. Critical for currency translation audit trail.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'Functional area classification (e.g., production, sales, administration, R&D). Supports cost-of-sales accounting and functional expense reporting.']
  group_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the group reporting currency. Typically USD or EUR for multinational consumer goods companies.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'Internal order for tracking costs of short-term jobs, events, or overhead activities. Enables job costing and overhead allocation.']
  item_text varchar
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp
  line_item_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the journal entry header. Determines the ordering and position of this line in the document.']
  line_number varchar
  line_text varchar
  local_amount decimal(18,2)
  local_currency_code varchar
  material_number varchar [note: 'Material or SKU associated with this line item for inventory and COGS postings. Links financial postings to product master data.']
  open_item_flag boolean
  payment_terms varchar
  payment_terms_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Payment terms applicable to this line item. Determines due date calculation and cash discount eligibility.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or distribution center associated with this line item. Supports plant-level cost accounting and inventory management.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'Two-digit posting key controlling account type, debit/credit indicator, and field status. Fundamental to SAP FI posting logic.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material or units associated with this line item. Enables unit cost calculation and inventory valuation.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External or internal document number referenced by this line (e.g., invoice, purchase order, contract). Supports document linkage and reconciliation.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this line item is a reversal of a previous posting. True if this is a reversal entry.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the original entry that this line reverses. Null if this is not a reversal.']
  segment_code varchar
  source_system_code varchar
  special_gl_indicator varchar [note: 'Special GL indicator for down payments, bills of exchange, guarantees, or other special transactions. Enables special ledger processing.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Storage location within the plant for inventory-related postings. Enables warehouse-level inventory tracking and valuation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount calculated for this line item in transaction currency. Supports VAT, sales tax, and other indirect tax reporting.']
  tax_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax code determining the tax treatment of this line item. Drives automatic tax calculation and tax reporting compliance.']
  text_description varchar [note: 'Descriptive text explaining the nature or purpose of this journal entry line. Provides human-readable context for audit and review.']
  trading_partner_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the intercompany trading partner for intercompany transactions. Enables intercompany reconciliation and elimination entries.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction amount. Identifies the currency in which the original transaction was denominated.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity field (e.g., EA, KG, LT). Standardizes quantity representation across transactions.']
  value_date date [note: 'Value date for cash management and bank reconciliation. Represents the effective date for interest calculation and cash position.']
  vendor_account_number varchar [note: 'Vendor account number for procurement and payables postings. Links financial transactions to vendor master data.']
  wbs_element_code varchar [note: 'WBS element for project-related postings. Links costs and revenues to specific projects, phases, or deliverables in project accounting.']

  Note: 'Individual line items of a general ledger journal entry. Each line captures the GL account, cost center, profit center, WBS element, business area, debit/credit indicator, amount in transaction currency, amount in company code currency, amount in group currency, tax code, and assignment field. Enables granular cost allocation, COGS decomposition, and audit trail tracing at the line level as required by SOX compliance.'
}

Table finance.ar_invoice {
  ar_invoice_id decimal(18,2) [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts receivable invoice record. Primary key for the AR invoice entity.']
  bank_account_id varchar
  company_code_id varchar
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AR invoices reference GL accounts by code; adding FK normalizes accounting data.']
  journal_entry_id varchar
  revenue_contract_id decimal(18,2)
  sales_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.invoice. Business justification: AR invoice reconciliation process matches each AR invoice to the originating sales invoice for cash application.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade account (customer) to whom this invoice was issued. Links to the customer master record.']
  amount_received decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount received from the customer against this invoice. May be less than total amount due to partial payments or deductions. Null for unpaid invoices.']
  billing_address varchar
  billing_date date [note: 'Date when the invoice was created and issued to the customer. Principal business event timestamp for the invoice lifecycle.']
  billing_document_type varchar [note: 'SAP billing document type code indicating the nature of the invoice (e.g., F2=standard invoice, G2=credit memo, L2=debit memo). Determines accounting treatment and revenue recognition.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date when the invoice was cleared in the financial system, either through full payment or write-off. Null for open invoices.']
  clearing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned when the invoice is cleared in the financial system. Links to the clearing document for audit trail. Null for open invoices.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity that issued the invoice. Required for multi-entity financial consolidation and statutory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the AR system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Critical for multi-currency reporting and foreign exchange management.']
  deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of deductions taken by the customer against this invoice. Includes short-payments, chargebacks, and unauthorized deductions requiring resolution.']
  deduction_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for customer deductions (e.g., pricing dispute, damaged goods, short shipment, promotional allowance). Used for deduction root cause analysis.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2)
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is currently under dispute by the customer. True if disputed, false otherwise. Disputed invoices are excluded from standard collection processes.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason the customer is disputing the invoice. Used for dispute resolution and root cause analysis.']
  dispute_reason_code varchar
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the goods were sold (e.g., retail, wholesale, DTC, e-commerce). Used for channel profitability analysis.']
  dso_aging_bucket varchar [note: 'Aging category based on number of days the invoice has been outstanding past the due date. Used for DSO reporting, collections prioritization, and bad debt provisioning.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is expected from the customer based on agreed payment terms. Used for Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculation and dunning.']
  dunning_date date
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current dunning level indicating the escalation stage of collection efforts (0=no dunning, 1=first reminder, 2=second reminder, etc.). Higher levels indicate more aggressive collection actions.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the invoice was posted. Used for monthly financial reporting and trend analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the invoice was posted. Used for year-over-year financial analysis and annual reporting.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Date when the invoice was posted to the general ledger. Used for financial period assignment and month-end close processes.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoice amount before any trade discounts, deductions, or adjustments. Represents the full billed value of goods or services.']
  invoice_date date
  invoice_number decimal(18,2) [note: 'Externally-known unique invoice number assigned by the billing system. Used for customer communication and payment reference.']
  invoice_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the accounts receivable process. Tracks progression from open through payment to clearing or write-off. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|partially_paid|paid|cleared|written_off|disputed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar
  last_dunning_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent dunning notice was sent to the customer. Used to track collection activity frequency and determine next dunning action.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified the invoice record. Required for SOX compliance and segregation of duties controls.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the invoice record was last modified. Used for change tracking and SOX compliance audit trails.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Invoice amount after trade discounts but before tax. This is the base amount subject to taxation and the primary value for revenue recognition.']
  outstanding_amount decimal(18,2)
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on the invoice. Calculated as total amount minus amount received. Zero when invoice is fully paid or cleared.']
  paid_amount decimal(18,2)
  payment_date date [note: 'Date when payment was received from the customer. Null for unpaid invoices. Used for DSO calculation and cash flow analysis.']
  payment_method decimal(18,2) [note: 'Method by which the customer is expected to remit payment. Used for cash application automation and payment reconciliation.']
  payment_terms varchar
  payment_terms_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Code representing the payment terms agreed with the customer (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Determines due date calculation and early payment discount eligibility.']
  posting_date date
  reference_document_number varchar
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date when revenue from this invoice is recognized in the financial statements under ASC 606/IFRS 15. May differ from billing date based on performance obligation fulfillment.']
  sales_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating sales order that generated this invoice. Links invoice to order fulfillment and enables order-to-cash traceability.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'SAP sales organization responsible for the sale. Used for sales performance reporting and commission calculation.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment or delivery document associated with this invoice. Links billing to physical goods movement for OTIF tracking.']
  source_system_code varchar
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount charged on the invoice, including sales tax, VAT, GST, or other applicable indirect taxes based on jurisdiction.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final invoice amount including all discounts and taxes. This is the total amount due from the customer and the target for cash application.']
  trade_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total trade discounts applied to the invoice, including volume discounts, promotional allowances, and negotiated rebates. Reduces gross amount to net amount.']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount written off as uncollectible bad debt. Null for invoices not written off. Impacts bad debt expense and allowance for doubtful accounts.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'Date when the invoice or portion thereof was written off as uncollectible. Null for invoices not written off.']
  write_off_flag boolean
  write_off_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for write-off (e.g., customer bankruptcy, uncollectible, small balance write-off). Used for bad debt analysis and credit policy refinement.']

  Note: 'Accounts receivable master record representing the full trade receivable lifecycle for consumer goods sales to retailers, distributors, and wholesalers. Owns invoice creation, payment terms, cash receipt application, partial payments, deductions/short-payments, clearing, and write-offs. Captures invoice number, billing date, due date, payment terms, gross amount, trade discounts, net amount, tax, currency, payment method, payment date, amount received, clearing reference, DSO aging bucket, dunning level, and deduction reason codes. The single source of truth for all receivable balances, cash application, deduction management, DSO tracking, cash flow forecasting, and revenue recognition under ASC 606/IFRS 15. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA SD billing and FI-AR.'
}

Table finance.ar_payment {
  ar_payment_id decimal(18,2) [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts receivable payment record. Primary key.']
  ar_invoice_id decimal(18,2)
  bank_account_id varchar
  company_code_id varchar
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AR payments store GL account code; FK to gl_account consolidates GL reference.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the AR invoice being paid. Links payment to the originating billing document.']
  payment_run_id decimal(18,2)
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade customer making the payment. Identifies the payer account.']
