  created_timestamp timestamp
  currency_code varchar
  ebitda_category varchar [note: 'EBITDA bridge category classification for this budget line (revenue, COGS, operating expense, depreciation, amortization, non-operating). Supports EBITDA waterfall and bridge analysis.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate used to convert the local currency amount to the group currency amount. Supports currency translation audit trail.']
  finance_budget_status varchar
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year for which this budget applies. Enables period-level budget tracking.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which this budget is planned. Supports multi-year budget planning and year-over-year analysis.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'Functional area classification code (e.g., sales, manufacturing, administration) for functional cost analysis and reporting.']
  group_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the group reporting currency in which the planned amount is converted.']
  ibp_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this budget line is integrated with the Integrated Business Planning (IBP) or Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) process.']
  justification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing business justification, rationale, or assumptions underlying this budget line. Supports zero-based budgeting (ZBB) and budget review processes.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the individual who last modified this budget record. Supports change tracking and audit trail.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this budget record was last modified. Provides audit trail for budget changes and revisions.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local currency in which the planned amount is denominated.']
  lock_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this budget line is locked for editing. Supports budget freeze and period-end close controls.']
  marketing_budget_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'FK to marketing budget']
  methodology varchar [note: 'Budgeting methodology applied to this budget line (incremental, zero-based, activity-based, driver-based, rolling). Supports methodological transparency and audit.']
  planned_amount_group decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned budget amount converted to the group reporting currency. Used for consolidated financial reporting and cross-entity analysis.']
  planned_amount_local decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned budget amount in the local currency of the company code. Primary budget value for local financial planning and reporting.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Planned quantity or volume associated with this budget line (e.g., units produced, headcount, hours). Used for activity-based costing and volume variance analysis.']
  product_line_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the product line or product category to which this budget is allocated. Supports product-level profitability analysis.']
  profile_code varchar [note: 'Budget profile or template code defining the budget structure, rules, and allocation logic applied to this budget line.']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this budget record. Incremented with each modification to support optimistic locking and change history.']
  revised_budget_amount decimal(18,2)
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this budget record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA CO, Oracle Cloud Financials). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  ssot_note varchar [note: 'Authoritative financial budget; marketing_budget references this']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date from which this budget record is valid and effective. Supports time-dependent budget versioning and historical tracking.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'End date until which this budget record is valid and effective. Null indicates an open-ended validity period.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2)
  variance_percent decimal(10,4)
  variance_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage threshold for budget variance alerting and exception reporting. Used to trigger management review when actual spending deviates from budget.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the budget record indicating whether it is the original budget, a revised budget, latest estimate, forecast, or rolling budget.']

  Note: 'Enterprise financial budget at cost center/profit center level for corporate planning and control. SSOT for corporate financial budgets. Distinct from marketing.marketing_budget which is the marketing-specific spend plan.'
}

Table finance.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id decimal(18,2) [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fixed asset record. Primary key for the fixed asset master data. Inferred role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  company_code_id varchar
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fixed assets are assigned to a cost center for expense tracking; the existing cost_center_code string is redundant once the FK is added.']
  environmental_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.environmental_permit. Business justification: Asset compliance requires linking each fixed asset to its environmental permit for regulatory reporting.']
  gl_account_id varchar
  profit_center_id decimal(18,2)
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or manager responsible for the asset. Used for accountability, maintenance coordination, and asset custody tracking.']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense recognized to date since the asset was placed in service. Represents the cumulative reduction in asset value over time. Used to calculate net book value.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original purchase price or historical cost of the asset at acquisition, including all costs necessary to bring the asset to working condition (purchase price, freight, installation, testing). Basis for depreciation calculation.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was acquired or purchased by the enterprise. Used as the starting point for depreciation calculation and asset lifecycle tracking.']
  asset_category varchar
  asset_class varchar
  asset_class_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Classification code that categorizes the asset by type (e.g., machinery, buildings, vehicles, IT equipment). Determines depreciation rules, useful life, and accounting treatment. Corresponds to SAP Asset Class or Oracle Asset Category.']
  asset_description decimal(18,2) [note: 'Detailed textual description of the fixed asset, including make, model, specifications, and distinguishing characteristics. Used for identification and reporting purposes.']
  asset_group_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Grouping code for reporting and analysis purposes. Used to aggregate assets by category, function, or business unit for management reporting.']
  asset_number decimal(18,2) [note: 'Externally-known unique asset identifier assigned by the enterprise. Business identifier used for asset tracking, reporting, and audit trails. Corresponds to SAP Asset Number or Oracle Asset Number.']
  asset_serial_number decimal(18,2) [note: 'Manufacturer serial number or unique identifier assigned by the vendor. Used for warranty tracking, maintenance, and physical verification.']
  asset_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fixed asset. Indicates whether the asset is operational, under construction, retired, or disposed. Controls depreciation posting and asset transactions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|in_service|under_construction|retired|disposed|impaired|transferred|inactive — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  asset_sub_number varchar
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Business area or division to which the asset belongs. Used for cross-company code reporting and segment analysis.']
  capex_project_code varchar [note: 'Capital project or work breakdown structure (WBS) element under which the asset was acquired. Used for capital budget tracking and project cost reconciliation.']
  capitalization_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was capitalized and placed in service. Depreciation begins from this date. May differ from acquisition date for assets under construction.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity or company code that owns the asset. Used for financial consolidation and legal reporting. Corresponds to SAP Company Code or Oracle Legal Entity.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created the fixed asset record in the system. Used for audit trail and data governance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fixed asset record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts (acquisition cost, depreciation, net book value). Typically the local currency of the company code.']
  decommission_date date
  depreciation_area_code varchar [note: 'Depreciation area or book (e.g., book depreciation, tax depreciation, IFRS depreciation). Allows parallel valuation for different reporting purposes. Corresponds to SAP Depreciation Area.']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation posting ends for the asset. Calculated based on useful life or set manually when the asset is fully depreciated, retired, or disposed.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to allocate the cost of the asset over its useful life. Common methods include straight-line, declining balance, and units of production. Determines the depreciation expense pattern.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation posting begins for the asset. Typically the capitalization date or the first day of the following month, depending on enterprise policy.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was disposed, sold, scrapped, or retired. Marks the end of the asset lifecycle. Used to calculate gain or loss on disposal.']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount received from the sale or disposal of the asset. Used to calculate gain or loss on disposal (proceeds minus net book value at disposal).']
  disposal_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for disposing of the asset. Used for asset lifecycle analysis, capital planning, and audit trails. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sale|scrap|obsolete|damaged|transfer|donation|end_of_life — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  impairment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative impairment loss recognized on the asset when its recoverable amount falls below its carrying value. Required under IAS 36 Impairment of Assets. Reduces net book value.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified the fixed asset record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fixed asset record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_physical_inventory_date date
  last_revaluation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent asset revaluation. Revaluations adjust the carrying value to fair market value under IAS 16 revaluation model. Used for compliance and financial reporting.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Specific location or sub-location within the plant where the asset is installed or stored. Enables granular asset tracking and physical inventory reconciliation.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer or vendor who produced the asset. Used for warranty claims, spare parts sourcing, and vendor performance tracking.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model or part number of the asset. Used for technical specifications, spare parts identification, and maintenance planning.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current carrying value of the asset on the balance sheet, calculated as acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation. Represents the remaining capitalized value of the asset.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant, warehouse, or facility where the asset is physically located. Used for operational tracking and maintenance planning. Corresponds to SAP Plant or Oracle Inventory Organization.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number used to acquire the asset. Links the asset to procurement records for audit trail and vendor reconciliation.']
  revaluation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the most recent revaluation adjustment. Positive values indicate upward revaluation; negative values indicate impairment. Posted to revaluation reserve or income statement per IAS 16.']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life. The amount expected to be recovered through sale or disposal. Depreciation is calculated on acquisition cost minus salvage value.']
  serial_number varchar
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which the fixed asset record originated (e.g., SAP, Oracle, legacy system). Used for data lineage and integration reconciliation.']
  useful_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected economic useful life of the asset in years, as determined by asset class and enterprise policy. Used to calculate annual depreciation expense. May be fractional (e.g., 7.5 years).']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or supplier from whom the asset was purchased. Used for vendor performance tracking and procurement analytics.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer warranty expires. Used to determine whether repairs are covered under warranty or require capital expenditure.']

  Note: 'Fixed asset master and lifecycle record for capital assets owned by the consumer goods enterprise — manufacturing equipment, production lines, warehouse automation, vehicles, buildings, and IT infrastructure. Covers the full asset lifecycle: acquisition, capitalization, depreciation, transfer, revaluation, impairment, and disposal. Captures asset class, description, acquisition date/cost, useful life, depreciation method, accumulated depreciation, net book value, plant/location, responsible cost center, and all transaction events (transaction type, posting date, amount, document reference, originating business process). Supports capex tracking, depreciation run reconciliation, asset lifecycle audit trail, and SOX-compliant fixed asset management. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AA and Oracle Cloud Fixed Assets.'
}

Table finance.intercompany_transaction {
  intercompany_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the intercompany transaction record. Primary key for this entity.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Creator of intercompany transaction needed for transfer‑pricing documentation and audit traceability.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Intercompany transactions record GL account code; FK to gl_account creates a proper accounting relationship.']
  intercompany_elimination_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the consolidation elimination journal entry that reversed this intercompany transaction in the group financial statements.']
  intercompany_journal_entry_id varchar
  netting_run_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'The unique identifier of the netting run in which this intercompany transaction was processed, if applicable.']
  receiving_company_code_id varchar
  sending_company_code_id varchar
  arms_length_validated_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether the transfer price has been validated as compliant with arms-length pricing principles under OECD transfer pricing guidelines.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'The business area code representing the line of business or product category associated with this intercompany transaction.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'The date on which the intercompany receivable or payable was cleared through payment or offsetting entry.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the payment or clearing entry that settled this intercompany transaction.']
  cost_center_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost center code associated with this intercompany transaction for management accounting and cost allocation purposes.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person who created this intercompany transaction record in the ERP system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this intercompany transaction record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar
  document_date date [note: 'The date on the source document (invoice, agreement, or internal charge memo) that originated this intercompany transaction.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which the intercompany receivable or payable arising from this transaction is expected to be settled.']
  elimination_date date
  elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this intercompany transaction has been eliminated in the consolidated group financial statements.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency amount to the group currency amount.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which the intercompany transaction was posted.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the intercompany transaction was posted, derived from the posting date and the company\'s fiscal calendar.']
  group_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The transaction amount converted to the corporate group\'s reporting currency for consolidated financial statements.']
  group_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the corporate group\'s reporting currency (typically USD, EUR, or other primary currency).']
  intercompany_transaction_description varchar
  intercompany_transaction_status varchar
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person who last modified this intercompany transaction record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this intercompany transaction record was last modified.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2)
  local_currency_code varchar
  matching_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this intercompany transaction has been successfully matched with the corresponding entry in the counterparty entity\'s records during the intercompany reconciliation process.']
  netting_eligible_flag boolean
  netting_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Indicates whether this intercompany transaction has been included in an intercompany netting process to reduce cash movements between entities.']
  payment_terms decimal(18,2) [note: 'The payment terms code defining the due date calculation and settlement conditions for this intercompany transaction.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the intercompany transaction was posted to the general ledger. This is the principal business event timestamp for financial reporting and period assignment.']
  profit_center_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'The profit center code associated with this intercompany transaction for internal profitability analysis.']
  receiving_company_code varchar [note: 'The company code of the legal entity receiving the intercompany transaction. Represents the crediting entity in the intercompany posting.']
  reconciliation_reference varchar [note: 'The unique reference number linking this intercompany transaction to its corresponding entry in the counterparty entity\'s books for reconciliation purposes.']
  reconciliation_status varchar
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'The external reference number from the source document (invoice, purchase order, service agreement) that originated this intercompany transaction.']
  sending_company_code varchar [note: 'The company code of the legal entity initiating or sending the intercompany transaction. Represents the debiting entity in the intercompany posting.']
  settlement_status varchar
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the source ERP system or financial application from which this intercompany transaction was extracted (e.g., SAP_S4, ORACLE_ERP).']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this intercompany transaction is subject to SOX internal control testing and audit procedures.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tax jurisdiction code applicable to this intercompany transaction for VAT, GST, or other indirect tax purposes.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross monetary value of the intercompany transaction in the transaction currency, before any adjustments or eliminations.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the intercompany transaction was originally denominated.']
  transaction_date date
  transaction_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context and business rationale for this intercompany transaction.']
  transaction_document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique document number assigned to this intercompany transaction in the ERP system. Used for cross-reference and audit trail.']
  transaction_number varchar
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the intercompany transaction in the financial close and consolidation process.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the intercompany transaction indicating the nature of the cross-entity posting: goods transfer (finished goods sales), service charge (shared services allocation), management fee, royalty payment, intercompany loan, or dividend distribution.']
  transfer_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The price charged for goods or services in this intercompany transaction, established according to the company\'s transfer pricing policy.']
  transfer_pricing_method varchar [note: 'The transfer pricing methodology applied to determine the arms-length price: Comparable Uncontrolled Price (CUP), Resale Price Method (RPM), Cost Plus Method (CPM), Transactional Net Margin Method (TNMM), Profit Split Method (PSM), or other.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary difference between this intercompany transaction and its matched counterparty entry, if any discrepancy exists during reconciliation.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of withholding tax deducted from this intercompany transaction, if applicable under local tax regulations.']

  Note: 'Intercompany financial transaction record capturing cross-entity postings between legal entities within the consumer goods corporate group. Covers intercompany sales of finished goods, cost allocations, management fee charges, royalty payments, intercompany loans, and dividend distributions. Stores sending company code, receiving company code, transaction type, posting date, amount, currency, transfer price, arm\'s-length validation flag, netting status, elimination flag, reconciliation reference, and matching status. Critical for consolidated financial reporting, transfer pricing compliance, intercompany elimination in group close, and regulatory documentation under OECD transfer pricing guidelines.'
}

Table finance.finance_accrual {
  finance_accrual_id decimal(18,2) [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the financial accrual record. Primary key for the accrual entity.']
  company_code_id varchar
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Finance accruals are posted against a cost center; the string code column becomes unnecessary after the FK is introduced.']
  gl_account_id varchar
  journal_entry_id varchar
  owner_employee_id varchar
  primary_finance_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the finance manager or controller who approved the accrual entry. Required for SOX compliance and segregation of duties.']
  profit_center_id decimal(18,2)
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Vendor or supplier identifier if the accrual relates to a specific vendor liability (e.g., rebate accrual, freight accrual). Null for internal accruals.']
  tertiary_finance_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified the accrual entry. Tracks changes for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Customer or trade account identifier if the accrual relates to customer-specific trade spend or rebate. Null for non-customer accruals.']
  accrual_amount decimal(18,2)
  accrual_category decimal(18,2) [note: 'High-level accounting category of the accrual entry. Determines whether this is an expense accrual, liability accrual, revenue accrual, or prepaid asset.']
  accrual_date date
  accrual_description varchar
  accrual_number varchar
  accrual_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accrual entry. Tracks the approval and posting workflow state for SOX compliance and audit trail.']
  accrual_type decimal(18,2) [note: 'Classification of the accrual by business purpose. Determines the nature of the expense or liability being recognized.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the accrual in the base currency. Represents the estimated expense or liability being recognized in the current accounting period.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the accrual entry. Tracks whether the accrual has been reviewed and approved by authorized finance personnel.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the accrual was approved. Part of the audit trail for period-end close activities.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the business rationale, assumptions, and any special circumstances related to the accrual. Supports external audit review.']
  auto_reversal_flag boolean
  basis varchar [note: 'Method by which the accrual was calculated and recorded. Indicates whether the accrual was manually entered by finance staff or automatically generated by system rules.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Business area or division code for cross-company segment reporting. Enables consolidated financial statements by business line.']
  business_justification varchar
  calculation_method_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of how the accrual amount was calculated. Supports audit review and ensures transparency in accrual estimation methodology.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity company code for which the accrual is recorded. Supports multi-entity consolidation and statutory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the accrual record was first created in the system. Establishes the audit trail timeline.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the accrual amount. Supports multi-currency accrual management for global operations.']
  document_date date [note: 'Date on the source document that triggered the accrual. May differ from posting date for period-end adjustments.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique document number assigned to this accrual entry in the financial system. Used for audit trail and cross-reference with source documents.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the accrual is recognized. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the accrual is recognized. Supports period-end reporting and year-over-year analysis.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'Functional area classification for cost of sales accounting. Distinguishes between COGS, SG&A, R&D, and other functional expense categories.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the accrual record was last updated. Supports audit trail and change tracking for SOX compliance.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'Product or material SKU if the accrual is product-specific (e.g., warranty accrual by SKU). Null for non-product accruals.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date when the accrual entry was posted to the general ledger. Determines the accounting period in which the accrual is recognized.']
  promotion_accrual_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Foreign key to canonical promotion_accrual in promotion domain']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the accrual will be automatically reversed in the subsequent accounting period. Null for accruals that do not auto-reverse.']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the reversal entry if the accrual has been reversed. Links the original accrual to its reversal for complete audit trail.']
  reversal_posting_date date
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the accrual was reversed. Supports audit review and analysis of accrual accuracy.']
  reversed_amount decimal(18,2)
  source_document_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the source document or transaction in the originating system. Enables traceability back to the source for audit purposes.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which the accrual originated (e.g., SAP_FI, ORACLE_FIN, MANUAL_ENTRY). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  sox_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accrual entry meets SOX compliance requirements including proper documentation, approval, and audit trail. True if compliant, False otherwise.']
  ssot_note varchar [note: 'Authoritative GL accrual record; promotion_accrual references this via finance_accrual_id']
  supporting_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documentation or source system record that justifies the accrual. Required for SOX audit trail and compliance.']

  Note: 'General financial accrual for period-end accounting (expenses, liabilities). SSOT for corporate accrual accounting. Distinct from promotion.promotion_accrual which tracks trade promotion-specific accruals.'
}

Table finance.sox_control {
  sox_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SOX control record. Primary key for the sox_control data product.']
  company_code_id varchar
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Identifier of the cost center to which the control owner belongs, used for organizational reporting and control coverage analysis.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: SOX controls capture GL account code; FK to gl_account enables direct validation against the chart of accounts.']
  owner_employee_id varchar
  primary_sox_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for executing and maintaining the control.']
  sox_control_owner_employee_id varchar
  tertiary_sox_tester_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal auditor or external auditor who performed the most recent control test.']
  automated_flag boolean
  company_code varchar [note: 'The legal entity or company code to which this control applies, supporting multi-entity SOX compliance programs.']
  control_category varchar
  control_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the control, typically following organizational control numbering scheme (e.g., FIN-AP-001, FIN-GL-005).']
  control_description varchar
  control_documentation_link varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the detailed control documentation, including control narratives, process flows, and evidence repositories.']
  control_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the control is performed or executed (e.g., daily reconciliation, monthly close review, event-driven approval).']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Short descriptive name of the control (e.g., \'Three-Way Match for Purchase Orders\', \'Journal Entry Approval\').']
  control_nature varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the control is performed manually by personnel, automated through system configuration, or semi-automated requiring both system and manual steps.']
  control_number varchar
  control_objective varchar [note: 'Detailed statement of what the control is designed to achieve, including the risk it mitigates and the assertion it supports (e.g., \'Ensure all vendor invoices are matched to purchase orders and receiving documents before payment to prevent unauthorized disbursements\').']
  control_owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the control owner for reporting and accountability purposes.']
  control_owner_title varchar [note: 'Job title of the control owner (e.g., Accounts Payable Manager, Financial Controller, GL Accountant).']
  control_performer_name varchar
  control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the control: active (in operation), inactive (temporarily suspended), under-review (being evaluated for changes), or retired (no longer applicable).']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the control based on its timing and purpose: preventive (stops errors before they occur), detective (identifies errors after occurrence), or corrective (remediates identified errors).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this control record in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this control record was first created in the system.']
  deficiency_classification varchar [note: 'Severity classification of any identified control deficiency: none (no issues), control deficiency (minor issue), significant deficiency (important enough to merit attention), or material weakness (reasonable possibility of material misstatement).']
  deficiency_flag boolean
  deficiency_type varchar
  effective_date date
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this control was retired or superseded. Null for active controls.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this control became effective and operational.']
  effectiveness_rating varchar [note: 'Overall assessment of control effectiveness based on testing results: effective (operating as designed), ineffective (deficiency identified), not-tested (testing not yet performed), or not-applicable (control not in scope for current period).']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the nature of exceptions identified during testing, including root cause analysis and impact assessment.']
  exceptions_identified_count varchar [note: 'The number of exceptions or deviations identified during the most recent control test.']
  external_auditor_firm varchar [note: 'Name of the external audit firm that reviewed this control (e.g., Deloitte, PwC, EY, KPMG).']
  external_auditor_reviewed_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this control has been reviewed by external auditors (true) or only by internal audit (false).']
  financial_statement_assertion varchar [note: 'The specific financial statement assertion that this control supports: existence (assets/liabilities exist), completeness (all transactions recorded), accuracy (amounts correct), valuation (proper measurement), rights and obligations (entity has legal claim), or presentation and disclosure (proper classification and disclosure).']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter for which this control test and assessment applies, supporting quarterly SOX certification requirements.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year for which this control test and assessment applies, supporting multi-year SOX compliance tracking.']
  it_dependency_flag boolean
  itgc_dependent_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this control relies on IT general controls (true) or operates independently (false). ITGC-dependent controls require underlying IT controls to be effective.']
  key_control_flag boolean
  key_control_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is a key control (true) or supporting control (false). Key controls are critical to preventing or detecting material misstatements and receive heightened testing and monitoring.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this control record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this control record was last modified in the system.']
  last_test_date date
  last_test_result varchar
  next_test_date date
  preventive_detective_indicator varchar
  process_area varchar
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which identified deficiencies must be remediated, typically driven by materiality and external audit timelines.']
  remediation_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes on remediation activities, including actions taken, resources required, and progress updates.']
  remediation_plan varchar
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of remediation efforts for identified deficiencies: not-required (no deficiency), planned (remediation plan approved), in-progress (actively remediating), completed (remediation finished), or validated (remediation effectiveness confirmed).']
  risk_area varchar [note: 'The financial statement area or business process that this control addresses (e.g., Revenue Recognition, Accounts Payable, Inventory Valuation, Financial Close, COGS Allocation).']
  risk_description varchar
  risk_rating varchar
  source_system_code varchar
  sub_process_area varchar
  test_conclusion varchar [note: 'Detailed conclusion statement from the most recent control test, including auditor assessment, basis for conclusion, and any qualifications or limitations.']
  test_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent control test was performed.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'The audit procedure used to test the control: inquiry (asking control owner), observation (watching control execution), inspection (examining evidence), re-performance (auditor executes control independently), or analytical review (comparing data patterns).']
  test_population_size varchar [note: 'The total number of items or transactions in the population from which the test sample was drawn.']
  test_result varchar
  test_sample_size varchar [note: 'The number of items or transactions selected for testing in the most recent control test.']
  tester_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the tester for audit trail and accountability purposes.']

  Note: 'SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley) internal control record defining and tracking the financial controls framework for the consumer goods enterprise. Covers the full control lifecycle: definition, testing, remediation, and certification. Captures control ID, name, objective, type (preventive/detective), frequency, risk area, responsible owner, test date, test method (inquiry, observation, inspection, re-performance), sample size, exceptions identified, remediation status, remediation due date, effectiveness rating, and test conclusion. Supports SOX 302/404 compliance, internal audit management, control deficiency tracking, and external auditor evidence packages.'
}

Table finance.revenue_recognition {
  revenue_recognition_id decimal(18,2) [pk, note: 'Primary key for revenue_recognition']
  company_code_id varchar
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Revenue recognition event creator is required for compliance reporting and audit of recognized revenue.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue recognition events reference GL account code; FK to gl_account aligns revenue posting with the chart of accounts.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer invoice associated with this revenue recognition event.']
  journal_entry_id varchar
  original_event_revenue_recognition_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reference to the original revenue recognition event ID if this record is a reversal or adjustment.']
  performance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific performance obligation within the contract that is being satisfied. A contract may have multiple performance obligations (e.g., product delivery, extended warranty, promotional services).']
  primary_revenue_contract_id decimal(18,2)
  product_lca_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.product_lca. Business justification: Revenue recognition reports include product LCA data to meet sustainability disclosure requirements.']
  revenue_contract_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reference to the sales contract or customer agreement under which revenue is being recognized.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating sales order or transaction that triggered this revenue recognition event.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or trade account for whom revenue is being recognized.']
  accounting_standard varchar
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved this revenue recognition event for posting.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revenue recognition event was approved for posting.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the audit trail or approval workflow associated with this revenue recognition event.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Sales channel through which the transaction occurred (e.g., retail, e-commerce, wholesale, DSD).']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Company code representing the legal entity for which revenue is being recognized.']
  constraint_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a constraint was applied to the variable consideration estimate to prevent revenue reversal in future periods.']
  cost_center_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost center code associated with the revenue recognition event for cost allocation and tracking.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this revenue recognition event record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revenue recognition event record was first created in the system.']
  cumulative_catch_up_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative catch-up adjustment to revenue recognized in prior periods due to changes in estimates of variable consideration or performance obligation satisfaction.']
  cumulative_recognized_amount decimal(18,2)
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which revenue amounts are denominated.']
  deferred_amount decimal(18,2)
  deferred_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of revenue deferred to future periods because the performance obligation has not yet been satisfied.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Unique accounting document number assigned to the revenue recognition journal entry in the financial system.']
  estimated_returns_reserve decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reserve amount set aside for estimated product returns based on historical return rates and current sales.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year in which the revenue recognition event occurred.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the revenue recognition event occurred.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this revenue recognition event record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revenue recognition event record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments providing additional context or justification for the revenue recognition treatment applied.']
  percentage_of_completion varchar
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the revenue recognition entry was posted to the general ledger.']
  product_line_code varchar [note: 'Product line or category code for the goods or services being recognized.']
  profit_center_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Profit center code for internal management reporting and profitability analysis.']
  rebate_accrual decimal(18,2) [note: 'Accrued rebate amount expected to be paid to the customer based on volume or performance thresholds.']
  recognition_date date [note: 'The date on which revenue is recognized in the financial statements under ASC 606 or IFRS 15. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'Method by which revenue is recognized: point-in-time (at delivery or transfer of control) or over-time (as performance obligation is satisfied over a period).']
  recognition_number varchar
  recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the revenue recognition event: draft (pending approval), posted (finalized in GL), reversed (cancelled), or adjusted (modified after posting).']
  recognition_trigger varchar
  recognized_amount decimal(18,2)
  recognized_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of revenue recognized in this event after applying all constraints and adjustments.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this revenue recognition event is a reversal or correction of a previously recognized amount.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit identifier for the specific product or service being recognized.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this revenue recognition event originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA FI, Oracle Cloud Financials).']
  sox_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this revenue recognition event complies with SOX internal control requirements and has passed all required approval workflows.']
  trade_promotion_deduction decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount deducted from transaction price due to trade promotion allowances, discounts, or incentives provided to customers.']
  transaction_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total transaction price allocated to this performance obligation before adjustments for variable consideration.']
  variable_consideration_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated amount of variable consideration including trade promotions, rebates, volume discounts, returns, and other price concessions that reduce the transaction price.']

  Note: 'Revenue recognition event record capturing the timing and amount of revenue recognized under ASC 606 / IFRS 15 for consumer goods sales transactions. Stores performance obligation ID, contract reference, recognition date, recognition method (point-in-time vs over-time), recognized revenue amount, deferred revenue amount, variable consideration estimate (trade promotions, rebates, returns), constraint applied flag, and cumulative catch-up adjustment. Supports compliant revenue reporting and audit trail for complex CPG revenue arrangements.'
}

  total_payment_amount decimal(18,2)
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual payment transactions included in the run.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payment_run. Referenced by payment_run_id.'
}

Table finance.material_ledger_posting {
  material_ledger_posting_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for material_ledger_posting']
  company_code_id varchar
  cost_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Material ledger postings are recorded for a specific cost center; the code column is replaced by a proper FK.']
  gl_account_id varchar
  journal_entry_id varchar
  material_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material (product) to which the posting relates.']
  material_reversal_posting_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original posting that this record reverses, if applicable.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business partner (vendor, customer, or internal entity) associated with the posting.']
  primary_reversal_material_ledger_posting_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on material_ledger_posting (reversal_material_ledger_posting_id)']
  profit_center_id decimal(18,2) [note: 'FK to finance.profit_center']
  sku_id varchar
  actual_cost_per_unit varchar
  amount decimal(18,2)
  amount_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax, fee, discount, or other monetary adjustment applied to the gross amount.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Base monetary value of the posting before taxes, fees, or discounts.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final monetary value after adjustments; the amount posted to the ledger.']
  closing_entry_flag boolean
  cost_component decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost component (e.g., material, labor, overhead) associated with the posting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the posting amounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CNY|CHF|AUD|CAD|... — promote to reference product]']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the accounting document generated by the posting.']
  exchange_rate_difference_amount decimal(18,2)
  fiscal_period varchar
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the posting belongs.']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this posting reverses a previous posting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp
  ledger_account varchar [note: 'GL account to which the posting amount is posted.']
  movement_type varchar
  period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) of the posting within the fiscal year.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing/warehouse plant where the material is stocked.']
  posting_date date
  posting_number varchar [note: 'External posting number assigned by the finance system for traceability.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the material ledger posting.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the posting was created in the source system.']
  posting_type varchar [note: 'Category of posting indicating its purpose or origin.']
  price_difference decimal(18,2)
  price_difference_amount decimal(18,2)
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Physical quantity of material posted (in the unit of measure).']
  quantity_uom varchar
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ledger posting record was first captured in the lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this ledger posting record.']
  source_system_code varchar
  standard_cost_per_unit varchar
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the posted quantity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M|M2|M3|... — promote to reference product]']
  valuation_area varchar [note: 'Accounting area used for material valuation (e.g., company code).']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'Classification that determines the valuation method for the material.']
  valuation_type varchar
  variance_amount decimal(18,2)

  Note: 'Master reference table for material_ledger_posting. Referenced by material_ledger_posting_id.'
}

Table finance.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for party']
  bank_account_id varchar
  parent_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent party in a hierarchical relationship.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'P4: Connect procurement.party by adding column supplier_id (BIGINT) with FK to procurement.supplier.supplier_id because procurement.party is isolated with only inbound relationships. P4: connect_table: procurement.party** - add column supplier_id (BIGI']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'P5: Connect sales.party by adding column trade_account_id (BIGINT) with FK to sales.trade_account.trade_account_id because a party should resolve to either a supplier or a trade account. P5: connect_table: sales.party** - add column tra']
  address_line_1 varchar
  address_line_2 varchar

  created_timestamp timestamp

  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the party\'s relationship ends or is expected to end (nullable for ongoing relationships).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the party became effective for business transactions.']
  email_address varchar
  external_party_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used for the party in external source systems.']
  global_location_number varchar [note: 'Standardized global identifier for the party\'s physical location.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry classification code (e.g., NAICS) describing the party\'s primary business sector.']
  is_blacklisted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party is on a blacklist for compliance reasons.']
  last_activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent interaction or transaction involving the party.']

  party_role varchar

  payment_terms decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the party.']
  phone_number varchar
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the party\'s primary address.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Language preferred for communications with the party.']

  primary_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the party\'s primary mailing address (optional).']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Main email address used for electronic communication with the party.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Main telephone number for contacting the party.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the party record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the party record.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number of the party with the relevant corporate authority.']
  risk_score decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned to the party based on internal models.']
  source_system_code varchar
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the party\'s primary address.']
  state_province varchar
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party is exempt from sales tax.']
  tax_identification_number varchar
  tax_identifier decimal(18,2) [note: 'Government‑issued tax identification number (e.g., EIN, VAT).']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the party\'s primary location.']
  vat_registration_number varchar

  Note: 'Master reference table for party. Referenced by party_id.'
}

  measurement_period_start date [note: 'Measurement period start']
  on_time_performance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'On-time performance percentage']
  on_time_shipments varchar [note: 'On-time shipments']

  Note: 'Carrier performance metrics'
}

Table research.rd_project {
  rd_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the R&D or NPD project in the innovation pipeline.']
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: ESG commitments drive R&D project prioritisation and reporting; linking enables project‑level ESG tracking in project portfolio dashboards.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand under which this product will be launched, linking to the brand master.']
  primary_rd_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the executive or business leader sponsoring this R&D project, linking to the employee or workforce master.']
  product_category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category or segment this project targets, linking to the product taxonomy.']
  product_lca_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.product_lca. Business justification: R&D projects generate product LCA studies; linking the LCA record to its originating project enables regulatory filing and market‑entry sustainability reporting.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to consumer.consumer_segment. Business justification: Project planning uses a defined consumer segment; FK enables segment‑level reporting and budget alignment.']
  actual_launch_date date [note: 'Actual date the product was launched commercially, if the project has reached commercialization.']
  budget_allocated decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated to this R&D project for all phases, including formulation, testing, regulatory, and scale-up activities.']
  budget_spent decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative budget spent to date on this R&D project across all cost categories.']
  business_opportunity_context varchar [note: 'Description of the originating business opportunity, market gap, or consumer insight that triggered this R&D project.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Business rationale for project cancellation if the project was terminated before completion (e.g., technical infeasibility, market shift, portfolio prioritization).']
  competitive_benchmark varchar [note: 'Description of key competitive products or market benchmarks this innovation is designed to compete against or differentiate from.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this R&D project record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for target COGS and RSP (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  current_milestone varchar [note: 'Description of the current stage-gate milestone or deliverable the project team is working toward.']
  desired_benefits varchar [note: 'Key consumer benefits or product claims the innovation aims to deliver (e.g., moisturizing, long-lasting, eco-friendly, allergen-free).']
  key_performance_indicators varchar [note: 'List of key performance indicators defined in the innovation brief to measure project success (e.g., consumer acceptance score, margin target, time-to-market).']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Summary of key learnings, best practices, and insights captured from this project for future R&D initiatives.']
  milestone_due_date date [note: 'Target completion date for the current milestone in the project timeline.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this R&D project record was last updated in the system.']
  patent_filing_status varchar [note: 'Status of intellectual property protection for this innovation: not applicable, planned, filed, granted, or rejected.']
  patent_number varchar [note: 'Patent application or grant number if intellectual property protection has been filed or granted for this innovation.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned alphanumeric code for the R&D project, used for external communication and cross-functional reference.']
  project_completion_date date [note: 'Date the R&D project was completed, either through successful commercialization or formal closure.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the R&D project, typically reflecting the innovation brief or product concept.']
  project_start_date date [note: 'Date the R&D project was officially initiated and entered the innovation pipeline.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the R&D project: active, on hold, cancelled, completed, or archived.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the R&D project by innovation category: new product development, reformulation, line extension, cost optimization, packaging innovation, or sustainability initiative.']
  regulatory_pathway varchar [note: 'Description of the regulatory approval pathway required for this product (e.g., FDA OTC monograph, EU cosmetics notification, EPA registration).']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Overall risk level assessment for the project considering technical feasibility, regulatory complexity, market acceptance, and supply chain readiness.']
  stage_gate_phase varchar [note: 'Current stage-gate phase of the project in the NPD process: ideation, concept, feasibility, development, validation, scale-up, launch, or post-launch review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ideation|concept|feasibility|development|validation|scale_up|launch|post_launch — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  strategic_fit_rationale decimal(18,6) [note: 'Business justification explaining how this project aligns with corporate strategy, brand positioning, and portfolio objectives.']
  strategic_priority_tier decimal(18,4) [note: 'Strategic importance classification of the project: tier 1 (critical/flagship), tier 2 (important), tier 3 (standard), or exploratory (research).']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative sustainability assessment score for this project, measuring environmental impact, renewable sourcing, and circular economy alignment.']
  target_cogs decimal(15,4) [note: 'Target cost of goods sold per unit for the product, as specified in the innovation brief to ensure profitability.']
  target_consumer_segment varchar [note: 'Description of the target consumer demographic, psychographic, or behavioral segment for this innovation, as defined in the innovation brief.']
  target_launch_date date [note: 'Planned date for commercial launch of the product resulting from this R&D project.']
  target_rsp decimal(15,4) [note: 'Target recommended selling price per unit for the product at retail, as specified in the innovation brief.']

  Note: 'Master record for all R&D and NPD projects in the innovation pipeline, from initial business opportunity through commercialization. Captures project identity, innovation brief details (target consumer, desired benefits, cost/RSP targets, competitive benchmarks, strategic fit rationale, key performance indicators), stage-gate phase, project type (new product, reformulation, line extension, cost optimization), target launch date, project sponsor, brand alignment, strategic priority tier, budget allocation, resource assignment, current milestone status, and the originating business opportunity context. Serves as the backbone of the R&D portfolio management function and links all downstream formulation, testing, regulatory, and scale-up activities. Subsumes the innovation brief as the project inception record.'
}

Table research.stage_gate_milestone {
  stage_gate_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stage gate milestone record. Primary key.']
  primary_stage_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the executive or senior leader responsible for chairing the gate review and making the final gate decision.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the R&D project to which this stage gate milestone belongs.']
  tertiary_stage_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who last modified this stage gate milestone record, used for audit and accountability purposes.']
  action_items varchar [note: 'Summary of key action items, conditions, and follow-up tasks assigned during the gate review that must be completed before proceeding or for the next gate.']
  consumer_acceptance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score from consumer testing and sensory evaluation reflecting target consumer acceptance, purchase intent, and preference versus competitive products.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stage gate milestone record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  decision_rationale decimal(18,6) [note: 'Detailed explanation of the business reasoning, risk assessment, and strategic considerations that led to the gate decision outcome.']
  delay_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of the root cause for any delay in the gate review milestone, such as incomplete deliverables, resource constraints, technical challenges, or regulatory issues.']
  deliverables_checklist_status varchar [note: 'Overall completion status of the mandatory deliverables checklist required for this gate. Complete indicates all deliverables met, incomplete indicates gaps, partial indicates some deliverables met.']
  deliverables_completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of mandatory gate deliverables that have been completed and approved at the time of review, expressed as a value between 0.00 and 100.00.']
  gate_attendees varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of names or identifiers of executives and stakeholders who attended the gate review meeting.']
  gate_criteria_met_count varchar [note: 'Number of mandatory gate criteria that were successfully met and approved during the review process.']
  gate_criteria_total_count varchar [note: 'Total number of mandatory gate criteria defined for this stage gate review, used to calculate criteria fulfillment rate.']
  gate_decision varchar [note: 'The formal decision outcome of the stage gate review. Go indicates approval to proceed to next stage, kill terminates the project, hold pauses for additional information, recycle returns to previous stage for rework.']
  gate_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between the previous gate review date and this gate review date, used to track stage cycle time and NPD velocity.']
  gate_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the stage gate milestone (e.g., Concept Review, Business Case Approval, Launch Readiness).']
  gate_number varchar [note: 'The formal stage gate identifier in the NPD (New Product Development) process. G0 represents concept ideation, G1 scoping, G2 business case, G3 development, G4 testing and validation, G5 launch.']
  gate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stage gate milestone. Scheduled indicates future gate, in_review indicates active review in progress, completed indicates decision made, cancelled indicates gate no longer required.']
  investment_approved_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The financial investment amount approved by the gate review committee to fund activities in the next stage of the project, expressed in the company\'s reporting currency.']
  investment_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the approved investment amount.']
  is_milestone_delayed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this gate milestone was delayed beyond its originally scheduled review date. True indicates delay occurred, False indicates on-time or early completion.']
  market_attractiveness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score reflecting the commercial attractiveness of the target market, considering market size, growth rate, competitive intensity, and strategic fit.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stage gate milestone record was last modified, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  next_gate_target_date date [note: 'The planned target date for the next stage gate review milestone in the project lifecycle, used for forward planning and resource allocation.']
  npv_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'The estimated net present value of the project calculated at this gate, used for portfolio prioritization and go/kill decision making.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of the project\'s compliance with applicable regulatory requirements (FDA, EPA, REACH, etc.) at this gate. Compliant indicates all requirements met, non_compliant indicates gaps, pending_review indicates assessment in progress.']
  review_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal stage gate review meeting was conducted and the gate decision was made.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score assigned during the gate review, reflecting technical, commercial, regulatory, and operational risks associated with proceeding to the next stage.']
  roi_estimate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The estimated return on investment percentage for the project at this gate, calculated as expected return divided by investment cost.']
  scheduled_review_date date [note: 'The originally planned date for the stage gate review, used to track schedule adherence and milestone slippage.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score reflecting the environmental and social sustainability profile of the project, considering carbon footprint, sustainable sourcing, packaging recyclability, and social impact.']
  technical_feasibility_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score assessing the technical feasibility of the project, considering R&D capability, technology maturity, formulation complexity, and manufacturing readiness.']

  Note: 'Tracks each formal stage-gate review milestone within an R&D project lifecycle. Records gate number (G0 concept through G5 launch), gate decision (go/kill/hold/recycle), review date, gate owner, decision rationale, deliverables checklist completion status, and next gate target date. Enables portfolio governance and NPD pipeline velocity tracking across all active projects.'
}

Table research.research_formulation {
  research_formulation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product formulation record. Primary key for the research formulation entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the principal scientist or formulation chemist responsible for developing this formulation.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this formulation to the parent R&D project portfolio. Enables tracking of formulations within broader innovation initiatives.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to consumer.consumer_segment. Business justification: Formulation development targets a specific consumer segment; linking ensures alignment with market research insights.']
  allergen_declaration decimal(18,4) [note: 'List of known allergens present in the formulation requiring declaration per regulatory requirements (e.g., EU Allergen List).']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the formulation received final approval for commercialization from the stage-gate review committee.']
  batch_size_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard production batch size in kilograms for manufacturing scale-up planning. Based on pilot plant or commercial production capacity.']
  claim_support_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the formulation requires clinical or consumer testing to substantiate marketing claims (e.g., anti-aging, moisturizing efficacy).']
  consumer_test_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average consumer acceptance score from home-use testing or clinical trials. Scale typically 0-10 or 0-100 depending on protocol.']
  cooling_rate_celsius_per_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Controlled cooling rate in degrees Celsius per minute after mixing. Important for emulsion stability and crystal formation control.']
  cost_currency_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost target (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  cost_target_per_unit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target manufacturing cost per unit for the formulation. Used for feasibility assessment and margin analysis during NPD.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the formulation record was first created in the R&D system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  cruelty_free_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the formulation and its ingredients have not been tested on animals, meeting cruelty-free certification standards.']
  development_status varchar [note: 'Current stage of the formulation in the R&D lifecycle. Tracks progression through stage-gate process from ideation to commercialization approval. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|experimental|prototype|pilot|validation|approved|discontinued|on_hold — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  discontinued_date date [note: 'Date when the formulation was discontinued or archived. Null if still active in the development pipeline.']
  formulation_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier assigned to the formulation in the R&D system. Used for cross-referencing across lab notebooks, pilot batches, and regulatory submissions.']
  formulation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed technical description of the formulation including intended use, key benefits, and distinguishing characteristics. Used for internal documentation and regulatory submissions.']
  formulation_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the formulation assigned by the R&D team. May include project codename or working title.']
  formulation_type varchar [note: 'Physical form and application method of the formulation. Critical for manufacturing process design and packaging selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: leave_on|rinse_off|solid|liquid|aerosol|gel|cream|powder|emulsion|suspension — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fragrance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of fragrance used in the formulation. Important for allergen labeling and consumer preference targeting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the formulation record. Tracks version history and change management.']
  microbiological_challenge_test_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the formulation passed preservative efficacy testing (challenge test) demonstrating adequate antimicrobial protection.']
  mixing_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Optimal mixing temperature in degrees Celsius for formulation processing. Affects ingredient solubility and stability.']
  mixing_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Required mixing duration in minutes to achieve homogeneous formulation. Critical process parameter for manufacturing instructions.']
  natural_content_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ingredients derived from natural sources per ISO 16128 standard. Used for natural/organic product claims.']
  patent_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a patent application has been filed for this formulation or its novel components.']
  patent_number varchar [note: 'Patent application or grant number if applicable. Confidential intellectual property reference.']
  preservative_system varchar [note: 'Description of the antimicrobial preservative system used in the formulation. Critical for microbial stability and regulatory compliance.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level product category classification for the formulation. Aligns with the company\'s product portfolio segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: skin_care|hair_care|oral_care|personal_hygiene|household_cleaning|baby_care|cosmetics|fragrance|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  product_formulation_id varchar
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory category classification for the formulation. Determines applicable compliance requirements and approval pathways.']
  safety_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Status of toxicological safety assessment and risk evaluation for the formulation. Required for regulatory compliance.']
  sensory_evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score from trained panel sensory evaluation assessing appearance, texture, fragrance, and application properties. Scale typically 0-10.']
  shelf_life_target_months varchar [note: 'Target shelf life duration in months from manufacturing date. Based on stability testing and regulatory requirements for the product category.']
  stability_test_status varchar [note: 'Current status of accelerated and real-time stability testing program for the formulation. Required for shelf-life validation.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite sustainability rating for the formulation based on ingredient sourcing, biodegradability, and environmental impact. Scale typically 0-100.']
  target_consumer_segment varchar [note: 'Intended consumer demographic or market segment for the formulation (e.g., sensitive skin, anti-aging, professional use, mass market).']
  target_ph_max decimal(4,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable pH value for the formulation. Defines upper bound of acceptable pH range for quality control.']
  target_ph_min decimal(4,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable pH value for the formulation. Critical for product stability, efficacy, and skin compatibility.']
  target_viscosity_cps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target viscosity measurement in centipoise (cps) for the formulation. Critical quality parameter for product consistency and manufacturing processability.']
  vegan_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the formulation contains no animal-derived ingredients and meets vegan certification criteria.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the formulation following semantic versioning (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1). Incremented with each iteration or modification during development.']

  Note: 'R&D experimental formulation developed during research phase - may have multiple iterations before commercialization. SSOT for research formulations. Distinct from product.product_formulation which is the approved production recipe.'
}

Table research.research_formulation_ingredient {
  research_formulation_ingredient_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each ingredient line within a formulation. Primary key for the formulation bill of materials (BOM) at ingredient level.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Ingredient approval workflow records which employee approved the ingredient, needed for traceability and GMP compliance.']
  raw_material_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.raw_material_spec. Business justification: Each formulation ingredient references a raw material; linking to raw_material_spec centralises material data and removes duplicated raw_material_code and supplier_code.']
  research_formulation_id varchar
  addition_order varchar [note: 'Order in which the ingredient is added within its assigned phase. Critical for process control and product quality.']
  allergen_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingredient is a known or potential allergen requiring special labeling or disclosure. True if allergen.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the ingredient was approved for use in this formulation by R&D and regulatory teams.']
  asean_maximum_concentration_percent decimal(8,5) [note: 'Maximum allowable concentration percentage for this ingredient under ASEAN regulations. Null if no ASEAN-specific restriction applies.']
  asean_restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingredient is subject to restrictions or prohibitions under ASEAN cosmetic regulations. True if restricted or prohibited.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique CAS registry number for the chemical substance. Used for regulatory submissions, safety data sheets, and global ingredient identification.']
  cost_currency_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the ingredient cost. Enables multi-currency cost management.']
  cost_per_kg decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard cost per kilogram for the ingredient. Used for formulation cost analysis and NPD financial modeling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ingredient line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  einecs_number varchar [note: 'EINECS registry number for substances marketed in the European Union before September 1981. Required for EU regulatory compliance.']
  eu_maximum_concentration_percent decimal(8,5) [note: 'Maximum allowable concentration percentage for this ingredient under EU regulations. Null if no EU-specific restriction applies.']
  eu_restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingredient is subject to restrictions or prohibitions under EU cosmetics regulations. True if restricted or prohibited.']
  function_class varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the ingredient within the formulation (e.g., emulsifier, preservative, fragrance, active, colorant). Used for regulatory categorization and formulation analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: emulsifier|preservative|fragrance|active|colorant|solvent|thickener|surfactant — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
