  preparation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the preparation of the regulatory dossier was initiated by the R&D or regulatory affairs team.']
  preservative_system_approved boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the preservative system used in the formulation has been approved for use in the target market.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level product category classification (e.g., Skin Care, Hair Care, Oral Care, Personal Hygiene) for the product covered by this dossier.']
  regulatory_affairs_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the regulatory affairs contact responsible for this dossier, used for communication with regulatory authorities.']
  regulatory_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or agency to which this dossier is submitted (e.g., FDA, European Commission, Health Canada, NMPA).']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory authority rejected the dossier submission. Null if not rejected.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation provided by the regulatory authority for rejecting the dossier submission, including specific deficiencies or non-compliance issues.']
  review_start_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory authority officially began their review of the submitted dossier.']
  safety_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the product safety assessment report required for regulatory compliance, typically prepared by a qualified safety assessor.']
  safety_assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the qualified safety assessor who prepared or reviewed the product safety assessment for this dossier.']
  submission_category varchar [note: 'Category of regulatory submission indicating whether this is a new product, modification, renewal, or other type of regulatory filing.']
  target_market_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the primary target market jurisdiction for this regulatory submission.']
  target_market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or regulatory zone for the submission (e.g., European Union, ASEAN, North America).']
  target_submission_date date [note: 'Planned date for submitting the completed dossier to the regulatory authority, aligned with project milestones and launch timelines.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date when the company voluntarily withdrew the dossier from regulatory review. Null if not withdrawn.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Internal business reason for voluntarily withdrawing the dossier from regulatory review (e.g., formulation change, market strategy shift, cost considerations).']

  Note: 'Master record for regulatory submission dossiers prepared for new product registrations and notifications. Captures dossier type (CPNP EU notification, FDA OTC monograph, ASEAN ASEAN Cosmetic Directive, Health Canada), target market, submission date, regulatory authority reference number, dossier status (in preparation, submitted, under review, approved, rejected), responsible regulatory affairs manager, and linked product/formulation. Distinct from the regulatory domain\'s product registration — this is the R&D-owned dossier preparation record.'
}

Table research.claim_substantiation {
  claim_substantiation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the claim substantiation record. Primary key.']
  consumer_test_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the consumer test record that provides supporting evidence for this claim.']
  primary_claim_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the business owner (typically from marketing or brand management) responsible for this claim and its usage.']
  product_formulation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific formulation version tested to substantiate this claim.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the R&D project that generated the evidence for this claim.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finished product for which this claim is substantiated.']
  study_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal study protocol document that defines the testing methodology used for substantiation.']
  tertiary_claim_regulatory_reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory affairs specialist who reviewed this claim for compliance with applicable regulations.']
  advertising_standard_compliance varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of advertising standards and self-regulatory bodies that this claim complies with (e.g., \'NAD,ASA,ASRC,CARU\').']
  challenge_history varchar [note: 'Summary of any historical challenges, complaints, or disputes related to this claim from regulatory bodies, competitors, or consumer advocacy groups.']
  channel_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of marketing and sales channels where this claim is approved for use (e.g., \'packaging,digital,print,broadcast,retail_pos\').']
  claim_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the claim substantiation within the internal governance process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|approved|rejected|conditional_approval|withdrawn|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  claim_category varchar [note: 'Broader categorization of the claim for organizational and reporting purposes.']
  claim_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code assigned to this claim substantiation record for tracking and reference purposes.']
  claim_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this claim substantiation becomes valid and the claim may be used in marketing materials.']
  claim_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this claim substantiation expires and the claim must be re-substantiated or withdrawn from use.']
  claim_text varchar [note: 'The exact wording of the marketing or product performance claim being substantiated (e.g., \'48-hour moisturization\', \'clinically proven to reduce wrinkles\', \'dermatologist tested\').']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the claim based on its nature: efficacy (performance), safety (non-irritating, hypoallergenic), environmental (sustainability, eco-friendly), comparative (vs. competitor), sensory (fragrance, texture), or nutritional (for food/beverage products).']
  clinical_study_reference varchar [note: 'Citation or reference number for the clinical study that substantiates this claim (e.g., internal study ID, ClinicalTrials.gov identifier, published journal article DOI).']
  confidence_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level of the study results expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00 for 95% confidence).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this claim substantiation record was first created in the system.']
  efficacy_result_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of the key efficacy findings from the substantiation study (e.g., \'87% of users reported smoother skin after 4 weeks\').']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this claim substantiation record was last updated or modified.']
  legal_review_date date [note: 'Date when the legal review was completed and the claim was approved or rejected by legal counsel.']
  legal_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of legal department review to ensure the claim is defensible and compliant with advertising standards.']
  market_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic markets or regions where this claim is approved for use (e.g., \'USA,CAN,GBR,DEU,FRA\' using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes).']
  next_renewal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next renewal or re-substantiation of this claim.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context regarding this claim substantiation, including any special conditions or limitations on claim usage.']
  p_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Statistical p-value from hypothesis testing, indicating the probability that the observed results occurred by chance.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory review and approval for this claim, if required by governing authorities.']
  regulatory_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_claim. Business justification: Each claim substantiation feeds a regulatory claim filing; linking provides the Claim Approval Dashboard that maps substantiation evidence to claim approval status.']
  regulatory_review_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory review was completed.']
  renewal_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required renewals or re-substantiation cycles for this claim.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this claim substantiation requires periodic renewal or re-validation.']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the legal and regulatory risk associated with using this claim, based on strength of evidence and potential for challenge.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of subjects, panelists, or test units included in the substantiation study.']
  statistical_significance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study results achieved statistical significance at the predefined confidence level.']
  substantiation_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to substantiate this claim, including study costs, testing fees, and expert consultation fees.']
  substantiation_cost_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the substantiation cost amount.']
  substantiation_method varchar [note: 'The primary methodology used to generate evidence supporting the claim (e.g., clinical study, consumer perception test, instrumental lab measurement, expert panel review, published literature). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clinical_study|consumer_test|instrumental_measurement|expert_endorsement|literature_review|in_vitro_test|in_vivo_test|historical_data — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  supporting_document_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of document IDs, file paths, or references to all supporting evidence documents (test reports, certificates, expert opinions, published papers) that substantiate this claim.']
  test_duration_days varchar [note: 'Duration of the substantiation study or test period measured in days.']

  Note: 'Records the evidence package assembled to substantiate specific marketing and product performance claims for regulatory and legal defense. Captures claim text (e.g., \'48-hour moisturization\', \'clinically proven\', \'dermatologist tested\'), claim type (efficacy, safety, environmental, comparative), substantiation method (clinical study, consumer test, instrumental measurement, expert endorsement), supporting study references, claim approval status, legal review sign-off, market applicability, and expiry/renewal date. Bridges R&D evidence to marketing claim usage and ensures claims are defensible under advertising standards (NAD, ASA) and regulatory requirements.'
}

Table research.innovation_brief {
  innovation_brief_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the innovation brief document. Primary key.']
  esg_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Innovation briefs must align with corporate ESG commitments; linking ensures new concepts are evaluated against ESG targets in the innovation pipeline.']
  innovation_linked_rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the R&D project that was created as a result of this approved innovation brief, establishing traceability from commercial trigger to execution.']
  innovation_rd_project_id varchar
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand under which the proposed product will be marketed.']
  primary_innovation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commercial or marketing leader who is sponsoring the innovation brief and will champion it through the approval process.']
  product_category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product category or sub-category that this innovation brief targets.']
  tertiary_innovation_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the executive or committee member who granted final approval for the innovation brief.']
  tertiary_quinary_innovation_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the innovation brief record.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the innovation brief was officially approved, triggering the creation of an R&D project and allocation of resources.']
  attachments_reference varchar [note: 'Reference or URI to supporting documents attached to the innovation brief (market research reports, consumer insights, competitive analysis, financial models).']
  brief_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the innovation brief, typically following a standardized format for tracking and reference across commercial and R&D teams.']
  brief_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the innovation brief document in the approval workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|on_hold|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  brief_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the innovation brief that summarizes the product concept or business opportunity being proposed.']
  brief_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the innovation brief based on the nature of the NPD initiative (new product development, line extension, reformulation, etc.).']
  budget_currency_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated development budget.']
  business_opportunity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the market opportunity, consumer need gap, competitive landscape, and strategic rationale for pursuing this innovation.']
  competitive_benchmark_products varchar [note: 'List of key competitor products that serve as performance, sensory, and pricing benchmarks for the proposed innovation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the innovation brief record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for target COGS and RSP financial figures.']
  desired_product_benefits varchar [note: 'List of key functional, emotional, and sensory benefits that the product must deliver to meet consumer needs and differentiate from competitors.']
  estimated_development_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total budget required for R&D, formulation development, testing, regulatory submission, and pilot production to bring the innovation to market.']
  estimated_market_size decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total addressable market size in monetary value for the target category and consumer segment.']
  estimated_npv decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated net present value of the innovation project, representing the discounted future cash flows minus the initial investment.']
  estimated_roi_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected return on investment percentage for the innovation project over a defined time horizon, used for financial justification and prioritization.']
  innovation_priority_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic priority classification assigned to the innovation brief, determining resource allocation and executive sponsorship level.']
  key_performance_indicators varchar [note: 'List of measurable success criteria and KPIs that will be used to evaluate the innovation project (e.g., market share target, NPS score, repeat purchase rate).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the innovation brief record was last updated or modified.']
  market_size_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated market size figure.']
  npv_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated NPV figure.']
  regulatory_pathway varchar [note: 'Expected regulatory approval pathway required for the product based on category, claims, and jurisdiction (e.g., FDA notification, EU cosmetic registration).']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date when the innovation brief was rejected or declined by the review committee.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the review committee for rejecting the innovation brief, including strategic, financial, or technical rationale.']
  risk_assessment_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of key risks identified for the innovation (technical feasibility, regulatory, supply chain, competitive response) and proposed mitigation strategies.']
  strategic_fit_rationale decimal(18,6) [note: 'Explanation of how this innovation aligns with corporate strategy, brand positioning, portfolio gaps, and long-term business objectives.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the innovation brief was formally submitted for review and approval by the stage-gate committee or innovation board.']
  sustainability_target_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target sustainability score or rating that the product must achieve, based on corporate sustainability commitments (e.g., carbon footprint, recyclability, natural content).']
  target_cogs decimal(15,4) [note: 'Target manufacturing cost per unit that R&D must achieve to meet profitability objectives. Critical constraint for formulation development.']
  target_consumer_segment varchar [note: 'Description of the primary consumer demographic, psychographic, or behavioral segment that the proposed product is designed to serve.']
  target_gross_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target gross margin percentage calculated from target RSP and COGS, representing the profitability threshold for project approval.']
  target_launch_date date [note: 'Desired commercial launch date for the product in the market, driving the R&D project timeline and stage-gate milestones.']
  target_launch_geography varchar [note: 'Geographic markets or regions where the product is intended to launch initially (e.g., USA, EUR, APAC).']
  target_rsp decimal(15,4) [note: 'Target retail selling price that the product is expected to command in the market, used to calculate margin and assess commercial viability.']

  Note: 'Formal innovation brief document that initiates an NPD project, capturing the business opportunity, target consumer, desired product benefits, cost target (COGS), RSP target, competitive benchmarks, key performance indicators, and strategic fit rationale. Serves as the contract between marketing/commercial and R&D at project inception. Distinct from the R&D project master — the brief is the upstream commercial trigger that precedes project creation.'
}

Table research.raw_material_spec {
  raw_material_spec_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the raw material specification record. Primary key for R&D-owned technical specifications governing formulation use.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the R&D scientist or manager who approved this raw material specification.']
  inci_library_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.inci_library. Business justification: Raw material specifications share many attributes with the INCI library; linking consolidates ingredient master data and eliminates duplicated INCI fields.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the raw material specification was approved by R&D for use in formulations.']
  approved_concentration_max_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable concentration of this raw material in formulations as approved by R&D and regulatory for safety and compliance.']
  approved_concentration_min_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable concentration of this raw material in formulations as approved by R&D for efficacy and safety.']
  cost_currency_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost per kilogram (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  cost_per_kg decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard cost of the raw material per kilogram for R&D costing and formulation budget estimation. Used for target COGS calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the raw material specification record was first created in the system.']
  einecs_number varchar [note: 'European regulatory identifier for chemical substances marketed in the EU before September 1981. Required for EU REACH compliance.']
  eu_maximum_concentration_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable concentration of this raw material in finished products as mandated by EU regulations. Null if no EU restriction applies.']
  eu_restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the raw material is subject to restrictions or prohibitions under EU cosmetics regulations.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'GHS hazard classification codes for the raw material indicating physical, health, and environmental hazards (e.g., H302, H315, H317). Used for SDS and safety compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the raw material specification record was last updated or modified.']
  material_name varchar [note: 'Common or trade name of the raw material as used in R&D documentation and formulation records.']
  moisture_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable moisture content expressed as a percentage. Important for stability and shelf life of formulations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special handling instructions for the raw material specification. May include formulation tips, compatibility warnings, or sourcing notes.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the raw material has organic certification from a recognized certifying body (e.g., USDA Organic, ECOCERT, COSMOS).']
  palm_oil_derivative_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the raw material is derived from palm oil. Important for sustainability tracking and RSPO compliance.']
  particle_size_microns decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average or target particle size measured in microns. Relevant for powders and solid materials affecting texture and performance.']
  purity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum purity level of the active ingredient or material expressed as a percentage. Critical specification parameter for formulation consistency.']
  raw_material_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the raw material by R&D for internal identification and formulation reference. Distinct from procurement supplier material codes.']
  rd_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the raw material specification for use in R&D formulations. Approved materials are cleared for inclusion in new product development.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory category or classification of the raw material (e.g., cosmetic ingredient, OTC active, food additive, fragrance component, colorant) determining applicable regulatory pathways.']
  sds_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a current Safety Data Sheet is available for this raw material in the document management system.']
  shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'Expected shelf life of the raw material in months under specified storage conditions before quality degradation.']
  specification_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the raw material specification document. Incremented when specification parameters are updated.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum storage temperature in degrees Celsius to prevent degradation or safety hazards.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum storage temperature in degrees Celsius required to maintain raw material stability and quality.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'Internal code identifying the approved supplier in the procurement and R&D systems.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the approved supplier or manufacturer providing this raw material. Used for R&D sourcing and formulation traceability.']
  us_fda_maximum_concentration_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable concentration of this raw material in finished products as mandated by US FDA regulations. Null if no FDA restriction applies.']
  us_fda_restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the raw material is subject to restrictions or prohibitions under US FDA regulations for cosmetics or OTC drugs.']
  vegan_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the raw material is free from animal-derived ingredients and suitable for vegan product formulations.']

  Note: 'R&D-owned specification records for raw materials and ingredients used in formulation development. Captures raw material code, material name, INCI name, supplier name, grade/quality level, physical form, specification parameters (purity, moisture content, particle size, color, odor), approved concentration range, storage conditions, and R&D approval status. Distinct from procurement\'s supplier material master — this is the R&D technical specification that governs formulation use.'
}

Table research.research_packaging_spec {
  research_packaging_spec_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the R&D packaging specification record. Primary key for this entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the R&D team member or manager who approved this packaging specification for use in prototypes or production.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the R&D project under which this packaging specification was developed or evaluated.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the packaging specification received R&D approval for use in the NPD project.']
  barrier_properties varchar [note: 'Description of the packaging materials barrier characteristics, including oxygen barrier, moisture barrier, light barrier, or UV protection properties critical for product stability.']
  capacity_ml decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominal fill capacity of the packaging component in milliliters, representing the target product volume for primary containers.']
  child_resistant_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the packaging component has been certified as child-resistant per regulatory requirements for hazardous products.']
  closure_type varchar [note: 'Type of closure mechanism for the packaging component, such as screw cap, pump dispenser, flip-top, spray nozzle, or child-resistant closure.']
  color_specification varchar [note: 'Color designation for the packaging component, typically using Pantone codes or descriptive color names for brand consistency.']
  compatibility_test_date date [note: 'Date when the formulation-packaging compatibility testing was completed or last reviewed.']
  compatibility_test_result varchar [note: 'Summary of the compatibility test findings, including any observed interactions, leaching, discoloration, or structural changes.']
  component_height_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the packaging component in millimeters, measured per the specification drawing.']
  component_length_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the packaging component in millimeters, measured per the specification drawing.']
  component_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the packaging component within the packaging hierarchy: primary (direct product contact), secondary (outer carton/box), tertiary (pallet/shipper), closure (cap/pump/dispenser), label, or insert.']
  component_weight_grams decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of the empty packaging component in grams, used for sustainability calculations and shipping cost estimation.']
  component_width_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the packaging component in millimeters, measured per the specification drawing.']
  cost_currency_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the cost per unit value.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or quoted cost per unit of the packaging component, used for NPD cost modeling and target COGS calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this packaging specification record was first created in the R&D system.']
  decoration_method decimal(18,4) [note: 'Method used to apply graphics, branding, or regulatory information to the packaging component. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: screen_print|pad_print|label|heat_transfer|in_mold_label|direct_print|embossing|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  eu_reach_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the packaging material complies with EU REACH regulation for chemical substance registration and restriction.']
  fda_food_contact_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the packaging material is approved for direct food contact per FDA regulations.']
  formulation_compatibility_status varchar [note: 'Status of compatibility testing between the packaging material and the product formulation, indicating whether the packaging is suitable for the intended product.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this packaging specification record was last updated, tracking iterative design changes and review cycles.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard lead time in days from order placement to delivery for this packaging component, critical for project timeline planning.']
  material_grade varchar [note: 'Specific grade or quality designation of the material, including food-grade, pharmaceutical-grade, or recycled content specifications.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Primary material composition of the packaging component, using standard polymer abbreviations and material classifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HDPE|PET|PETG|PP|glass|aluminum|paperboard|corrugated|LDPE|PVC|bioplastic|composite — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum order quantity required by the supplier for this packaging component, impacting prototype and pilot production planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional technical details, design rationale, testing observations, or special handling requirements for the packaging specification.']
  prototype_batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number of the prototype packaging samples produced for evaluation, linking to physical samples in R&D inventory.']
  rd_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the packaging specification within the R&D stage-gate process, indicating readiness for prototype or production use.']
  recyclability_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the packaging components recyclability based on material type and local recycling infrastructure availability.']
  recycled_content_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of post-consumer or post-industrial recycled material content in the packaging component, supporting sustainability goals.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall regulatory compliance status of the packaging specification against applicable FDA, EU, and regional packaging regulations.']
  specification_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the packaging specification, used for cross-referencing in R&D documentation and prototype builds.']
  specification_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the packaging specification, typically including component type and material summary.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the supplier or manufacturer of the packaging component, used for sourcing and procurement coordination.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite sustainability score for the packaging specification, considering recyclability, recycled content, material weight reduction, and renewable material usage.']
  tamper_evident_feature boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the packaging includes tamper-evident features such as breakable seals, shrink bands, or induction seals.']
  technical_drawing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the detailed technical drawing or CAD file for the packaging component.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the packaging specification, following major.minor versioning convention to track iterative design changes.']

  Note: 'R&D packaging specification for prototypes and trials - experimental materials and designs under evaluation. SSOT for research packaging development. Distinct from product.product_packaging_spec which is the approved production spec.'
}

Table research.safety_assessment {
  safety_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for safety_assessment']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved the safety assessment for regulatory submission.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the R&D project under which this safety assessment was conducted.']
  research_formulation_id varchar
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finished product being assessed, if applicable. May be null for formulation-only assessments.']
  supersedes_assessment_safety_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous safety assessment record that this version supersedes, maintaining audit trail of assessment history.']
  allergen_declaration decimal(18,4) [note: 'List of allergenic substances present in the formulation that must be declared on the label per regulatory requirements (e.g., EU Allergen List per Regulation 1223/2009 Annex III).']
  application_frequency varchar [note: 'Recommended frequency of product application (e.g., once daily, twice daily, as needed, weekly).']
  application_site varchar [note: 'Body site(s) where the product is intended to be applied (e.g., face, hands, scalp, body, mucous membranes).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the safety assessment was formally approved for use in regulatory submissions and product commercialization.']
  assessment_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the safety assessment, used for external reference and regulatory submissions.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the safety assessment was completed and signed off by the assessor.']
  assessment_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the safety assessment work commenced.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the safety assessment record.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Type of safety assessment conducted. CPSR (Cosmetic Product Safety Report) per EU Regulation 1223/2009, toxicological risk assessment, dermal sensitization, HRIPT (Human Repeat Insult Patch Test), ocular irritation, or mutagenicity assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cosmetic_product_safety_report|toxicological_risk_assessment|dermal_sensitization_assessment|hript|ocular_irritation_assessment|mutagenicity_assessment|phototoxicity_assessment|reproductive_toxicity_assessment — promote to reference product]']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the qualified safety assessor who conducted the evaluation.']
  assessor_qualification varchar [note: 'Professional qualifications and credentials of the safety assessor (e.g., PhD Toxicology, Registered Pharmacist, Diploma in Pharmaceutical Medicine).']
  assessor_registration_number decimal(18,4) [note: 'Professional registration or license number of the safety assessor, as required by regulatory authorities.']
  conditions_of_use varchar [note: 'Specific conditions under which the product is deemed safe, including application site, frequency, duration, and target population (e.g., adults only, rinse-off only, not for use on damaged skin).']
  contraindications varchar [note: 'Specific conditions or populations for which the product should not be used (e.g., not for use on broken skin, avoid if allergic to fragrance, not suitable for children under 3).']
  cpnp_notification_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this assessment supports a product requiring notification in the EU CPNP system.']
  cpnp_reference_number varchar [note: 'CPNP notification reference number if the product has been notified in the EU portal.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this safety assessment record was first created in the system.']
  document_location varchar [note: 'File path or document management system reference where the full safety assessment report is stored (e.g., Veeva Vault document ID).']
  exposure_duration decimal(18,4) [note: 'Duration of product contact with skin or mucous membranes (e.g., rinse-off, leave-on, brief contact).']
  fda_registration_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this assessment supports a product requiring FDA registration or notification in the United States.']
  label_warnings_required varchar [note: 'Mandatory label warnings and precautionary statements required based on the safety assessment (e.g., avoid contact with eyes, for external use only, keep out of reach of children).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this safety assessment record was last modified.']
  margin_of_safety decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated margin of safety ratio (NOAEL/SED), indicating the safety buffer between the no observed adverse effect level and systemic exposure dose.']
  next_review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the safety assessment, typically required every 3-5 years or when formulation changes occur.']
  noael_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'No Observed Adverse Effect Level in mg/kg body weight/day, derived from toxicological studies.']
  regulatory_market_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic markets and regulatory jurisdictions for which this safety assessment is intended (e.g., EU, USA, ASEAN, China, Brazil).']
  restricted_substances_compliance boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the formulation complies with all restricted substance regulations (EU Annexes II-VI, FDA prohibited ingredients).']
  restricted_substances_findings varchar [note: 'Details of any restricted substances identified in the formulation and their compliance status with maximum concentration limits.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the safety assessment was last reviewed or updated to reflect new data or regulatory changes.']
  safety_conclusion varchar [note: 'Overall safety conclusion reached by the assessor: safe for intended use, unsafe, conditionally safe (with restrictions), or insufficient data to conclude.']
  supporting_studies_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of key toxicological and clinical studies used to support the safety assessment, including study types and outcomes.']
  systemic_exposure_estimate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Estimated systemic exposure dose in mg/kg body weight/day, calculated from dermal absorption and usage patterns.']
  target_population varchar [note: 'Intended consumer population for the product (e.g., adults, children over 3 years, infants, pregnant women, general population).']
  toxicological_endpoints_evaluated varchar [note: 'List of toxicological endpoints assessed (e.g., acute toxicity, skin irritation, eye irritation, skin sensitization, repeated dose toxicity, mutagenicity, carcinogenicity, reproductive toxicity).']
  undesirable_effects_reported varchar [note: 'Description of any undesirable effects observed in toxicological studies or post-market surveillance that are relevant to the safety assessment.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the safety assessment document, incremented with each revision or update.']

  Note: 'Formal product safety assessment records required for regulatory compliance and consumer safety. Captures assessment type (cosmetic product safety report per EU Regulation 1223/2009, toxicological risk assessment, dermal sensitization assessment, HRIPT), assessor name and qualification, assessment date, product/formulation assessed, safety conclusion (safe/unsafe/conditionally safe), conditions of use, and any required label warnings. Mandatory for EU CPSR and other market registrations.'
}

Table research.scale_up_trial {
  scale_up_trial_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the manufacturing scale-up trial record. Primary key for the scale-up trial entity.']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_facility. Business justification: Technology‑transfer records require the specific manufacturing facility where the scale‑up trial is performed for capacity planning.']
  primary_scale_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing manager or technical authority who signed off on the scale-up trial and manufacturing readiness.']
  product_formulation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the R&D formulation being scaled up from bench scale to pilot or commercial production scale.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Scale‑up trials are scheduled on a particular production line; linking enables line utilization reporting and OEE tracking.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent R&D project under which this scale-up trial is conducted.']
  actual_output_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of finished product produced during the scale-up trial measured in kilograms.']
  appearance_assessment varchar [note: 'Visual evaluation of product appearance including texture, homogeneity, and any defects observed during the trial.']
  batch_size_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total batch size produced during the scale-up trial measured in kilograms, representing the scale of production tested.']
  color_measurement varchar [note: 'Objective color measurement of the scaled-up batch using colorimetry or visual assessment against standard.']
  cooling_rate_celsius_per_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rate at which the batch was cooled after processing, critical for product texture and stability.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions implemented during or after the trial to address deviations or quality issues.']
  cost_currency_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the trial cost amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the scale-up trial record was first created in the data system.']
  deviation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any process deviations, non-conformances, or unexpected observations during the scale-up trial.']
  deviation_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the impact of observed deviations on product quality, safety, and manufacturing readiness.']
  deviation_observed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether any process deviations from the standard manufacturing procedure were observed during the trial.']
  equipment_configuration decimal(18,4) [note: 'Description of the manufacturing equipment setup used during the trial, including mixer type, vessel capacity, and auxiliary equipment.']
  heating_rate_celsius_per_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rate at which the batch was heated during processing, important for thermal-sensitive formulations.']
  in_process_test_results varchar [note: 'Summary of all in-process quality control tests performed during the scale-up trial, including pass/fail status for each checkpoint.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the scale-up trial record was most recently updated or modified.']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Key insights, learnings, and recommendations captured from the scale-up trial for future technology transfers and process improvements.']
  manufacturing_readiness_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of manufacturing readiness for commercial production based on trial outcomes and technology transfer completion.']
  manufacturing_sign_off_date date [note: 'Date when manufacturing operations formally approved the scale-up trial results and confirmed readiness for commercial production.']
  manufacturing_site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing facility or plant where the scale-up trial was executed.']
  mixing_speed_rpm varchar [note: 'Rotational speed of the mixing equipment during the trial, critical process parameter for scale-up consistency.']
  mixing_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the mixing phase during the scale-up trial, key parameter for process reproducibility.']
  next_steps varchar [note: 'Documented next actions required following the scale-up trial, such as additional trials, process optimization, or commercial launch preparation.']
  odor_assessment varchar [note: 'Sensory evaluation of the product fragrance and odor profile compared to bench-scale formulation.']
  order_of_addition varchar [note: 'Documented sequence in which raw materials and ingredients were added during the manufacturing process, critical for formulation reproducibility.']
  pass_fail_rationale decimal(18,6) [note: 'Detailed justification for the trial status decision, including quality assessment, process performance, and readiness for commercial production.']
  ph_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Measured pH of the scaled-up batch, critical quality parameter for product stability and skin compatibility.']
  process_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target processing temperature maintained during the scale-up trial, critical for formulation stability and quality.']
  production_line varchar [note: 'Specific production line or equipment train used for the scale-up trial within the manufacturing site.']
  quality_sign_off_date date [note: 'Date when quality assurance formally approved the scale-up trial results and confirmed product quality meets specifications.']
  scale_factor decimal(8,2) [note: 'Multiplication factor representing the scale-up ratio from bench scale to pilot or commercial scale (e.g., 10x, 100x, 1000x).']
  technology_transfer_checklist_status varchar [note: 'Completion status of the formal technology transfer checklist documenting all required activities and sign-offs.']
  trial_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the scale-up trial for external reference and tracking across R&D and manufacturing systems.']
  trial_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for conducting the scale-up trial including raw materials, labor, equipment time, and testing.']
  trial_date date [note: 'The date on which the scale-up trial was conducted at the manufacturing facility.']
  trial_status varchar [note: 'Overall outcome status of the scale-up trial: pass (successful), fail (unsuccessful), conditional_pass (passed with minor issues), in_progress (ongoing), or cancelled.']
  trial_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scale-up trial based on production scale: pilot (small-scale test), semi-commercial (intermediate scale), commercial (full production scale), or validation (qualification run).']
  viscosity_cps varchar [note: 'Measured viscosity of the scaled-up batch, key quality attribute for product consistency and consumer experience.']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual production yield as a percentage of theoretical yield, indicating manufacturing efficiency and material loss.']

  Note: 'Records manufacturing scale-up trials conducted to transfer formulations from R&D bench scale to pilot or commercial production scale, serving as the formal technology transfer record. Captures trial date, manufacturing site, batch size, equipment used, process parameters (mixing speed, temperature, time, order of addition), yield, in-process test results, deviation observations, scale-up status (pass/fail/conditional), technology transfer checklist completion, and manufacturing readiness sign-off. Bridges R&D formulation development with manufacturing operations and commercial launch readiness.'
}

Table research.study_protocol {
  study_protocol_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for study_protocol']
  amended_study_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on study_protocol (amended_study_protocol_id)']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the sponsor entity.']
  rd_project_id varchar
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date on which the amendment became effective.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the amendment applied to the protocol.']
  blinding varchar [note: 'Degree of masking applied in the study.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the protocol record was first created in the system.']
  data_retention_period varchar [note: 'Number of days the study data must be retained after completion.']
  data_sharing_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing external sharing of study data.']
  design_type varchar [note: 'Structural design of the study.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the protocol ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the protocol becomes operationally effective.']
  ethics_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the ethics committee granted approval.']
  ethics_approval_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ethics committee approval.']
  exclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Conditions that disqualify participants.']
  inclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Conditions participants must meet to be eligible.']
  indication varchar [note: 'Specific medical condition or consumer need addressed by the study.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the protocol contains confidential information.']
  primary_endpoint varchar [note: 'Main outcome measure used to assess study success.']
  protocol_code varchar [note: 'Business code or alphanumeric identifier assigned to the protocol by the sponsor or R&D organization.']
  protocol_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the study protocol.']
  protocol_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the overall nature of the protocol.']
  randomization_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assign participants to study arms.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when regulatory approval was granted.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory authority approval.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Target number of participants or units to be enrolled.']
  secondary_endpoints varchar [note: 'Additional outcome measures, stored as a delimited list.']
  sponsor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the organization sponsoring the study.']
  statistical_analysis_plan varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed statistical methodology document.']
  study_phase varchar [note: 'Regulatory phase of the study (if applicable).']
  study_protocol_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description summarizing the study protocol.']
  study_protocol_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the protocol.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Broad therapeutic domain (e.g., dermatology, oral care).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the protocol record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the protocol (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for study_protocol. Referenced by study_protocol_id.'
}

Table research.respondent {
  respondent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for respondent']
  panel_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the panel or source group the respondent belongs to.']
  referring_respondent_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on respondent (referring_respondent_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of respondent\'s mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of respondent\'s mailing address.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of residence.']
  consent_given_date date [note: 'Date when the respondent gave consent for participation.']
  consent_revoked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the respondent has revoked consent.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of respondent.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the respondent record was first created.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of the respondent.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Primary device type used for online surveys.']
  education_level varchar [note: 'Highest education level attained by the respondent.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for contacting the respondent.']
  ethnicity varchar [note: 'Self‑reported ethnicity of the respondent.']
  external_code varchar [note: 'External identifier from third‑party panel provider.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the respondent.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self‑reported gender of the respondent.']
  household_size varchar [note: 'Number of individuals in the respondent\'s household.']
  income_bracket varchar [note: 'Income bracket category of the respondent.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Last known IP address used during survey participation.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred language for survey communications.']
  last_participation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent survey completed by the respondent.']
  marital_status varchar [note: 'Marital status of the respondent.']
  opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the respondent opted out of future research contact.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary phone number of the respondent.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the respondent\'s address.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for contacting the respondent.']
  recruitment_source varchar [note: 'Source through which the respondent was recruited (e.g., online panel, social media, referral).']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region of the respondent.']
  respondent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the respondent.']
  respondent_type varchar [note: 'Category of respondent based on role in research.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of residence.']
  survey_participation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of surveys the respondent has completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the respondent record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for respondent. Referenced by respondent_id.'
}

  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sample was collected.']

  concentration_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Concentration of active ingredient expressed as a percentage.']

  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Environmental condition required for sample storage.']

  volume_ml decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical volume of the sample in milliliters.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for sample. Referenced by sample_id.'
}

  strategic_importance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0-100) indicating strategic value of the family.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for patent_family. Referenced by patent_family_id.'
}

Table regulatory.regulatory_registration {
  regulatory_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  dossier_id varchar [note: 'Supporting dossier']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Financial posting of registration fees requires a GL account; finance uses GL accounts for all fee postings.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing facility or establishment where the registered product is manufactured. Links to facility master data.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Registration approval requires a designated regulatory affairs employee; linking enables audit trail of responsible employee.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Product being registered']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Related submission']
  active_ingredients varchar [note: 'List of active ingredients or substances covered by this registration, using INCI (International Nomenclature of Cosmetic Ingredients) or CAS (Chemical Abstracts Service) numbers where applicable. Critical for EPA pesticide and antimicrobial registrations.']
  annual_report_due_date date [note: 'Date by which annual registration reports or updates must be submitted to the regulatory authority. Used for compliance tracking and reporting calendar management.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory authority officially approved the registration application.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date of approval']
  cfr_citation varchar [note: 'Specific CFR (Code of Federal Regulations) citation or section under which the product is registered (e.g., 21 CFR 314 for NDAs, 40 CFR 152 for pesticides). Applicable primarily to US registrations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO country code']
  cpsc_tracking_label_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether CPSC tracking label requirements apply to this registered product. True if tracking labels are required, False otherwise. Applicable to children\'s products and certain consumer goods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record creation timestamp']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of fee']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the registration becomes legally effective and the product may be marketed or distributed under this registration.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Registration expiration date']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fee amount paid or payable for this registration in the registration holder\'s reporting currency. Includes initial registration fees and any applicable renewal fees.']
  fee_currency_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the registration fee amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD).']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product and manufacturing facility meet Good Manufacturing Practice requirements as verified during registration. True if GMP-compliant, False otherwise.']
  holder_address varchar [note: 'Full business address of the registration holder as registered with the regulatory authority. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  holder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the company or entity that holds the registration. This is the registrant of record with the regulatory authority.']
  ifra_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product formulation complies with IFRA standards for fragrance ingredients. True if IFRA-compliant, False otherwise. Relevant for cosmetic and personal care products.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority name']
  jurisdiction_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the jurisdiction where the registration is valid (e.g., USA, DEU, GBR, CAN, BRA, JPN, AUS, CHN).']
  jurisdiction_region varchar [note: 'Specific region, state, or province within the country where the registration applies (e.g., California, Ontario, Bavaria). Used for sub-national regulatory requirements.']
  label_approval_number varchar [note: 'Approval number for the product label as reviewed and accepted by the regulatory authority. Critical for EPA pesticide registrations where label claims are strictly regulated.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Last update timestamp']
  market_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Market authorization reference']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this registration record. Audit trail field for accountability and compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this registration record was last modified or updated. Audit trail field for change tracking and compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes']
  notification_reference varchar [note: 'Notification portal reference']
  post_market_surveillance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether post-market surveillance or pharmacovigilance reporting is required for this registration. True if surveillance is required, False otherwise.']
  product_category_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory product category']
  product_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the product scope covered by this registration, including specific formulations, SKUs (Stock Keeping Units), or product variants included under this registration.']
  reach_registration_number decimal(9,4) [note: 'EU REACH registration number for chemical substances. Applicable for products containing substances subject to REACH regulation in the European Union.']
  registered_product_name varchar [note: 'Official registered name']
  registering_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority or governing body with which the product is registered (e.g., FDA, EPA, EU Commission, Health Canada, ANVISA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EPA|CPSC|EU_REACH|Health_Canada|ANVISA|PMDA|TGA|SFDA|MHRA|BfR — promote to reference product]']
  registration_category decimal(9,4) [note: 'Regulatory category or class of the registration as defined by the authority (e.g., OTC drug, prescription drug, general use pesticide, restricted use pesticide, cosmetic product, biocidal product).']
  registration_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee paid for registration']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current status (pending, approved, rejected, expired)']
  registration_type varchar [note: 'Type of registration (e.g., cosmetic, drug, device)']
  regulatory_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary regulatory affairs contact person for this registration. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  regulatory_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary regulatory affairs contact person responsible for this registration. Used for correspondence and regulatory inquiries.']
  regulatory_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary regulatory affairs contact person for this registration. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Next renewal due date']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the registration renewal application must be submitted to maintain continuous registration status. Used for proactive renewal management.']
  responsible_person_name varchar [note: 'Responsible person for registration']
  sds_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document number for the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) or Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) associated with this registered product. Links to safety documentation repository.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the registration application or dossier was submitted to the regulatory authority.']
  submission_reference_number varchar [note: 'Internal or external reference number for the regulatory submission dossier associated with this registration. Links to detailed submission documentation.']
  submitted_date date [note: 'Date submitted to authority']
  use_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the intended use or application of the product as defined by the regulatory authority (e.g., cosmetic, drug, pesticide, antimicrobial, food contact substance, medical device). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cosmetic|drug|pesticide|antimicrobial|food_contact|medical_device|biocide|industrial_chemical — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Regulatory registration records for products in various jurisdictions'
}

Table regulatory.submission {
  submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Target jurisdiction']
  previous_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the prior submission record that this submission amends, supersedes, or follows up on.']
  rd_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Regulatory submissions are initiated by a specific R&D project; tracking rd_project_id enables the Submission Tracking Report linking project milestones to submission status.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Product being submitted']
  submission_assigned_reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory submission review is performed by a specific employee; FK supports reviewer assignment tracking.']
  submission_submitted_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Submitter']
  actual_decision_date date [note: 'Actual decision date']
  authority_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the jurisdiction of the regulatory authority (e.g., USA, DEU, GBR, CAN).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel or method through which the submission was filed (electronic, paper, regulatory portal, EDI electronic data interchange).']
  contact_person_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the internal regulatory affairs contact person for correspondence related to this submission.']
  contact_person_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal regulatory affairs contact person or responsible party for this submission.']
  contact_person_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the internal regulatory affairs contact person for inquiries related to this submission.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action required or implemented in response to the submission (e.g., voluntary recall, labeling update, product reformulation).']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the regulatory authority has mandated corrective action (recall, labeling change, product modification) as a result of this submission.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record creation']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome (approved, rejected, pending)']
  deficiency_letter_received boolean [note: 'Deficiency flag']
  deficiency_response_date date [note: 'Response date']
  dossier_id varchar [note: 'Associated dossier']
  expected_decision_date date [note: 'Expected decision date']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Regulatory filing fee amount charged by the authority for processing the submission.']
  fee_currency_code decimal(18,2) [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the regulatory filing fee (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  fee_payment_status decimal(18,2) [note: 'Payment status of the regulatory filing fee (paid, pending, waived, refunded).']
  hazard_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the hazard, incident, or safety concern that prompted the submission (applicable for CPSC Section 15(b) reports, SaferProducts.gov filings, and post-market surveillance submissions).']
  incident_date date [note: 'Date on which the reported incident, adverse event, or safety issue occurred (applicable for safety-related submissions).']
  language varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the primary language in which the submission was prepared and filed (e.g., en for English, de for German, fr for French).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified the submission record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Last update']
  method varchar [note: 'Method (electronic, paper)']
  notes varchar [note: 'Notes']
  population_at_risk varchar [note: 'Estimated number of consumers or units in the market that may be affected by the reported hazard or safety issue (applicable for safety-related submissions).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the submission by the regulatory authority or internal regulatory affairs team (expedited, standard, routine).']
  reference varchar
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Authority name']
  regulatory_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the product registration record that this submission supports or updates.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Authority reviewer']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Assessed severity level of the hazard or incident reported in the submission (critical, high, medium, low).']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date submitted']
  submission_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee amount']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'Unique submission identifier']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current status']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Type (initial, variation, renewal)']
  supporting_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents, attachments, or exhibits included with the submission (e.g., safety data sheets, clinical studies, ingredient declarations).']
  target_decision_date date [note: 'Expected or target date by which the regulatory authority is anticipated to issue a decision on the submission.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Authority tracking reference']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the submission, incremented when amendments or resubmissions are filed.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created the submission record in the regulatory management system.']

  Note: 'Regulatory submissions to authorities'
}

Table regulatory.dossier {
  dossier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Owner']
  primary_contact_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Dossier primary contact is an employee responsible for dossier management; needed for responsibility reporting.']
  product_formulation_id varchar [note: 'Formulation']
  product_lca_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.product_lca. Business justification: Regulatory dossiers often require Life‑Cycle Assessment data; FK attaches the LCA record to the dossier.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Product']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory submission that this dossier supports. One dossier may support multiple submissions across jurisdictions.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the dossier was formally approved by the regulatory affairs leadership or quality assurance team for submission or archival.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the regulatory affairs manager or quality assurance officer who approved the dossier for submission.']
  cfr_reference varchar [note: 'Specific CFR title and part number(s) that govern the regulatory requirements addressed by this dossier, applicable for U.S. FDA and EPA submissions.']
  clinical_data_included boolean [note: 'Clinical data flag']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or contextual information related to the dossier compilation, review, or submission.']
  compilation_date date [note: 'Date when the dossier compilation was completed and finalized for submission or review.']
  compiled_date date
  completeness_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric representation of dossier completeness, calculated as the percentage of required sections and documents that have been compiled and approved.']
  completeness_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether all required documentation sections and supporting materials have been compiled and verified for regulatory submission readiness.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level indicating the sensitivity and access restrictions for the dossier content.']
  created_date date [note: 'Creation date']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record creation']
  document_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual documents included in this regulatory dossier package.']
  document_repository_path varchar [note: 'Storage path']
  dossier_number varchar [note: 'Dossier identifier']
  dossier_status varchar [note: 'Status (draft, final, submitted)']
  dossier_type varchar [note: 'Type (CTD, eCTD, ANDA)']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the dossier becomes the active regulatory documentation package for the associated product or submission.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the dossier is no longer valid or requires renewal, typically driven by regulatory authority requirements or internal policy.']
  finalized_date date [note: 'Finalization date']
  format varchar [note: 'Format (eCTD, PDF)']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the dossier includes documentation demonstrating compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice standards as required by regulatory authorities.']
  ifra_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the dossier includes documentation demonstrating compliance with IFRA standards for fragrance ingredients.']
  ingredient_declaration_included boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether a complete ingredient declaration (INCI list or equivalent) is included in the dossier.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country or region code representing the regulatory jurisdiction for which this dossier applies.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Language']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Last update']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent periodic review or audit of the dossier content for accuracy, completeness, and regulatory compliance.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review or update of the dossier, ensuring ongoing compliance with evolving regulatory requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Notes']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the regulatory affairs team responsible for compiling, maintaining, and submitting this dossier.']
  post_market_surveillance_included boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether post-market surveillance data, adverse event reports, or vigilance documentation is included in the dossier.']
  product_classification varchar [note: 'Classification']
  reach_registration_number decimal(9,4) [note: 'REACH registration number for chemical substances included in the dossier, required for EU market compliance.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory body or agency for which this dossier is intended. FDA (U.S. Food and Drug Administration), EPA (Environmental Protection Agency), CPSC (Consumer Product Safety Commission), EMA (European Medicines Agency), MHRA (Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency), ANVISA (Brazilian Health Regulatory Agency), PMDA (Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency Japan). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EPA|CPSC|EMA|MHRA|ANVISA|PMDA — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_category varchar [note: 'Broad regulatory classification of the dossier content, such as cosmetics, personal care, household products, food contact materials, or pesticides, aligning with applicable regulatory frameworks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cosmetics|personal_care|household_cleaning|food_contact|pesticides|biocides|medical_devices — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Framework (FDA, EMA, PMDA)']
  safety_assessment_included boolean [note: 'Safety assessment flag']
  sds_included boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether Safety Data Sheets (SDS) or Material Safety Data Sheets (MSDS) are included in the dossier for hazardous substances or mixtures.']
  stability_data_included boolean [note: 'Stability data flag']
  vault_folder_path varchar [note: 'Hierarchical folder path within Veeva Vault where the dossier documents are stored, enabling navigation and retrieval.']
  vault_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference identifier linking this dossier record to the corresponding document repository location in Veeva Vault for full document access and version control.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the dossier, tracking revisions and updates to the compiled documentation package.']

  Note: 'Regulatory dossiers containing product documentation'
}

Table regulatory.sds {
  sds_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Product']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Supplier']
  accidental_release_measures varchar [note: 'Personal precautions, protective equipment, emergency procedures, and methods for containment and cleanup of spills or releases.']
  appearance varchar [note: 'Visual description of the substance including color and form.']
  authoring_chemist varchar [note: 'Name of the qualified chemist or toxicologist who authored or reviewed this SDS for technical accuracy.']
  autoignition_temperature varchar [note: 'Temperature at which the substance will spontaneously ignite without an external ignition source.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'CAS number']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Country']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record creation']
  disposal_considerations decimal(9,4) [note: 'Guidance on proper disposal methods, waste treatment, and applicable regulations for the substance and contaminated packaging.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the safety document type: SDS (Safety Data Sheet per GHS), MSDS (Material Safety Data Sheet legacy format), or Extended SDS (with additional exposure scenarios).']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Document URL']
  ecological_information varchar [note: 'Environmental impact data including aquatic toxicity, persistence and degradability, bioaccumulative potential, and mobility in soil.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this SDS version becomes the official document for regulatory and operational use.']
  emergency_phone varchar [note: 'Emergency contact']
  emergency_phone_number varchar [note: '24-hour emergency contact telephone number for chemical emergency response (e.g., CHEMTREC, poison control).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this SDS version is superseded or no longer valid, requiring update or renewal.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date']
  exposure_limits varchar [note: 'Occupational exposure limits (OELs), permissible exposure limits (PELs), threshold limit values (TLVs), and biological exposure indices.']
  firefighting_measures varchar [note: 'Suitable extinguishing media, unsuitable extinguishing media, and special hazards arising from the substance or mixture during fire.']
  first_aid_measures varchar [note: 'Detailed first aid instructions for different exposure routes (inhalation, skin contact, eye contact, ingestion) as required by GHS Section 4.']
  flash_point varchar [note: 'Lowest temperature at which vapors of the substance will ignite when given an ignition source, typically in degrees Celsius.']
  ghs_classification varchar [note: 'GHS classification']
  handling_precautions varchar [note: 'Safe handling practices and precautions for use to prevent exposure and ensure worker safety.']
  hazard_statements varchar [note: 'Hazard statements']
  hazardous_decomposition_products varchar [note: 'Hazardous substances that may be formed under fire conditions or other decomposition scenarios.']
  incompatible_materials varchar [note: 'Materials or substances that should not be stored or used with this product due to potential hazardous reactions.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Issue date']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Language']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Last update']
  manufacturer_address varchar [note: 'Complete physical address of the manufacturer or supplier for regulatory contact purposes.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Manufacturer']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SDS record was last modified or updated in the system.']
  odor varchar [note: 'Characteristic odor of the substance or mixture, if detectable.']
  packing_group varchar [note: 'UN packing group indicating degree of danger for transport: I (high danger), II (medium danger), III (low danger).']
  personal_protective_equipment varchar [note: 'Required personal protective equipment for safe handling: respiratory protection, hand protection, eye protection, skin protection.']
  ph_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'pH measurement of the substance or mixture, indicating acidity or alkalinity.']
  physical_state varchar [note: 'Physical form of the substance or mixture at ambient conditions.']
  pictograms varchar [note: 'GHS pictograms']
  precautionary_statements varchar [note: 'Precautionary statements']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Product code']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Product name on SDS']
